name: Aal Salma
blurb: An old and careful nation on Terrenia's northwestern coast, Aal Salma has survived centuries by trading with everyone and trusting no one.

name: Abaddan
blurb: A mining settlement in the central mountains, smaller than Phorysia but significant for its iron deposits.

name: Aboyinzu
blurb: The southwestern of Alaria's three central continents: a hooked landmass of temperate coast, savanna heartlands, rainforest, and a cold southern mountain wall.

name: Achtomo

name: Aciabro
blurb: Goblins whose handmagic-cut augmentations run on their own blood; a surviving fragment of a lost craft, bound to a ruinous need to remake themselves.

name: Adal
blurb: The second city, on the smaller Iri peak.

name: Adi Assabehta
blurb: The capital city, built into and around the larger of the two Iri peaks.

name: Adjacent Waters
blurb: Traitor Bay — Named for a civil war naval battle where captains switched sides.

name: Admiral Azmantir
blurb: Killed by Gyash.

name: Adnar
blurb: Erasnus's southern grain port, swollen with refugees from the silvertongue plague; its failed grain bond is now the case that may break the Alliance.

name: Adrak
blurb: The capital city, seat of the monarchy, and home to the Royal Adrak Bank.

name: Adron
blurb: Adron is the easternmost state on the Middle Sea, a proud Naga kingdom set between mountain ranges, once the realm of bonded dragon-riders.

name: Aedorath
blurb: Domain: harvest, abundance, fertility; dead cohort (Golden Age); goddess of the cultivated earth.

name: Aelwennar
blurb: Domains: language, inherited memory, song-as-archive; current cohort; Druidic Long-Song daemon whose name refuses to be broken into two.

name: Aether

name: Aether-tapper
blurb: Goblins whose post-eruption sensitivity lets them pull raw Aether from the air and fix it into the fuel a continent's sky-trade runs on.

name: Aetherial Reckoning
blurb: A mysterious cult known as the Aetherial Reckoners exists, claiming to possess knowledge of an impending cosmic event called the Aetherial Reckoning.

name: Ageito
blurb: The eastern anchor.

name: Ages & History
blurb: The shape of Alarian time: three great eons, the catastrophes that ended each, and the world they left behind.

name: Aglip Scha
blurb: A village abandoned during a plague called the Weeping Rot, now a pilgrimage site for death-cultists, plague-doctors, and those who seek to understand mortality.

name: Agony Stones
blurb: The Agony Stones are not ruins.

name: Agora
blurb: The third great city of Hephake, positioned in the western reaches of the kingdom along the trade routes to Camaran and Adron.

name: Agorf Island
blurb: A state on Agorf Island.

name: Agreben Sea

name: Agtakkeri
blurb: The Flayed Banner.

name: Aikon Tidal Islands
blurb: A chain of low-lying islands north of Sheîr where the tides reshape reality twice daily.

name: Air
blurb: The Kethic element of moving air and carried sound, channeled through the Sulus leyline. Not the brute kinetic push of force.

name: Aknimchuk
blurb: The largest island in the northwestern Northern Isles: an uninhabited expanse split between a volcanic south and the frozen waste of Scheyolonae.

name: Akratian Plateau

name: Al Iwash
blurb: The largest city in the Kingdoms of Fire, built around the natural harbor protected by the Great Lighthouse.

name: Al Khamilah
blurb: The largest of the Sal'emri settlements, a proper town of perhaps five thousand.

name: Al Maqlubah
blurb: Built on the rim of the volcanic basin that contains the Pit of First Fire, Al Maqlubah is a city that exists in constant awareness of…

blurb: Upriver from Al Khamilah, Al Muqlib sits where the river narrows enough to bridge.

name: Alacayir
blurb: A city-state in the United Free Nations.

name: Albersea Islands
blurb: A scattered archipelago in the northern Alrock.

name: Aldric IV

name: Alegheim
blurb: The forest covering northern Moigos, stretching from the Qulyintes River to the coast near Blink Creek.

name: Alejendrina
blurb: A town in the central region, known for its pottery.

name: Alekroin

name: Alezre
blurb: A northern settlement in the foothills, near the approaches to Smokestack and the New Chimea border.

name: Alisandra the Merciful
blurb: Employs Viktor, a rising lich mage and crafter

name: Alyzon
blurb: A smaller island settlement that's developed a reputation for training household slaves.

name: Am Batr
blurb: A smaller port town on the bay's northern shore, Am Batr handles the fishing trade and serves as a waypoint for coastal traffic moving between…

name: Amaro
blurb: A northern city near the Torulius Mountains, serving as the primary market for giant herder goods during migration seasons.

name: Ámas Kiori
blurb: The sacred basin north of Adrak, holy land to all dragonborn.

name: Amber Coast

name: Amholia Greras
blurb: The central forest of the Farlands, positioned between all three elven kingdoms.

name: Amvinwir
blurb: Domains: the long-lived city, refined wisdom, civilization-as-art; current cohort; Amverela patron of the city the centuries do not erase.

name: Anthorlax
blurb: Giant, 18-foot tall, blue-scaled, lizard creatures.

name: Anu'itiklik
blurb: Anu'itiklik is a frozen, windblasted wasteland, home only to the Atowatowa and caribou.

name: Anu'Itiklik

name: Aphenlein
blurb: The northern portion of Aphenlein.

name: Aphenlein
blurb: Jungle with many, large, slow moving rivers passing through it.

name: Aphoryis
blurb: At the heart of Slumber Deep, where the stasis is absolute and nothing should be able to move, a fortress stands.

name: Apketrii
blurb: The mountainous island home to Keletus.

name: Apuindi Mountains
blurb: Mountains on Pesalolo.

name: Argum Hills
blurb: A hill range in the Wanderlands.

name: Argysis
blurb: A Kharvorn sub-range that was once a great dwarven kingdom, now a black dragon's empty hoard worked by his Shyka servants.

blurb: Colonial halflings of the Greenwater Isles who built a mercantile empire on emptied land and do not speak of who lived there before.

blurb: Objects that hold power their makers bound into them, and which usually outlast the makers.

name: Asgrael (Village)
blurb: Population: ~250

name: Asha

name: Aspenthar (Town)
blurb: Population: ~900

name: Astalani
blurb: Underground, massive glacial tunnel system, freezing rivers running throughout.

name: Aster
blurb: A city-state within Griselia.

name: Astraeva
blurb: The unmediated presence of Aurus himself—not a plane, but the sub-presence core of the White Sun that underlies the Astral Plane.

name: Astral Currents

name: Astral Plane
blurb: The Astral Plane consists of a massive sea with floating islands, consisting of a special stone-like material that floats in Astral solution.

name: Astrella Forest
blurb: The beautiful forest.

name: Astrelle
blurb: A vassal state of Nektuna, occupying the southern shore of the Tarkhon Passage directly across from the imperial capital.

name: Ataç
blurb: A city-state in the United Free Nations.

name: Athatgarun
blurb: Athatgarun is a satyr demigod.

name: Atlangsow

name: Atowatowa

name: Atra
blurb: The capital, such as it is—the largest tree-settlement in Stipen.

name: Atriik

name: Attla
blurb: A village on Phyr Island.

name: Atyeska
blurb: A state in the Frostmarch Peninsula.

name: Aureum
blurb: An ancient, mostly vanished dwarf civilization that captured sunlight in mirror-and-glass engines a mile underground; their works are ruins, stranded when the sun's path shifted.

name: Aurigeum
blurb: Domains: captured sun, liquid sunlight, the oath-bound light; current cohort; Aureum patron whose Nydor heretics pray to him inverted.

name: Avakus Forest
blurb: A stretch of dense woodland west of Syvlius, climbing into the mountain foothills.

name: Avar Sonnon
blurb: Eastern city, near the Crags of Geth and Griselia.

name: Avil Lakes
blurb: A cluster of shallow lakes in the southern plains, fed by seasonal streams that flow down from the desert highlands before the land dries completely.

name: Avuul (Village)
blurb: Population: ~180

name: Awelde
blurb: Long dead wizard.

name: Awobiso
blurb: The gatekeepers of the Free Isles, where paranoia is policy and strangers are screened before they're welcomed.

name: Ay'Nbhad
blurb: Like burning man, but is located on the River Yuforhi, which cuases ephoria when drunk.

name: Ayashk Island
blurb: A rocky archipelago in the Deaddore Strait, home to the Free Colonies.

name: Ayblek

name: Azak
blurb: Domains: time, clairvoyance, omens; current cohort; oracle deity whose following predates most living nations.

name: Azantir
blurb: The capital.

name: Azawahasi

name: Azkirk

name: Azur
blurb: The largest village, positioned along the primary path to the monastery.

name: Azuros

name: Balduahr
blurb: The last dwarven kingdom in Wycendeula—a mountainous holdout wedged between the satyr-occupied plains to the east and the monster-haunted Kilbyurn range to the west.

name: Banaki
blurb: Domains: controlled rage, emotion-mastery, the eye inside the storm; current cohort; Danaki patron of the withheld strike.

name: Banesnap Hills
blurb: The Banesnap Hills run along the eastern face of the Foggy Mountains, forming a broken barrier of ridges and valleys between the mountain wall and…

name: Banguna
blurb: A small village east of Moura, in a clearing near the jungle's edge.

name: Bani Bari
blurb: The capital of Kura sits at the heart of the nation, carefully positioned to straddle the Ley Line of Fire.

name: Bank of Infindior
blurb: The material-world door to the Bank of Infindior, the time-stilled vault where the great houses keep what must never be reached.

name: Bank of Infindior
blurb: Bank where time doesn't pass, but anything is immediately accessible.

name: Barren Hills
blurb: Hills in the Dragon's Spine Coast.

name: Barro Triste
blurb: A village in the eastern region whose name—"sad mud"—reflects its primary characteristic.

name: Bata Nujo Hills
blurb: Hills in Gorath.

name: Bathemiel
blurb: The chained dragon beneath Mpehi, bound in the depths since before humans arrived at the Free Isles.

name: Bay of Lords
blurb: A bay on Urok.

name: Bay of Monkeys
blurb: A deep inlet on the northeastern coast, where the Beastlands jungle meets the Krell-infested territories to the east.

name: Bay of Roule
blurb: The great southern bay that gives Roule its naval power and commercial reach.

name: Bay of Wolves
blurb: A deep inlet cutting into the western coast of Wycendeula, framed by the Wolfmaw peninsula to the south and forested headlands to the north.

name: Bazaar of Scales
blurb: A sprawling desert market in the southern Luquihn Desert, positioned at the edge of the Lyagnadarr Mountains.

name: Bazz
blurb: The de facto capital of modern Mueras, built on a series of cliff-carved terraces overlooking the junction of Labyrinth Sound and Corpse Bay.

name: Beastman
blurb: Gaea's minor animal-children: dozens of distinct animal-shaped peoples, all made at the Birth of Man and scattered too widely for the Shattering to find.

name: Beauty Bay
blurb: South of Tyberoskos, where the Maun River meets the sea, Beauty Bay earned its name honestly.

name: Bejakørn (Town)
blurb: Population: ~600

name: Bejeweled Sea
blurb: Home to many triton and Bfaspeen

name: Belagria
blurb: Belagria is the upper half of the Material Plane—the inhabited world where most of 'life' occurs.

name: Belanora

name: Belén

name: Belu Jenari

name: Beshu's Court
blurb: Massive (18 feet) four-armed ogre.

name: Bestacia
blurb: A Goshwen gnome republic on the Middle Sea coast, known for scholarship, invention, and a complete lack of military ambition.

name: Bettle Lake
blurb: A mountain lake in the northeastern Scarlet Peaks, one of the few permanent water sources in the range.

name: Bevvon
blurb: Domain: drunkenness, feast, ecstatic communion; dead cohort (Golden Age); a measure of the surplus the Winter destroyed first.

name: Bexlex
blurb: Domains: jungle flame, the controlled burn, the green that knows it must sometimes char; current cohort; Neka patron of the fire the jungle agrees to.

name: Bfaspeik

name: Bhutag
blurb: A town in the eastern desert near the coast, built atop millennia-old catacombs.

name: Birthday Wreck
blurb: A famous shipwreck visible at extreme low tide in the waters near the Shardmaw Isles.

name: Bisaro
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Bitterlands
blurb: South of Shargon, the terrain rises into a desolate expanse of rocky highlands, ice fields, and howling wind—the Bitterlands.

name: Bittermarsh
blurb: Swampland where the forest meets standing water.

name: Bjerno
blurb: A lost Uline mine settlement in Ubrik's northern field, the first to be overrun after Lohva fell to the silvertongues.

name: Bjornheim (Village)
blurb: Population: ~200

blurb: A sprawling swamp in the north-central Dygon Beastlands, where dark water pools beneath a broken canopy and the dead do not always stay dead.

name: Blackport
blurb: Named after the black, lavarock bluffs the surround the island.

name: Bladewood
blurb: A forest within Shyona.

name: Blof Point

blurb: A forest within Shyona.

blurb: The northernmost coastal settlement, near where New Chimea's territory meets the open sea.

name: Blossom Valley
blurb: A forested valley on Apketrii.

name: Blue Lake
blurb: A lake in Tornia, Ofrenia.

name: Blue Shalewood
blurb: A forest in Wycendeula.

name: Blueshale Peaks

name: Bluesong Bay

name: Blvnird
blurb: A city-state in Westrim.

name: Blvnird
blurb: One of the dwarven citystates and part of First Brotherhood.

name: Blyss
blurb: The eastern reaches of the Dygon Beastlands, where the jungle grows thickest and the canopy blocks nearly all sunlight.

name: Bonnetaz
blurb: Inhabited by Kuzagt

blurb: A stretch of dangerous rapids in the mountain rivers, notorious for claiming unwary travelers.

blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

blurb: A bay in L'Coth D'Hari.

blurb: A mountain state nestled within the Drystone Mountains, notable for its ancient ruins and dangerous magical sites.

name: Brutemage
blurb: Giant mages.

name: Bryn

blurb: Domains: living stone, deep silence, geological patience; current cohort; the Hestrube patron whose name carries no open vowels.

name: Droaz River
blurb: A river in Gorath.

name: Drollaróth
blurb: Domain: agriculture in the reborn world, the first ploughing, breaking new ground; dead cohort (Craggus era); the god of the founding cut, killed mid-furrow.

name: Drøm the Patient-Mountain
blurb: Domains: mountain-shoulder, the slow weight, the patience of stone; current cohort; Hill Giant patron of what waits.

name: Drømgar
blurb: Domains: siege endurance, the sealed door, the held line; current cohort; Dern's defensive patron whose body is the closed gate.

name: Droughd Sound
blurb: The gateway to Gnotobi's empire.

name: Druids
blurb: The elder nature-race that arrived with Gaea and crafted the elves; a dwindling handful, each of whom pays for creation in their own waning life.

name: Drunken Forest
blurb: East of Wisgarrd, between the city-state and Xoth's western territories, the Drunken Forest earns its name with every step.

name: Dryasa
blurb: Pixie cut, tan robes, strawberry hair.

name: Dryenthia Valley
blurb: The Dryenthia Valley cuts through the heart of Argysis, running roughly east-west between the mountain peaks.

name: Drystone Mountains
blurb: A major east-west mountain range within the Sandreach Mountains system, running through the central portion of the range.

name: Dunes of Evioli
blurb: A sea of sand hiding an empire of orange.

name: Dunes of Kalamesh
blurb: A vast expanse of towering sand dunes in the northeastern Luquihn Desert, stretching inland from the Sunshine Coast.

name: Dunes of Kunagi
blurb: A vast rain-shadow desert in Westrim's interior, hunted by giant sand wurms and crossed only by those who can reach Benshu's Promise.

name: Dunes of Tithonius
blurb: Northeast of Kumi Wood, where the forest gives way to scrubland and then to sand, lie the Dunes of Tithonius—a desert that shouldn't exist.

name: Dungeon of Flowers
blurb: Filled with flowers that never decay.

name: Durnigan Highlands
blurb: Highlands in Taulo Magi, Dalizi.

name: Duskwood Forest
blurb: A dense forest in southwestern Phirexes named for the permanent twilight under its canopy.

name: Dustforge
blurb: A city-state in the southeastern Luquihn Desert, built around Esedin Lake—a large oasis fed by underground aquifers.

name: Dustwind Plateau
blurb: The arid heart of Mueras, a high tableland swept by winds that never cease.

name: Dwarblin
blurb: The Dwarblin look like dwarf-goblin crossbreeds but are dwarves remade goblin-shaped by another cause, residing primarily in Watar.

name: Dwarf
blurb: Dwarves are a short but hearty people, and are generally more pragmatic than the other races.

name: Dwelyn
blurb: Fierce green-skinned warriors of the Green Wilds, bound to a failing line of dragonriders and locked in a war they once dominated.

name: Dwexes
blurb: The Dwexes is a circular river 200 miles undeground.

name: Dy
blurb: A small settlement in Oskwatch Valley, in the southwestern part of Qet Yemani.

name: Dygon
blurb: A city-state within Griselia.

name: Dygon Beastlands
blurb: The Dygon Beastlands sprawl across the entire southern end of Ve, a primordial wilderness roughly four hundred miles north to south and six hundred miles…

name: Dylania
blurb: The capital and largest city in Eoga.

name: Dyos
blurb: Golden Age daemon of sacrifice and divine war; dead cohort; a follower's act ended the God War and triggered the Long Winter.

name: Dywea
blurb: A western settlement in the jungle interior, serving as a waypoint for goods moving between the deep jungle and Vystril.

name: Dzobi Lands
blurb: Southwestern territory, bordering Kazül.

name: Earth
blurb: The Kethic element of stone and soil, channeled through the Golus leyline. Not a gift of Gaea, and split between shapers and diviners.

name: East Naruaghin Tribes
blurb: The East Naruaghin occupy the borderlands between the Ishnit Jungles and the Green Wilds of Enapay.

name: East Whitewall
blurb: The first major face of cliffs in Venalthier, home to the Nabuhe dwarves who have carved extensive mining operations into the frozen stone.

name: Easthelm
blurb: A fortified town in the eastern passes, controlling the approaches from the Grey Mountains and Myorna beyond.

name: Eberri Ygonzi
blurb: The great forest of southwestern Wycendeula—a dense expanse of ancient conifers and tangled undergrowth stretching from the Neurian Hills to the edge of Kama Sa'malina.

name: Eceraen Mountains
blurb: A rugged east-west mountain range forming the southern boundary of Wycendeula, separating the forested lowlands from the frozen wastes of Venalthier beyond.

name: Echea
blurb: A tiny gnomish magocracy on a mountainous peninsula at the tip of the Watar peninsula, jutting into the Middle Sea.

name: Echem Yiakraxes
blurb: A vast killing ground in northwestern Central Aboyinzu, Echem Yiakraxes is Terrogone territory—and everyone knows it.

name: Echi
blurb: Gnomes of the Echean magocracy, raised in the mountain universities into elemental war-casters who would rather argue a citation than fight.

name: Edari
blurb: The golden savannah of northwestern Ve, Edari is a vast wilderness claimed by no settled nation.

name: Eddletress
blurb: A northern settlement near the border with Qet Yemani.

name: Ederri
blurb: An underwater sinkhole region off the eastern coast of Iqes in the Agreben Sea.

name: Edko Sound
blurb: The sheltered waters between the Floss Islands and the Enavadi coast, Edko Sound is the heart of Enavadi's fishing industry.

name: Edkovic
blurb: The capital, if Enavadi can be said to have one.

name: Edric
blurb: Prince of Joswik, the youngest of Ofrenia's three royal siblings; he has tried to make peace with Tornia for years without ever learning why it keeps failing.

name: Edryss the Unsworn
blurb: The pale daughter of Lorus and his wife, the Countess Buchannon.

name: Eelapede
blurb: Giant swamp centipedes, grow incredibly long and carry disease, poison prey, and drag people into the water

name: Egül Mountains
blurb: The eastern arm of the Gül Mountains, separating the Breidleheis basin from the broader Kharvorn range.

name: Egzahd
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Eidlenight Eight
blurb: A coalition of eight major daemons: Bylzar, Varn, Sainu, Íritiv, Solas, Uun, Qoponatai, and Azak.

name: Eidlsandres
blurb: Eidlsandres doesn't exist.

name: Eindumor
blurb: The unmediated presence of Nydus himself—not a plane, but the sub-presence core of the Black Sun that underlies Malstaris.

name: Eir
blurb: A small rocky island north of Pii, uninhabited except for seabirds and the occasional hermit.

name: Ekadriel
blurb: A location in Old Tolaria.

name: Ekfusy
blurb: A functional but miserable fortress on Midton Island, watching the eastern approaches to the Tarkhon Passage.

name: Ekomorn
blurb: Rift in the Ishnit Jungles.

name: Ekri Towers
blurb: Twin spires of dark stone rising from the ice plain between Morelous and the Selengreyb Plateau—the only structures in Venalthier's interior, standing where no structures…

name: Ektolmni Grif
blurb: The transitional hill country between Vokas Enrisikna and the Tyror plains—broken terrain where the mountains give way to grassland in a tumble of ridges, draws,…

name: El Djele
blurb: Yaif's capital is built into the slopes of an extinct volcano, its buildings carved from the same dark stone that surrounds it.

name: Elbow
blurb: A coastal settlement south of Garum, serving fishing communities and providing an alternate port for coastal traffic.

name: Elder Mountains
blurb: The Elder Mountains rise at the southeastern corner of the Dalizi Highlands, a weathered range of ancient stone that connects the Foggy Mountains to the…

name: Elder Wilds
blurb: The Elder Wilds occupy the entire eastern peninsula of Aboyinzu—a vast region of ancient rainforest, winding rivers, and primordial wilderness.

name: Elderran
blurb: The ruined capital of Old Tolaria, and the epicenter of one of the most catastrophic magical disasters in Alarian history.

name: Elebria
blurb: The Furnace-Dweller.

name: Elebria
blurb: A lava dragon who claims the southwestern Pindolin Islands, where the Earth and Fire leylines converge into volcanic fury.

name: Elemental Knight
blurb: War-constructs in which a fragment of an elemental plane is bound inside a sealed suit of armor: deathless, hollow-willed, and long since maddened.

name: Elemental Planes
blurb: A separate adjacent planar stack of nine elemental layers, reached only via leylines—the source of Kethic magic's borrowed power.

name: Elephant Groves
blurb: The Elephant Groves are the largest forest in the region—a vast, ancient woodland that serves as the birthplace and spiritual homeland of the loxodon race.

name: Elessi Bay
blurb: The main bay on Eloesi's western coast, sheltering Kair and several smaller settlements.

name: Elf
blurb: Elves are a race of fiercely independent, ancient, culturally rich people.

name: Elfekin
blurb: A mining settlement deep in the peaks, named for a vein of pale green gems that the original settlers thought looked like elf eyes.

name: Elnir
blurb: The Elnir were once druids who protected the wilds, but they have been corrupted by dark magic and a desire to control nature rather than…

name: Eloesi
blurb: Eloesi occupies the northwestern coast where the landmass connecting Ofrenia to the southern interior meets the sea.

name: Elorshin Grera
blurb: A forest within Lenora.

name: Eloweir
blurb: Proud coastal elves renowned for poison mastery, skilled soldiers, water magic attunement, and sophisticated democratic governance.

name: Eluvarin Aelweir
blurb: Domains: the First Grove, arboreal memory, the elven dead; current cohort; Druidic forefather who came back wearing new bark.

name: Elvanweir
blurb: Domains: the measured dose, the assembly's vote, the curve of water; current cohort; Eloweir patron of poison and democracy as one craft.

name: Elves of the Gray Order
blurb: An Amverela elf cult that worships the sleeping titan Hykravones and hunts his resting place to wake him and reset a world they hold corrupt.

name: Ember Isles
blurb: A volcanic island chain in the southwestern Western Isles, where the earth still burns and ancient Neferati experiments left scars that haven't healed.

name: Emblydium
blurb: A state in the northwest tip of the Dragon's Spine Mountains, populated by the Nydor.

name: Emerald Coast
blurb: A stateless jungle coastline along Rimihuica's eastern seaboard—no overarching authority, just scattered harbor settlements, sub-nations, and slowly building catastrophe.

name: Emkoa Greranu
blurb: A forest within Illron.

name: Emtus
blurb: An impact crater in the Celedrim Plains that resists being mapped or approached.

name: Emyrron the Vile
blurb: A dragon in the Mighty Mountains.

name: En'tashri
blurb: Domains: slave-chain, conquest as virtue, the unbroken column; current cohort; Enti patron of the chain held together.

name: Enapay
blurb: Ancient Dwelyn monarchy occupying the Green Wilds, its queens bonded to the emerald dragon Surrey Mahaila.

name: Enavadi
blurb: A stretch of rocky coastline and sheltered bays along the Seacleft Coast, home to the most skilled fishermen in Alaria.

name: Encaspius
blurb: The northern gatehouse of Argysis, Encaspius controlled access from the Celedrim Plains and the frozen north.

name: Endari Hills
blurb: The transitional highlands between the forests and the lakes—a band of rolling terrain separating Eberri Ygonzi from the northern reaches of Kama Sa'malina.

name: Ender's Inlet
blurb: A major indentation in the southern coast, roughly forty miles wide at its mouth and extending twenty miles inland.

name: Enera Savaci
blurb: The fae court's seat at the heart of Amholia Greras, where Faesong pools thickest and condenses into the unnamed queen of the forest.

name: Enimogos
blurb: At the desert's heart stands Enimogos—the shattered capital of the Postronamas Empire.

name: Enki's House
blurb: A mysterious structure in the southwestern mountains, near Eyrar.

name: Ennros Lake
blurb: Filled with Rusalka.

name: Enti
blurb: Northern orc slavers and raiders who trade captives for furs and gold, living by the belief that might makes right.

name: Enuau Kemjallb
blurb: The radiant House of the Sun—a golden city-state of the Kryaaji sun elves, forbidden to outsiders.

name: Enymu
blurb: The breadbasket of Tarkhon—a farming state that feeds the empire but sees little of the wealth.

name: Eoga
blurb: The most isolated of the five Jungles of Godahi nations—hemmed by the Cloud Mountains and known for cunning diplomacy over military strength.

name: Epyphiozath
blurb: Epyphiozath is a major planar stack existing elsewhere in the cosmos.

name: Erasnus
blurb: A Belenstrope halfling nation in the central Middle Sea region, defined by trade, diplomacy, and a complete inability to fight.

name: Erdel
blurb: Use water and jungle plants to make dyes.

name: Erdene
blurb: The capital city, seat of both the House of Nobles and the House of Commerce.

name: Erebinthias
blurb: Hephake's northern fortress-city on the Argysis border—once a trade hub between dwarven kingdoms, now the military stronghold that watches dragon-occupied territory.

name: Erindath of Sennos
blurb: Dwarven annalist of the Age of Craggus; reads every winter as a god loosening his grip.

name: Eriyen
blurb: The primary gathering place of the Gamori republic in L'Coth D'hari.

name: Erpeus
blurb: Erpeus is a major island in the Middle Sea, positioned between Ofrenia to the east and the mainland to the west.

name: Erzqin
blurb: Bug eyed, 4 armed, clawed, crustacean-y sea monsters.

name: Esedin Lake
blurb: The oasis that makes Dustforge possible.

name: Eshbya Hills
blurb: A region of low hills in the southern foothills of the Kelder Mountains, within Deo Esari's claimed territory.

name: Eshire Moors
blurb: Rolling moorlands in the Wanderlands.

name: Eskagon Hills
blurb: A region of broken highlands northeast of the central peaks, where the Aktarvkus begins to descend toward the eastern coast.

name: Esmeraz
blurb: Praetor of Azantir and master of its slave-markets. He has never won a battle, and he means to prove an emperor does not need to.

name: Esorin
blurb: The Titanic Priesthood's measurer, who surveyed the poisoned south, mapped the whole truth of it, and keeps that map sealed to hold titan-bone prices high.

name: Esteves
blurb: The northern gateway.

name: Estri Islands
blurb: A small archipelago lies offshore to the northwest, separated from the mainland by a few miles of open water.

name: Ethadia
blurb: Ethadia is what remains when ambition meets geography.

name: Ethereal and Nethereal Planes
blurb: The Ethereal and Nethereal planes are two thin planes that overlay all other planes within Alaria.

name: Etherweaver
blurb: Magic-attuned goblins born with affinity for non-basic elements; reserved and cautious, forming smaller broods with stronger individual identity.

name: Ethundra
blurb: South of Withepest, where the steppe begins to transition toward the forested foothills of the Kharvorn Mountains, lies the region called Ethundra—a borderland of scrubby…

name: Evertorch
blurb: The eternally burning flame in Tarkhetan's cathedral—lit at the Neferati founding, never extinguished, and the empire's most contested symbol.

name: Everwood
blurb: A vast forest stretching across the southern Krenglelan Range and into the lowlands beyond, the Everwood is not a forest in any normal sense—it is…

name: Evurcluuh
blurb: A southern town on the lower Nuodyak, near Da Trang's borders with Qet Yemani and New Chimea.

name: Extolmni Furis
blurb: Forested hills in the central part of Rakite, where the Ver Pes river cuts through rising terrain between the Ektolmni Grif and the plains proper.

name: Eyatora
blurb: The eldest living child of the dragon-father Nagatayora, keeper of the hatch-ground at Nagayeshi, who chooses which Naga ride and which do not.

name: Eyendra
blurb: The Eyendra is one of Alaria's great forests—a vast expanse of boreal woodland stretching across southeastern Wycendeula, from the Eceraen Mountains in the south to…

name: Eynmore
blurb: The Rhea capital on Mt.

name: Eyrar
blurb: A southwestern settlement near the edge of Breian territory, where the Drystone Mountains begin their descent toward the Giant Lands.

name: Ezcelmez Lake
blurb: Smaller and shallower than Hakö, Ezcelmez Lake sits near Ovabasi in the agricultural heartland.

name: Ezelmere
blurb: Ezelmere is the ancient, dragon saint of the Ythari elves.

name: Ezra Olkanis
blurb: The dragon called Ezra Olkanis has dwelt in the Eyendra forest for longer than any human civilization on Upoceax has existed.

name: Ezra's Temptation
blurb: A winding river that flows through the eastern portion of Milrar Forest before emerging onto the Steppe of Aziirn, named for a legendary explorer whose…

name: Ezuri
blurb: Tree-dwelling gnomes who fled jungle disease into the canopy; value connection to nature and communicate freely with animals.

name: Ezz
blurb: The universal substrate—the thought-and-emotion medium in which the planes hang. Not itself a plane.

name: Ezzal Artifacts
blurb: Some materials and items of Alaria were imbued with ezz during the Ezz Rift.

name: Ezzaróth
blurb: Domain: beasts, the hunt, predator-prey; dead cohort (Golden Age); the wild counterpart to Aedorath, outlasting her by a single bad decade.

name: Factions & Powers
blurb: How organized power works in Alaria: the churches, orders, alliances, and cabals that outlast any single ruler.

name: Faeja
blurb: Condensed Faesong of the Lethos jungle worn in a small body; iridescent jungle-dwellers each bound to one current of the world's feeling, which they tend and guard to the death.

name: Faeries
blurb: Beings of light, children of Aurus, who descend from the Astral Plane during Faesummer — and hear Faesong without being made of it.

name: Faesong
blurb: Melera's imprisoned song still leaking into the world — the emotion-face of Ezz, which condenses into the fae where it pools thick.

name: Faesummer
blurb: The long warm phase when Aurus drifts near the Material boundary and the creatures of the Astral Plane spill down into Alaria.

name: Faewoods
blurb: A forest in Old Tolaria that should not exist.

name: Fallen Angels
blurb: The Fallen Angels are celestial beings cast down from their eternal war among the stars, their wings clipped as punishment for bringing their conflict to…

name: Fallen Tower
blurb: Home to The Fallen

name: Falsehaven Bay
blurb: A broad, sheltered bay on Central Aboyinzu's western coast, where Satyr Wood meets the sea.

name: Fantasy Lakes
blurb: Home to many rusalka and dwarven skeletons.

name: Farlands
blurb: The Farlands occupy the western coastal region of Upoceax—a vast stretch of land running nearly 600 miles from north to south.

name: Fata M'darah
blurb: A trading post and waystation on the eastern edge of Kura, where the dunes begin to give way to the rocky terrain approaching the Wyrm…

name: Fatiik Forest
blurb: A transitional forest in central-southern Atriik, straddling the border between settled Kor lands and the contested Bloodlands to the south.

name: Favind (Village)
blurb: Population: ~250

name: Fengruk
blurb: Sacred smiths of Gondurak who treat the forge as a life metaphor; patient teachers shaped by the belief that hardship and discipline temper true potential.

name: Feriptus
blurb: Feriptus is a very powerful lich, living in his reclusive fortress Ipt, located in the far North, near the Nirvanis Mountains.

name: Fernius Sound
blurb: The body of water east of Petrona Septia Island, separating the island from the scattered archipelagos and open waters beyond.

name: Fernling Whist
blurb: Halfling survivor-chronicler; the sole eyewitness account of the World Fire from within a halfling settlement.

name: Feyatrov
blurb: A lost Uline mine settlement in Ubrik's northern field, the last to send word south before the outbreak swallowed the high galleries.

name: Feycreek
blurb: A small stream in western Tangiern where fey activity is unusually high.

name: Fieri
blurb: Underground halflings of Hell Creek in Chimea who built civilization from molten stone, believing they were chosen by the fires below.

name: Filgerran II
blurb: King of the Güli giants from Gülheim for over two centuries, ceremonial arbiter of the clans, lately restless and looking west toward Camaran.

name: Findra, the Glassy Eyed
blurb: A Force dragon who lost her eyes three centuries ago and replaced them with something better.

name: Findra, the Glassy Eyed
blurb: A Force dragon who lairs in the northeastern Pindolin Islands, on the small island northwest of Tidewatcher's Perch.

name: Finori
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Fire
blurb: The Kethic element of flame and heat, channeled through the Yolus leyline, whose surfacing beneath Tarkhetan feeds the Evertorch and the Firemage Corps.

name: Firefell
blurb: The shattered remains of an ancient Neferati colony in the Ember Isles—a city that tried to harness volcanic power and paid the price.

name: Firefrost Hills
blurb: A transitional zone between the southern Caffas Mountains and the White Wastes, where the elevation drops from mountain heights to the flat expanse of the…

name: Fireknife Mountains
blurb: Part of the greater Wurmspine range, the Fireknife is a volcanic segment along the northwestern border of Kerwin, marking the boundary between settled Tarkhon territory…

name: First Brotherhood
blurb: The ancient pan-dwarf coalition, now a dues-collecting union three city-states monopolize, whose two patron gods are fed by the diaspora it shuts out.

name: Firstiddle
blurb: An eastern mountain town on the coastal side of the Seawatch range, serving as a waypoint between the highlands and the coast.

name: Fleimrut Mountains
blurb: Extremely treacherous.

name: Flejdeaako Yni River
blurb: The primary river feeding Sopona Fia, flowing from the Torulius Mountains through the northern lowlands.

name: Flesh Giant
blurb: Colossal, undead giant.

name: Floating Islands of Aleve Akribus
blurb: A cluster of islands that hang suspended in the air above the sea, southwest of the Torguli Islands chain.

name: Flurin
blurb: The northernmost of the three Kendrel Kingdoms, occupying a chain of barrier islands and submerged reef-cities along the eastern edge of the Agreben Sea.

name: Foedros
blurb: Domains: contract, the held signature; rising cohort; patron of the Aldriktch Trade Alliance's cross-state notaries.

name: Foggy Isles
blurb: A fog-drowned island cluster in the northern Shattered Sea where the mist never lifts. The Veilkeepers sell sanctuary here to anyone who must vanish.

name: Foggy Mountains
blurb: The Foggy Mountains form the eastern anchor of the Dalizi Highlands' mountain wall, a range perpetually shrouded in mist that has never been fully explored.

name: Fool's Keep
blurb: A crumbling fortress on the western coast, now serving as a minor garrison and watchtower.

name: Fool's Pass
blurb: Fool's Pass is the only known crossing through the Thundering Mountains, a treacherous route that connects the Dalizi Highlands to the Wanderlands beyond.

name: Force
blurb: The Kethic element of momentum and weight, channeled through the Dynus leyline. Plain physical force, never the mind-driven 'force' of the Psywinds.

name: Forest of Avarice
blurb: A dense, ancient woodland marking the eastern boundary of Echem Yiakraxes, the Forest of Avarice earned its name from the prospectors and treasure-hunters who enter…

name: Forest of Durnos
blurb: Southeastern forest, wilder than Gleiwood.

name: Forest of Esperanza
blurb: The northern reaches of Azawahasi give way to the Forest of Esperanza, a dense woodland that marks the transition between savanna and the more temperate…

name: Forest of Statues
blurb: A stretch of the Elder Wilds where the Stillness, a permanent Izzus breach, runs time unevenly and wears the unwary into standing stone.

name: Forsaken of Anubis
blurb: Those who turned from Anubis, wielding inherited powers as self-appointed judges who feed souls to Ammit with no divine restriction.

name: Fort Tenze
blurb: The cursed ruins of a once-vital fortress on Pyris Island's eastern coast.

name: Foxborne
blurb: Chivalric fox-riding gnomes of Cherry Blossom Valley, built around courtly traditions and honor above all else.

name: Fozyader
blurb: The capital of Roule sprawls along the northeastern coast where the Arins Plains meet the sea.

name: Fragenstor Mielthøn
blurb: The great forest of Tangiern—hundreds of square miles of dark conifer woodland covering most of the human kingdom's territory.

name: Free Colonies
blurb: A confederation of settlements across the Ayashk archipelago, governed by joint assembly at Ashk.

name: Free Isles
blurb: A western Upoceax archipelago of four wealthy trading city-states, each ruled by one of the great merchant families under the Blood Pact.

name: Freshwater Sound
blurb: A large bay or sound dominating the northwestern coast, providing Qintas with access to maritime trade.

name: Frialve
blurb: The Frialve are a bird-like race residing in the sprawling jungle city of Bygos Shemazari.

name: Frost Fall
blurb: 8,840–8,695 BSD. For 145 years the water plane Pelus drifted close against the material world, and a deep cold settled over the south.

name: Frostgrim
blurb: Domains: frozen waste, hook-and-line survival, the catch pulled through the ice; current cohort; Hookling patron of what comes up when the line is hauled.

name: Frostmarch Peninsula
blurb: The Frostmarch Peninsula is a long landmass that juts eastward from the Celedrim Plains, forming the mainland anchor of the Northern Isles archipelago.

name: Frostrien (Town)
blurb: Population: ~1,200

name: Frostwalkers
blurb: Humanoid figures of animated ice and frozen flesh that emerge from the Pale Peaks during deep winter, drawn northward toward heat and life.

name: Frostwing Island
blurb: A large island off the southeastern Dragon's Spine Coast, separated from the mainland by roughly five miles of treacherous water.

name: Frowning Forest
blurb: A twisted woodland in the east where the trees grow in downward-curving shapes, their branches drooping like disapproving brows.

name: Frozen Sea
blurb: A frozen sea in Venalthier.

name: Fulon
blurb: A fishing town of perhaps three thousand souls, nestled in a protected cove between the Hammer Points.

name: Further Sound
blurb: The open water east of Phyr Island, connecting to the Sea of Fools to the south.

name: Fury Hills
blurb: The Fury Hills are a range of rugged highlands in southeastern Kadroka, forming part of the border zone between Kadrokan territory and Tythikerys.

name: Fya'haa
blurb: A dragon of the Western Isles.

name: Fylereün
blurb: A secondary peak in the Mount Tolaria range, once home to an astronomical observatory operated by the mage-kings.

name: Fylvrae Sylvrym
blurb: One of Alaria's largest continuous forests, Fylvrae Sylvrym blankets the western half of Wycendeula—a sea of ancient trees stretching roughly 700 miles north to south…

name: Fynol
blurb: A fishing and raiding settlement on Tykos's southern edge.

name: Fyrsan Al Troikas
blurb: A jagged mountain range forming the eastern boundary of Satyr Wood, running roughly north-south for over a hundred miles.

name: Gaea
blurb: The Earth Mother; creator of flesh life through the language Kethic; now diffused into the world she made.

name: Gaeic Melodies
blurb: Gaeic Melodies are the lingering echo of Gaea's creation-song, still reverberating in living things she made — caught and reproduced by mortal bards as a learned craft.

name: Galebound
blurb: Beings of the high open air into whom Sulus fused where it pressed close enough to bind, left thin and scattered, animate but not living.

name: Gallows Giant
blurb: A giant—still huge, but horribly malnourished.

name: Galore
blurb: A farming town in central Camaran, north of the capital Rathscar.

name: Gamgara
blurb: The capital, a surface settlement above the main mine complex.

name: Gamori
blurb: Nocturnal jungle elves who ride giant bats on territorial raids; matriarchal moon-worshippers who have rejected civilization for canopy savagery.

name: Gaplands
blurb: A stretch of open terrain west of The Glog where the Dragon's Spine briefly relents, creating the only significant lowland passage along this section of…

name: Garlow
blurb: The industrial heart of the Twin Cities, Garlow produces the weapons, traps, and supplies that keep Yuki's defenses functional.

name: Garum
blurb: The primary port on Vystrilik's eastern coast.

name: Gavelos
blurb: Domain: sea, navigation, safe passage; current cohort; patron of maritime trade, arose in the Great Expansion.

name: Gelnor

name: Instruxofinum

name: Inthikrilix Dungeon
blurb: On the northern side of the Nysanna Range, where water and force leylines intersect, lies the Inthikrilix Dungeon—a massive fortress carved into the mountainside.

name: Iorsi the Terrible
blurb: Brutal, cruel dragon of wind, snow, and ice.

name: Ios
blurb: The southwestern state on Petrona Septia Island, Ios occupies the lands between the Screech Peaks and the sea.

name: Iqes
blurb: A large, heavily forested island off the Seacleft Coast, roughly three hundred miles long and two hundred fifty miles wide.

name: Iqes Sound
blurb: The body of water south of Iqes, between the island and the Upoceax mainland.

name: Iques
blurb: Land of coral and black gold (micah).

name: Íritiv
blurb: Domains: theft, attention, alacrity; current cohort; patron of those who live by speed and sleight.

name: Iron Academy
blurb: The greatest combat school in Alaria, and the de facto capital of Knova.

name: Ironforge
blurb: The industrial heart of the kingdom, positioned in the central valley where ore deposits are richest.

name: Ishnit Jungles
blurb: The lethal jungle ringing the southern Dunes of Kunagi, ruled in pieces by two false-dragon gods, three Naruaghin confederations, and a bottomless rift.

name: Islands of Gold
blurb: The southernmost archipelago of the Western Isles—fantastically rich in gold and utterly deadly to reach.

name: Isle of the Night
blurb: West of the mainland, situated in the waters between Moigos and the Tyvern Isles, lies the Isle of the Night — a mysterious island enveloped…

name: Ismail (Port City)
blurb: Where the Honor River empties into Munari Inlet stands Ismail, Avalon's window to the world.

name: Issasmir
blurb: A northern port town, smaller than Kair but prosperous.

name: Issoroth
blurb: The thing reassembling itself beneath the Veluminante from the wreck of a Glyssen sky-worm and the minds of everyone who comes too near.

name: Istokos
blurb: A southern island nation in the Western Isles, home to the Chargon—an eel race that has rejected their aquatic heritage in favor of land-dwelling and…

name: Istor XXVI
blurb: The murdered last king of the unified Winter Elves, whose death split the realm into Klevnaf and Istora and fed fresh shadow into Murder Creek.

name: Istora
blurb: The eastern half of the broken Winter Elf kingdom, ruled by Queen Lamenrae, the first woman to claim the elven throne.

name: Istori
blurb: Ice-glass-forging elves of frozen Istora, torn between traditionalists preserving culture through art and industrialists weaponizing their unbreakable craft.

name: Istori Agnostus
blurb: A dead city in the Wastes of Al G'hesh, emptied not by war or plague but by the pixies who once wintered in its walls.

name: Ix'Lorett
blurb: Iguana-like contemplatives of M'Svyla's forests and Crystal Mountains; philosophers and artists with legendary regenerative abilities who prize beauty.

name: Ix'Meglyakuk
blurb: Raptor-bonded Lizardfolk of the Dygon Beastlands; sacred rider culture built on lifelong bonds with utahraptors since birth.

name: Ix'Moreth
blurb: Domains: crystal-light, contemplative stillness, the unmoved eye; current cohort; Ix'Lorett patron of the long looking.

name: Ix'Tyrann
blurb: Numerically dominant Lizardfolk of Da Trang's swamps, ruled by chieftain strength and brutality; battle scars are prized, weakness cast out.

name: Ix'Vashlu
blurb: Domains: poisoner's patience, oyster-and-blade, the pearl that kills; current cohort; Ix'Vasyla patron of the slow kill.

name: Ix'Vasyla
blurb: Black-scaled venomous assassins of Nashua's toxic marshlands; patient hunters waging endless cunning war against Trømgar and Gorath.

name: Ix'Vranok
blurb: Domains: chieftain rule, swamp-blood, the kill that proves the throne; current cohort; Ix'Tyrann patron of succession by combat.

name: Ixchanil
blurb: The closest thing the Tamadrezan north has to a resistance leader, holding a rising back until a Gorathi succession crisis opens the door.

name: Ixvelaran
blurb: Lizardfolk linguist; the authority on the Ix- naming tradition and the reconstruction of the lizardfolk proto-language.

name: Iyaklomori Grera
blurb: The great forest at the heart of Illron.

name: Iyalmr
blurb: A settlement in the northern part of Tora Mazîl, at the edge of the Oto Q'Azadi marsh.

name: Iylovia
blurb: The forests of lies.

name: Iypos
blurb: The dominant city-state on the island of Migos—a trading hub perched on the southeastern crag, controlling the island's only sheltered deepwater harbor.

name: Jabok Lake
blurb: The smallest and southernmost of the western lakes, Jabok is nearly dry—a muddy depression that fills only during heavy rains.

name: Jackal Mountains
blurb: The Jackal Mountains form the central spine of the Dalizi Highlands, a vast wall of stone that separates the eastern ranges from the western approaches…

name: Jahazai Desert
blurb: The vast interior desert.

name: Jaipon
blurb: The ruined fortress-city of Jaipon stands at the edge of Blisterswamp, its towers still visible above the jungle canopy after two centuries of abandonment.

name: Jamtaland
blurb: A northern mountain town positioned at a key pass through the Drystone Mountains.

name: Jeh Bli
blurb: A Xi'ivrach orc city-state on the shores of Lake Kro Nymos, in the remote northern Farlands.

name: Jiji River
blurb: A river in Nashua.

name: Jingu
blurb: Domain: flame; current cohort; twin sister of Kiev, one of the Flame Twins.

name: Jinxz Plains
blurb: The Jinxz Plains occupy the southern stretch of the central grasslands, named (according to the goblins) for the bad luck that follows anyone who crosses…

name: Joswik
blurb: Joswik occupies southern Ofrenia, a land of mountains, fertile valleys, and windswept plateau.

name: Jotunhills
blurb: The Jotunhills are a range of rolling highlands southwest of Konai Mora, home to the Jotun—smaller frost giants with cold attunement and remarkable regenerative abilities.

name: Jotuns
blurb: Frost giants.

name: Jowdo
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Julrus the Fierce
blurb: Sand dragon.

name: Julrus the Fierce
blurb: A dragon of Westrim.

name: Jungle of Spines
blurb: The transformed jungle covering most of Phyr Island's western and central territory.

name: Jungles of Godahi
blurb: The Jungles of Godahi form the vast interior basin of the northern Emerald Coast—a sea of green canopy stretching roughly 300 miles east to west…

name: Jungles of Titania
blurb: The green heart of Gorath.

name: Jungula Serpentine
blurb: The northwestern peninsula, a finger of jungle extending toward the Sea of Seven Snakes.

name: Juniper Swamp
blurb: A fetid wetland in the heart of Moonwood, where the forest floor drops into permanent marsh.

name: Jüt
blurb: A port city of roughly twenty thousand people positioned on Kettle Bay, where the Westwilds coast meets the sea.

name: K'rovaxi
blurb: Domains: warband honor, forge and blood, the Iron Father; current cohort; pan-orc patron of the oath given with the axe.

name: Kabilcuz
blurb: The oldest port in Roule, Kabilcuz on the northwestern coast is where halfling colonists first made landfall three centuries ago.

name: Kabir
blurb: The northeastern Kingdom of Fire, facing the Sea of Merchants.

name: Kadroka
blurb: Kadroka is a savanna region in southwestern Central Aboyinzu, dominated by open grasslands and the drainage basin of the Royko River system.

name: Kaerath
blurb: Domain: forge fire, metalcraft; dead cohort (Golden Age); fire deity whose sacred site was consumed by the Kajiit eruption.

name: Kafula
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Kagoro
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kair
blurb: The capital city, built around a natural harbor on Elessi Bay.

name: Kalemeydani
blurb: A city-state in the United Free Nations.

name: Kama Sa'malina
blurb: The lake district of southern Wycendeula—hundreds of bodies of water scattered across roughly three thousand square miles of boggy forest and scrubland, pressing against the…

name: Kameesi Jungle
blurb: The Kameesi Jungle dominates the northwestern peninsula of Urok—a dense, ancient rainforest where the canopy closes so thick that twilight persists even at midday.

name: Kampaga
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kanamazi
blurb: "Those Who Walk Without Light"

name: Kanguka
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kanüli Daha Plains
blurb: The entire steppe is slightly magical.

name: Kanzekill
blurb: A rust-orange Force dragon who has hunted the same quarry for over four centuries.

name: Kanzekill
blurb: The lair of Kanzekill, a rust-orange Force dragon who has hunted the hidden fortress of Mountainveil for over four centuries.

name: Kappa
blurb: The Kappa are massive, toad-like beings that dominate the criminal underbelly of Moigos.

name: Kapsayi
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Karahoroz
blurb: Avalon's eastern city, Karahoroz sits where the Honor River approaches Dern.

name: Karchon
blurb: The Karchon are shark-headed humanoids from the Shattered Sea.

name: Kareshi
blurb: Founder of the Waterdark order and the master who cut every dragon-bond in Adron; remembered as the realm's reluctant savior.

name: Kargak
blurb: The capital city covers most of the main island's northern coast.

name: Karik Hills
blurb: Hills within the Grendenheim region.

name: Karnaeum
blurb: Domains: magma-interior, sacred fury, passion-as-discipline; current cohort; Verucan patron of the fire one lives inside.

name: Kaspion
blurb: The old capital of Mueras, built on a dramatic headland overlooking Corpse Bay.

name: Kassander Volso
blurb: Tribune of Slavewatch, the corrupt officer who polices the slave passes by selling passage through them to everyone at once.

name: Katat
blurb: An agricultural village upriver from Glezamos, where the Zelle cultivate the jungle's bounty: fruits that induce prophetic dreams, fungi that enhance blood-magic, flowers whose nectar…

name: Kavnendal
blurb: The southernmost major settlement, positioned where the mountains give way to foothills.

name: Kazisezi
blurb: A location in Kafula.

name: Kazül
blurb: A desert kingdom on the southern Middle Sea coast, ruled by King Salomor and his legendary summoning ring.

name: Kebuz Bakh
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Kedari Emnath
blurb: A region on Mount Kajiit's slopes where obsidian flows have created fields of volcanic glass.

name: Kelder Mountains
blurb: The bracket-shaped range along the eastern Farlands, a titan graveyard whose buried bones are the source of most of the world's titan bone.

name: Keletus
blurb: The Lorean minotaur democracy occupying Apketrii island.

name: Kelwin
blurb: Controls the central passage through Labyrinth Sound—the Kelwin Throat, the most technically demanding stretch of navigation in the region.

name: Kendor
blurb: The Kendor are an ancient aquatic people whose grace and beauty are matched only by their mastery of courtly intrigue.

name: Kendrel
blurb: Pearl-iridescent Kendor masters of etiquette and social manipulation who inhabit hidden palace-fortresses beneath tropical reefs.

name: Kerikos
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kerwin
blurb: The northeastern territory of the Tarkhon Empire—though "territory" implies more control than Tarkhon actually exercises.

name: Keshwindi
blurb: The city-state of the nameless.

name: Kessus Lake
blurb: Eastern lake providing Sy with water and a secondary food source.

name: Kestrel
blurb: Another tree-settlement, positioned between the two forests.

name: Kethic
blurb: Kethic is the caster's own emotion structured through the Elemental Planes via leylines—Gaea's bridge-language for bringing flesh into being.

name: Kettle Bay
blurb: A sheltered natural harbor at the base of the Cliffs of Shondromos—calm waters surrounded by honeycombed cliffs riddled with caves.

name: Khalweir
blurb: Domains: ancestor-voice, the unbroken line, the wind that still answers when called; current cohort; Neth patron of the dead who are not gone, only colder.

name: Kharvorn Mountains
blurb: The Kharvorn Mountains are a massive diagonal range that defines the heart of Clueanda, running from the center-north down to the southeast and spanning roughly…

name: Khravobi
blurb: Domains: blade discipline, the sworn companion, the cut that completes the thought; current cohort; Shontobi patron of the bond between swordsman and sworn brother.

name: Khurgril
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Kibuhanda
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kidwood
blurb: The southern forest, between the Aphenlein system and the Dalizi Highlands.

name: Kiev
blurb: Domain: flame; current cohort; twin sister of Jingu, one of the Flame Twins.

name: Kilik
blurb: The most forward outpost of Roule as it ventures into Rakite.

name: Killing Hills
blurb: The southern highlands between the Aktarvkus and Mythlias, where the mountains break down into a chaotic landscape of steep ridges and narrow valleys.

name: Kilren's Palace
blurb: The fortress King Kilren raised in the fae's forbidden wood; his ten thousand vanished overnight, and he came home a crown with no one inside.

name: Kimril
blurb: Goat legged, crazy earthling creatures.

name: King Rashaan
blurb: King of Kabir, fifty-two, risen from the merchant houses. He means to reclaim Tarkhon through trade, not war. His rivals call it collaboration.

name: King Samaryn
blurb: King of Myorna.

name: Kingdoms of Fire
blurb: The Kingdoms of Fire are four Neferati monarchies—Yaif, Kabir, Gissemari, and Wadiyah—that occupy a peninsula in southwestern Tarkhon.

name: Kirthdron Soytas
blurb: The southernmost of the abandoned harvesting regions in Deo Esari's territory.

name: Kishuntar
blurb: Harbor-Reeve of Nadang and the Tamadrezan end of Slavewatch's navigation-fee system. Secretly, the Moon Road's single most important asset.

name: Kisoy
blurb: The second major city, located just north of the southern mountains.

name: Klevnaf
blurb: The western remnant of the once-unified Winter Elf kingdom, claiming the throne through Prince Taoinor, uncle to the murdered King Istor XXVI.

name: Klokas
blurb: Klokas is a vast void of empty space filled with huge demonic creatures floating through the skies.

name: Klor the Blood Lord
blurb: The Tarkhon general who cleared the Eronia Range two centuries ago—honored as empire's greatest hero, remembered by others as its greatest butcher.

name: Klovuk
blurb: A west-central town positioned between the jungle and the mountain foothills.

name: Kmokosiri
blurb: A forest within Shyona.

name: Knova
blurb: An archipelago in the northern Western Isles, south of the Iron Sea.

name: Knulak
blurb: Knulak is a forest-and-lake region in the north-central Frostmarch Peninsula, named for the cluster of interconnected lakes at its center.

name: Kobuk
blurb: Kobuk is a rugged mountain territory in the central Wurmspine range, bounded by the Hellion Hills to the north and the Hills to Nowhere to…

name: Koilos
blurb: The forest covering southern Ios, stretching from the Dipindre valley to the coast.

name: Koisokamba
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kokotintin
blurb: The jewel of the Free Isles and the wealthiest city in southern Alaria.

name: Koldkin Hills
blurb: Hills in the Dragon's Spine Coast.

name: Konai Mora
blurb: East of the Bitterlands, where the mountains curve toward the coast, lies Soya Grei—a peninsula of rocky highlands overlooking Soothesong Sound.

name: Kopanes
blurb: A mining settlement in the western valleys, where Breian prospectors extract copper and tin from the mountain rock.

name: Kor
blurb: The ruling class of Koren society, bound by intricate customs where every gesture carries meaning and exile is worse than death.

name: Koras
blurb: The Starborn tender of the sun's celestial-glass core; once the sun's master, now its caretaker, serving Bryn since the sun woke.

name: Korath
blurb: The Korel pack-lead who breaks Edari's one law for outsiders, guiding a single Nameless mercenary through the savannah in exchange for blades.

name: Korel
blurb: Feral Koren who embrace their predatory nature, living by instinct in loose packs as scouts, thieves, and mercenaries.

name: Koren
blurb: The Koren are a feline people with the grace and power of great cats.

name: Korgül
blurb: Home to Greater Satyr

name: Korlan
blurb: Slaver minotaurs of the Tykos island chain who kept the Lorean's tactical genius and cold logic while inverting their protective ethos into systematized dominance.

name: Korn
blurb: Domains: caste-order, labor as prayer, the work that holds the world up; current cohort; pan-koren patron of the load borne.

name: Korrun
blurb: A stone-touched people born of Golus, Ezz, and Gaea's song of creation: fully alive, patient and unmoving, holding the Blueshale Peaks as their own.

name: Kos Askati
blurb: A river flowing from the Myjornis range northward into Qet Yemani territory, where the settlement of Tel Askati sits along its banks.

name: Kosko
blurb: A Bogie state on Pesalolo, immediately south of Tamadrez in the Shacklands.

name: Krakkek
blurb: Domain: failed sacrifice, the broken altar, theology that didn't take; dead cohort (Craggus era); chthonic god of the cults that never consolidated, killed almost incidentally.

name: Kraselium
blurb: Domains: memory-in-exile, cultural preservation, education-as-survival; current cohort; Drasnian patron who walks beside the diaspora.

name: Krathokh
blurb: Domain: the anchor-vessel rite, the moment of binding daemon to conduit; dead cohort (Golden Age); chthonic god of the act, whose death made every other Golden-Age god's death inevitable.

name: Krazhaya
blurb: Domains: giant-blood inheritance, sea-storm endurance, the body that remembers what it descended from; current cohort; Qord'ik patron carried over from the migration coasts.

name: Kregkard
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Krell
blurb: The Krell are an ancient insectoid race whose very existence revolves around their massive colonial structure and absolute devotion to their queen.

name: Krell Lands
blurb: A colossal expanse of jungle in southeastern Ve, consumed by the Krell over the past three centuries.

name: Krenglelan Range
blurb: A vast mountain range spanning the northern Westwilds from coast to interior, the Krenglelan forms a natural barrier between the frozen Northlands and the temperate…

name: Krinea Mikea
blurb: A marsh in the Wanderlands.

name: Krizan
blurb: The central village, positioned where Edko Sound narrows toward Prince's Bay.

name: Kro Nymos
blurb: The lake at the heart of Xi'ivrach territory—a crescent-shaped body of water cradled in a mountain valley, its western shore rising into the Sunset Mountains,…

name: Kro Shiik
blurb: The central lake of Rakite—a long, shallow body of water fed by the Ver Pes river and drained toward the eastern coast.

name: Kroikylo's Tomb
blurb: Located at the exact intersection of the force and void leylines on Terwin Lake, Kroikylo's Tomb is one of the most dangerous dungeons in Alaria.

name: Krondeum
blurb: Domains: hammered iron, anvil-craft, the hammer-stroke; current cohort; pan-dwarf forge patron of the First Brotherhood.

name: Krunites
blurb: Domains: iron, industrial discipline, the lit shaft against the dark; current cohort; Uline patron of the first ore from a new vein.

name: Kryaaji
blurb: Sacred sun elves of the golden House of the Sun, trainers of luminant Wysynnbre eagles, isolated guardians of light and purity.

name: Kryviel
blurb: Domains: winter-as-entropy, emotional vampirism, the cold that hollows; current cohort; Ikriel patron of the predatory feed.

name: Kumi Wood
blurb: North of Umlas, Kumi Wood stretches from the foothills of the Pearly Mountains to the coast near the Bay of Lords.

name: Kunagi Hills
blurb: A transitional range of scrub-covered ridges and rocky outcrops marking the boundary between Atriik's settled lands and the Dunes of Kunagi proper.

name: Kung
blurb: An isolated village in the southwest interior, far from the pilgrim routes.

name: Kura
blurb: A state in Westrim, occupying the western margins of the Dunes of Kunagi and the southeastern foothills of the mountains that separate Westrim from Tarkhon.

name: Kuru
blurb: Southern humans who fuse with beast spirits through ancient skull rituals, gaining primal power and instincts at the cost of human speech.

name: Kuzagt
blurb: Tall, spindly bone-featured elves of Bonnetaz's salt flats who maintain vast human slave camps, viewing humans as invaders to be exploited.

name: Kuzvarakt
blurb: Domains: salt, the chain, the broken human; current cohort; Kuzagt slaver-patron of the Bonnetaz camps.

name: Kyagos
blurb: A mountainous island roughly 400 miles across, Kyagos rises from the waters between the Sea of Seven Snakes and the Alrock Ocean like a clenched…

name: Kyedibugyo
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kyorda
blurb: A small state on the southern coast of the Frostmarch Peninsula, clinging to the harsh shoreline where the peninsula meets the Denrik Sea.

name: Kyth
blurb: The Kyth are spectral ghost-like creatures that inhabit the Nirvanis Mountains.

name: Kyullyuk
blurb: In the heart of the Murderwood, the survivors built a town.

name: L'Coth D'hari
blurb: The northern peninsula of the Elder Wilds—a multi-lobed landmass of dense jungle surrounded by the South Sea.

name: Labakh
blurb: The northernmost city in Kura, Labakh sits in the foothills where the dunes give way to the mountains of the Eronia Range.

name: Labyrinth Sound
blurb: The defining feature of western Mueras: a maze of channels threading between cliff-walled islands, sea stacks, and submerged rock formations.

name: Lacire
blurb: A city of perhaps fifteen thousand people built on and around the Springs of Vyowehr, deep within Ierya at the heart of the Walking Forest.

name: Lacirean
blurb: Gnomes of the Walking Forest who have given up speech, descended from Postronamas refugees and raised from birth in the silent sign-tongue called Stillspeech.

name: Lady
blurb: A teal dragon who has made her lair at the intersection of Force and Void leylines, somewhere beneath the waters or islands near the Tyvern…

name: Laeren
blurb: The settled elves of the Farlands' Three Kingdoms, a treaty-bound people who keep their true names as fiercely as their borders.

name: Laeroth Esori
blurb: The meeting ground of the elven confederation.

name: Lake Morgan and the Erginya River
blurb: Lake Morgan sits in the northwestern corner of The Glog, a cold, deep body of water approximately two miles across at its widest point.

name: Lake of Rainbows
blurb: A lake in the Crimson Coast.

name: Lake Shikaya
blurb: The lake still exists, now surrounded by ruins rather than palatial estates.

name: Lake Splendor
blurb: A small alpine lake nestled in a cirque between the highest peaks of the central Widebarrow Mountains, on the border between Dern and Thorum.

name: Lake Teklan
blurb: A large freshwater lake providing Tekos with water, fish, and a natural defensive barrier on one flank.

name: Lake Tonactlet Chipe
blurb: The defining geographic feature of the region—a massive freshwater lake roughly 80 miles long and 40 miles wide at its broadest point.

name: Lamenrae
blurb: Queen of Istora and first woman to claim the Winter Elf throne; daughter of the murdered Istor XXVI, certain past all argument that Taoinor, her father's uncle, killed him.

name: Languages
blurb: Alaria has a handful of major spoken languages and many minor ones; Northern Commonfolk comes closest to a shared tongue.

name: Lanthornia
blurb: A planar stack neighbouring Alaria where Aether is the native energy. An eruption there flung Aether and Lanthornium onto Alaria, seeding its whole sky-trade.

name: Lanthornium
blurb: An uncommon gray-blue stone from the Lanthornia stack; purified and vibrated at frequency, it makes struck Aether discharge the work an Aether engine runs on.

name: Lanwadanzi
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Larsoia Lake
blurb: A larger lake in the central Grognil, partially fed by underground springs that keep sections from freezing completely.

name: Last Chance
blurb: High on the northern face of the Nysanna Range, where the peaks give way to the polar wastes beyond, stands the ruined fortress of Last…

name: Lazaro
blurb: The speculator who got rich betting that slave prices would only ever climb. The Kyagos uprising broke that bet, and now it is breaking him.

name: Lean D'Kas
blurb: The northwestern section, named for reasons lost.

name: Lean D'Riin
blurb: The central and most ancient section of the Eyendra forest.

name: Leather Skeleton
blurb: Leather skeletons look like horribly malnourished humanoids—just skin and bones.

name: Leering Wood
blurb: Northern forest with an unsettling reputation.

name: Lekia
blurb: The cultural co-capital, a coastal city on Whitefin Bay famous for its amphitheaters, festival grounds, and the Philosopher's Wharf—a long stone pier where Scalawag thinkers…

name: Lemru Nymor
blurb: The southeastern hills, between Kidwood and the coast.

name: Lenora
blurb: The agricultural heartland of the elven confederation, occupying the fertile lowlands and coastal forests of the western Farlands.

name: Lesser Satyr
blurb: Feral, chaotic satyrs who spread panic and terror, preferring ambush and misdirection over direct combat.

name: Lethos
blurb: A waterlogged jungle peninsula extending south into the Sea of Sighs, Lethos is one of the smallest and strangest states in the Shacklands.

name: Lethring
blurb: Domains: shadow, lightless cunning, ambush-patience; current cohort; Darkling goblin patron of the predator's dark.

name: Lev's Salvation
blurb: A prominent rock formation in the northeastern Westwilds where the open grassland meets the foothills approaching the Kharvorn range.

name: Levke
blurb: The capital, carved into the heart of the central Thunderdark range.

name: Liches
blurb: A lich is a being that has split its spirit in two, and usually, is using another being’s soul and shadow to remain alive.

name: Lier
blurb: Lier occupies the central portion of Erpeus Island, sandwiched between the gnomish mountains of Mkinti to the south and the monastic peaks of Azuros to…

name: Life and Death
blurb: A creature’s life is dictated by the health and union of their soul, shadow, and spirit.

name: Light
blurb: The Kethic element of radiance, channeled through the Kunus leyline. Prized by sun-temples who credit Bryn for power borrowed from the leyline stack.

name: Lightning Raptor
blurb: Giant pterodactyl like creatures that travel in thunderstorms, drag prey up to sky where they get struck by lightning.

name: Limoste
blurb: Southwestern coastal city on the Chedwin Strait.

name: Linas
blurb: A city-state on the Seacleft Coast.

name: Lindworms
blurb: More vicious, stupid descendents of dragons.

name: Lise
blurb: The capital of Enapay and seat of the Dragonbound Queen.

name: Lizard's Maw
blurb: A distinctive inlet on the southwestern coast, where the Beastlands meet the Gulf of Chimea.

name: Lizardfolk
blurb: The Lizardfolk are an ancient people who walked Alaria long before the rise of the younger races.

name: Locquine
blurb: A city-state deep in the jungles of southern Ve, ruled by pixies whose perception of reality has fundamentally diverged from that of other races.

name: Lodrian Hills
blurb: Hills in Old Tolaria.

name: Lohva
blurb: A lost Uline mine settlement in Ubrik's northern field, built above the Dnyrrak Cut where the first slick silver vein was struck.

name: Lomas Radiales
blurb: A town in the Sothills, serving as the center of Qintas's pastoral economy.

name: Lonely Lakes
blurb: Lakes in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Lonesome Lake
blurb: Lonesome Lake is a small, isolated body of water in northern Kadroka, notable for having no visible inlet or outlet.

name: Lorean Minotaur
blurb: Tactically brilliant Minotaur commanders who place duty and protection of others above all; logic governs every decision while emotion is suppressed.

name: Lorus
blurb: Domain: darkness; current cohort; one of the oldest living daemons, surviving through primal fear rather than organized worship.

name: Lost Lake
blurb: High in the Sorgül cirques, hidden from casual view, lies Lost Lake—a dark, still body of water that the giants refuse to approach.

name: Loyera
