blurb: A lake in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Ntando
blurb: The forested western interior of Iqes, Ntando is the traditional Qindo heartland — the region where native culture survived most intact after foreign merchants took…

name: Nuin Bay
blurb: The southwestern coast curves around Nuin Bay, a sheltered natural harbor that makes Mu possible.

name: Nuun
blurb: Golden- or ice-eyed desert people of Kura; master traders who blend elemental magic with a deep reverence for beauty and knowledge.

name: Nuvia
blurb: A war-profiteer grown rich on Gorath's endless campaigns, now quietly funding the escape network that bets the empire will fall.

name: Nyavminthk Castle
blurb: A crumbling fortress at the heart of Echem Yiakraxes, Nyavminthk Castle is the reason the Terrogones are here—or at least, the reason they stay.

name: Nydor
blurb: Aureum breakaway who embraced darkness; tusked, ash-skinned master slavers of Emblydium who trade war machines for captives under the black sun.

name: Nyinsylys
blurb: Nyinsylys is a white dragon who claims the glacial highlands between the Nirvanis Mountains and the Knives, in the far northern reaches of the Kharvorn…

name: Nyinsylys's Domain
blurb: The glacial highlands between the Nirvanis Mountains and the Knives are claimed by the white dragon Nyinsylys.

name: Nykotheryx Amberylika
blurb: A dream-warped subregion of the Rootspire Wilds, lying east of Dalizi territory.

name: Nylaus Kraken
blurb: Giant sea-snake creature, with 100 foot diameter body.

name: Nylen (Capital)
blurb: The largest settlement in Windor sits where the main road from the south meets the primary pass into the Green Mountains.

name: Nymphs
blurb: Nymphs are servants of Aurus.

name: Nyolci
blurb: The Nyolci are a maritime people whose distinctive cephalopod features mark them as some of the finest sailors and navigators in the known world.

name: Nysanna Range
blurb: The Nysanna Range is one of the most inhospitable places on Alaria—and by some measures, the most dramatic.

name: Nyuonaw
blurb: The largest settlement in Da Trang, Nyuonaw sits in a clearing in the central jungle along the Nuodyak.

name: Oakheart
blurb: A town near Blue Lake, surrounded by old-growth forest.

name: Oblexan
blurb: Gnome empiricist writing near the end of the Lost Ages; trusts only what can be touched, measured, or dated.

name: Oblivion
blurb: A standing memory-storm northeast of the Emerald Coast, the dragon-father's last breath made weather; it scrubs the mind of any who enter, save Naga.

name: Oboros, the Cunning
blurb: A Water dragon who collects debts the way other dragons collect gold.

name: Oboros, the Cunning
blurb: A Water dragon who controls the central Pindolin Islands, moving through the Water leyline like a current himself.

name: Octavus Marsh
blurb: Where the Twilight River meets the bay, the land flattens into a broad coastal marsh known as Octavus Marsh.

name: Ofrenia
blurb: The largest island of the inner Middle Sea, a strategic crossing held by three sibling-kingdoms locked in a barely-managed feud.

name: Ogre
blurb: Ogres are massive, powerful greenskins standing around seven to eight feet tall with small tusks and hairy bodies.

name: Ogre Hills
blurb: The Ogre Hills occupy the eastern Troyan Mountains, a region of broken terrain that supports the largest concentration of ogres in the Dalizi Highlands.

name: Ogzwad Swamp
blurb: The southeastern corner of Phirexes, a remote marshland where the queen's control is weakest.

name: Ohblex
blurb: Crystalline gnomes born from Gemstone Peaks gems; sole clockmaking masters who sustain themselves on jewels and scheme endlessly.

name: Ojos Goros
blurb: A southern forest whose name translates to "watching eyes"—a reference to the sensation all visitors report of being observed from within the trees.

name: Okesha's Passage
blurb: The waterway on the northwest side of Erpeus Island, connecting different parts of the Middle Sea.

name: Oklyüne
blurb: A northwestern town near the borders with New Chimea and Da Trang.

name: Olarane
blurb: The Tolarian mage-queen who argued the Apparatus would not hold, was overruled, and stayed to stop it. Frozen mid-warning at Elderran's center.

name: Old One's Wood
blurb: A primordial forest in southern Phirexes that predates the fogs by millennia.

name: Old Tolaria
blurb: What remains of Alaria's magical heartland after the Tolarian mage-kings switched on the Apparatus of Severance at Elderran and tore the region apart.

name: Old Tyabb
blurb: A city within Chimea.

name: Olisea
blurb: Domains: green-water shore, new-land arrival, the shore that becomes home; current cohort; Arinsfold patron of the welcome and the silence that follows it.

name: Olivar
blurb: A Gorathi war-chaplain tranced in the Moon Wilds years ago and never consumed, now the living heart of the one Vexling brood that does not kill.

name: Olluréth
blurb: Domain: the moons, tidal magic, lunar timing; dead cohort (Golden Age); single goddess paired to both moons, lost when the Long Winter's cloud cover broke the cycle's visibility.

name: Omegra's Crypt
blurb: An Eloweir tomb in the southern hills of Eloesi, cut long before Old Tolaria fell and the Glass Forest watch was set.

name: Ondheil
blurb: A mountain lake in the eastern Cloud Mountains, fed by glacial melt and runoff.

name: Onnoróth
blurb: Domain: rivers, freshwater, irrigation; dead cohort (Golden Age); Aedorath's hydrological complement, drowned when the rivers froze.

name: Opela Sopona
blurb: A lakeside town on Sopona Strempele, the accessible lake.

name: Orangeport
blurb: The most corrupt town in Ve, and proud of it.

name: Orc
blurb: Orcs are a diverse and hardy race known for their physical prowess, tribal cultures, and fierce independence.

name: Orenna
blurb: The Tender of Candelum, who leads the Aureum resistance in the Blood Mountains and rations the last decanted sunlight her people own.

name: Ornath
blurb: Domain: sky, lightning, storm; dead cohort (Golden Age); storm deity consumed when the Long Winter's permanent overcast lasted decades.

name: Orwin
blurb: The first Foretold of Sestros, whose seawall miracle at Mu founded the Spoken Throne and, unknowingly, the debt that is bankrupting his god.

name: Oshtravak
blurb: Domain: the thawing waste, ash-and-snowmelt landscapes, the recovering wild; dead cohort (Craggus era); chthonic god of a domain that literally shrank under its worshippers.

name: Oskwatch Valley
blurb: A southern valley in the foothills, containing the settlement of Dy.

name: Oswald
blurb: A western settlement in the jungle lowlands, near the border with Da Trang and Vystrilik.

name: Ot'Pelyon
blurb: A ruined mining village in Tomgryir Eror, within Deo Esari's territory.

name: Otho
blurb: Domain: threshold, doorway, the act of crossing; dead cohort (Golden Age); a god of gesture-frequency, killed the day the doors stopped mattering.

name: Othyndria
blurb: The former capital, now Rothogomos's lair.

name: Oto Q'Azadi
blurb: A northern marsh in the upper reaches of Tora Mazîl.

name: Otrakh
blurb: Domain: madness, divine inspiration, the unhinged seer; dead cohort (Golden Age); chthonic god whose domain leaked past its channels and dissolved his cult.

name: Ovabasi
blurb: Near Ezcelmez Lake in the agricultural heartland, Ovabasi is the center of Roule's honey industry.

name: Ovuluria River
blurb: The lifeblood of Poum Titch—a major river flowing from the western highlands through the heart of the nation, connecting Tadinu to Lekia before emptying into…

name: Oxwen Amberyskos
blurb: First Speaker of the Bestacian republic, a Goshwen gnome managing three crises with a treasury and no army.

name: Oyster Bay
blurb: The smaller bay just south of Kair, and the source of Eloesi's famous pearls.

name: Ozimar
blurb: The Hammer-Primate of Gorath's war-faith, who means to put out every Drasnian forge-fire until nothing of the conquered remains to worship.

name: Oznak
blurb: Proud, fiercely competitive humans of Central North Alaria whose culture revolves around mastery of the blade and skill earned without cutting corners.

name: Oznaryn
blurb: Human desert-cultures specialist; expert on the pre-Expansion tribal confederacies of the Oznak and Tarnak peoples.

name: Ozravak
blurb: Domain: plague, sickness, contagion; dead cohort (Golden Age); the propitiated malevolence whose cult was the first to die when the Winter compressed populations indoors.

name: Padena
blurb: The capital city, nestled in a river valley in the northern interior.

name: Pain Priests
blurb: Traveling priests.

name: Pain Walkers
blurb: Cause slow, deep pain in a radius around them, growing more intense as you get closer.

name: Painted Hills
blurb: Hills in the Farlands.

name: Palace of Flickering Lights
blurb: A marble fortress at the northern edge of Tyror Miig, where the plains give way to the Ver Kanis headwaters.

name: Palace of Light
blurb: A fortress-monastery built directly over the point where the light leyline surfaces, located in the high Widebarrow Mountains on the border between Dern and Thorum.

name: Pale Death
blurb: The central-eastern expanse of Venalthier's frozen interior—a vast plain of ice and compacted snow stretching from The Face in the north to the Selengreyb Plateau…

name: Pale Peaks
blurb: The southernmost mountains of the Dragon's Spine, rising beyond the Shrapnel Strait in a wall of ice-sheathed stone.

name: Paradise Inlet
blurb: An inlet in L'Coth D'Hari.

name: Partsmouth
blurb: An interior Joswik settlement at the edge of the Singing Trees, where timber work and the strange sounds from the forest keep the town perpetually uneasy.

name: Pastrova
blurb: A town famous for one of the best law schools in Alaria.

name: Pathanul
blurb: A forest of ancient trees spreading across northern Avalon, where the kingdom's heartland meets the Sestran border.

name: Pavik's Cave
blurb: A coastal cave system on the western shore, near the Gulf of Kazül.

name: Pearly Mountains
blurb: The Pearly Mountains arc across SW Urok's midsection, older and more weathered than the Tethryns to the north.

name: Pebble Islands
blurb: A group of islands in Tarkhon.

name: Peecked Hills
blurb: Western hills between the coast and the desert interior.

name: Pelaendor
blurb: Nomadic Kendor who travel ocean currents and venture onto land; primary ambassadors between Kendor society and the surface world.

name: Pelaria
blurb: The volcanic kingdom.

name: Pelarian
blurb: Volcanic-vent Kendor with fire attunement who distinguish honorable combat from aggression; considered borderline heretics by northern Kendor.

name: Peleletra Hills
blurb: Hills in the Farlands.

name: Pelera
blurb: A dark emerald dragon who claims The Glog forest and surrounding territory as her domain.

name: Pelera
blurb: A dark emerald dragon who claims The Glog forest as her domain, laired at Shimmer Lake on the forest's eastern edge.

name: Pelmar Mountains
blurb: The Pelmar Mountains form the northeastern spur of the greater Kharvorn range, rising where the Celedrim Plains give way to the frozen approaches of the…

name: Pelwin
blurb: The second Foretold of Sestros, whose drought-breaking miracle turned Orwin's founding into an institution, and the king who came closest to the truth.

name: Pembling of Grendenheim
blurb: Halfling theologian of the Seventh Dawn; holds that the Dark Ages were a sacred season the living age has abandoned.

name: Penor Syalki
blurb: A forest in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Peoples of Alaria
blurb: The peoples who share the world, where their lineages came from, and the substance-deep lines that keep them apart.

name: Pesalolo
blurb: A thousand-mile island running northeast to southwest, split in half by geography and politics alike.

name: Petra
blurb: The capital city.

name: Petrona Septia Island
blurb: A major island in the Seacleft Coast containing the states of Ios and Moigos, as well as the city-state of Rilyn.

name: Pfustias
blurb: A swamp dragon of unusual temperament who dwells in the Ogzwad Swamp at the southeastern edge of Phirexes.

name: Pfustias
blurb: A dragon in Ogzwad Swamp.

name: Phaerhom
blurb: A hidden valley deep in the far southern Morygun Mountains, southeast of the Korgül forest region.

name: Phalkrene Mountains
blurb: The Phalkrene range runs north-south through the center of Urok's northwestern peninsula, separating Thrallwood to the west from Chitinwood to the east.

name: Phendexelas the Beautiful
blurb: Gorgon queen of Phirexes, who saved the Dengar from a doom of her own making. In sixty years she has never once left the swamp.

name: Phenol
blurb: Hills on Phenol.

name: Pherezi Imnuttut
blurb: The massive frozen eastern landmass of the Northern Isles—roughly a thousand miles of lawless, monster-haunted ice with no governing power.

name: Phiona Bay
blurb: The western coast of SW Urok curves inward to form Phiona Bay, a broad shallow anchorage sheltered by the Pearly Mountains' western extent.

name: Phiora
blurb: A state on Phii.

name: Phirexes
blurb: A fog-shrouded swampland at the southeastern edge of the Emerald Coast, Phirexes is a place of creeping dread disguised as salvation.

name: Phlethageros
blurb: Home to the Zylogmus Eternus and the Stalactites of Geth.

name: Pholiki
blurb: Easternmost city-state, closest to Erasnus.

name: Phorysia
blurb: The capital city of Hephake, a major dwarven mining settlement with massive silver extraction operations.

name: Phrelen
blurb: Domains: alchemy, volatile vapor, substance-into-substance and back; current cohort; Dengar patron of the flask that remembers what it held.

name: Phriorys
blurb: The northwestern edge of the Dygon Beastlands, where the jungle breaks against a landscape of rolling hills, scattered groves, and open grassland.

name: Phylos Bay
blurb: A large bay on the western coast of Petrona Septia Island, where the Dipindre River meets the sea and the city of Methons maintains its…

name: Phyndarr Sound
blurb: The major waterway connecting the northern Western Isles to the Iron Sea and mainland coast—a broad channel of relatively calm water between the Shattered Sea's…

name: Phyr Island
blurb: A jungle island in southeastern Ve, separated from the mainland by the treacherous Disaster Passage.

name: Piedritas
blurb: A town near the Sondoros forest, existing primarily to support the wine industry.

name: Piktiniti Desert
blurb: A vast arid expanse south of the Everwood, the Piktiniti is less a sea of sand dunes than a harsh shrubland—cracked earth, scrub vegetation, and…

name: Pindolin Islands
blurb: A sprawling archipelago stretching roughly 800 miles east-to-west and 500 miles north-to-south off the Dragon's Spine Coast, where the mountain range fragments into the sea…

name: Pinnacles of Rosensaw
blurb: A distinctive mountain range between the Gaplands and the Shasalassere Mountains, characterized by needle-sharp spires that rise from gentler slopes like teeth from a jaw.

name: Pino River
blurb: The Pino drains the southwestern Wilds, smaller and slower than the Byzkus.

name: Pity Knights
blurb: An order of lich-hunters who fought the dead with their own life-spending Deoric, until one of their own became the thing they hunted.

name: Pixie
blurb: Diminutive winged beings born where Melera's escaped music runs thick through the world; they hear the Faesong in all things and live by emotional resonance.

name: Pixies
blurb: Diminutive winged fae — Faesong condensed into flesh, not controllers of emotion but emotion itself. See the Pixie race entry for the full account.

name: Plains of Eregnar
blurb: Plains in Azawahasi.

name: Plains of Oblivion
blurb: Northwest of the mountains of Murth (a state in Tarkhon), stretching from the Western Isles coast in the west to the orc state of Hedroscobb…

name: Plains of Wycendeula
blurb: A vast grassland stretching across eastern Wycendeula, from the Kilbyurn Mountains in the west to the shores of God's Bathtub in the east.

name: Plainwatch
blurb: The frontier outpost, built on a ridge where the mountains give way to the plains.

name: Plathk
blurb: Ravenous, yellow-orange demons that occasionally show up from other planes.

name: Plenjorn Swamp
blurb: A marshy region within Tarn, fed by the Grand Tolkarsus—a massive river that stretches from the Nirvanis Mountains (part of the Kharvorn range) in the…

name: Plutachranes
blurb: A vast forest in the Celedrim Plains.

name: Plyrthasnyach
blurb: A forest within the Dragonsong region.

name: Poison Hills
blurb: The toxic hill country on Gorath's western threshold, where alchemists harvest venoms worth dying for and the settled empire gives way to the Moon Wilds.

name: Polar Unicorn
blurb: A giant, white shaggy beast with a single, large horn.

name: Ponoigari
blurb: Ponoigari is the capital of Kadroka and one of the most unusual cities in Aboyinzu.

name: Pool to Infinity
blurb: A lake in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Pools of Tragedy
blurb: A cluster of dark lakes in the northwestern Crimson Coast, fed by the River of Wights, where the still water tastes of grief.

name: Porcuni
blurb: A forest within Illron.

name: Port Echo
blurb: The largest settlement on the Beak, named for the acoustic phenomenon in its harbor.

name: Port Edott
blurb: A western harbor town, primary departure point for ships heading to the mainland.

name: Port Perith
blurb: The last "normal" port before the haunted zone—normal being relative in Mueras.

name: Postronamas Empire
blurb: An ancient civilization that rose to greatness through mastery of twyl crystals, built an empire spanning the western Westwilds, and collapsed into madness and civil…

name: Poum Titch
blurb: A Scalawag goblin nation in southeastern Terrenia, wedged between the Green Mountains to the north, Aal Salma to the west, and the coastal waters of…

name: Poum-Titch
blurb: Located in Terrenia, south of the Green Mountains and east of Aal Salma.

name: Predatak
blurb: A crater lake in the Celedrim Plains.

name: Prince-Consort Vhelan
blurb: Sculptor and reluctant regent of Gissemari, ruling for his child-queen daughter. On the Council he votes for whatever ends the meeting fastest.

name: Prince's Bay
blurb: The large southern bay where Enavadi's coast curves toward Gyosha's waters.

name: Prison Bay
blurb: A southern bay below Hillat Abu Hizam, guarded by the island fortress of Vadu Inat.

name: Prisoner Hills
blurb: Hills in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Promise of Mythras
blurb: A massive stone object, permanently trapping the Sennites within Ennros Lake.

name: Promisewood
blurb: The largest forest in Avalon, dominating the southern reaches of the kingdom where the land rises toward the Widebarrow Mountains.

name: Prophecy Island
blurb: An island in the Agreben Sea.

name: Providence
blurb: A forest within Thorum.

name: Prowling Hills
blurb: Hills within the Crystal Mountains.

name: Pseilemori
blurb: Pretty little winged creatures.

name: Psy Magic
blurb: Psy magic harnesses the Psywinds, the swirling mass of thought that every living being sheds across Alaria; it is the counterpart to Faesong.

name: Psywinds
blurb: The flow of thought and intention across Alaria; the thought-face of Ezz, inseparable from Faesong's emotion-face.

name: Ptequin Lakes
blurb: A cluster of mountain lakes in the northern Caffas, fed by snowmelt and underground springs.

name: Puerto Carmele
blurb: A coastal town south of Mataojo, smaller and quieter.

name: Pugrai D'had
blurb: The northernmost lake, where the Krelfassa river meets the coast.

name: Pugrai Naihim
blurb: The largest and most dangerous lake, fed by the Elmynfrosk river from the north.

name: Pugrai Shisk
blurb: The smallest lake, positioned between D'had and Naihim.

name: Pul
blurb: A smaller settlement higher up the mountain, home to monks in the middle stages of their training.

name: Puma Wood
blurb: The forest that contains and defines Rilyn, spreading across the northeastern slopes of the Screech Peaks and down toward the Fernius Sound coast.

name: Pyaganos
blurb: A green dragon who has ruled the Phriorys hills and northwestern Dygon Beastlands for over eight centuries—the longest continuously held dragon territory in Ve.

name: Pyceuri Tuni
blurb: A tundra region in the Frostmarch Peninsula.

name: Pyndar
blurb: A port town that handles legitimate trade—the non-slave commerce that Tykos needs to survive.

name: Pyoa Noi
blurb: Within the eastern Nysanna Range stands one of the most bizarre landmarks in the Northlands: Pyoa Noi, a hundred-mile-long stone serpent that twists through the…

name: Pyonokraus
blurb: A city-state built into the skull of a dead titan.

name: Pyrgion Forest
blurb: Western Xoth, between the Siddaneas River and the heartland, is dominated by Pyrgion Forest—dense woodland that serves as the buffer between the covenant and non-Xothic…

name: Pyris
blurb: A moderate-sized island southwest of the Tarkhon Strait, part of Nektuna's territory.

name: Pyrisi
blurb: Stocky, stubborn islanders of Pyris, sprung from one closed human-dwarven cross; farmers, fishers, and dry-stone masons who want only to be left alone.

name: Pystinwood
blurb: The northernmost of the three eastern forests, Pystinwood marks where the Westwilds' woodlands finally give way to the Steppe of Aziirn.

name: Pythalomos
blurb: Dragon of constrictor snakes.

name: Pythalomos
blurb: A dragon of Westrim.

name: Qavindal
blurb: Human specialist on the False God's reign; the only scholar to have reconstructed its administrative structure from material evidence.

name: Qepi Sandelyx
blurb: The Goshwen gnome jurist of Bestacia who drafted the Aldriktch charter, turning Corwen Hostling's bargain into terms five rival states could sign.

name: Qet Yemani
blurb: Qet Yemani is the largest of the five jungle nations, controlling the western reaches of the Jungles of Godahi.

name: Qhytheryx
blurb: Domains: echo and depth, the hidden voice, the answer the sinkhole returns; current cohort; Qipi patron of the call into the dark that is answered, sometimes by what was called.

name: Qindo
blurb: Blue-skinned, charming sailors with sapphire eyes who run on their own time and blur the line between merchant and pirate.

name: Qintas
blurb: A coastal state in Central Aboyinzu, home to a significant Amverela high elf population and famous across Alaria for Sondoros wine.

name: Qipi
blurb: Smallest gnome heritage, dwelling in Tyberoskos sinkhole cities; green-skinned with huge eyes, masters of acoustic communication.

name: Qlyp Augo
blurb: The ruins of the Gorathi provincial capital rise from the mangrove swamps of northeast Lethos—massive stone towers still standing after three centuries, their foundations drowned…

name: Qoponatai
blurb: Domains: health, strength; current cohort; deity whose dual domain bridges healer and warrior traditions.

name: Qord'ik
blurb: Tall, powerful half-giants of the Free Isles, descended from a settled giant line; feared traders and master sailors of the southern seas.

name: Qorgath
blurb: The Qorgath is a giant, creepy sea monster that births all the Erzqin that live in the Dragons' Teeth.

name: Qovryx
blurb: A gnome born with a Deoric command grown into its own flesh. Its stray power kills kin by accident; survivors bind themselves into lichdom.

name: Queen Eranes
blurb: Lives in Satyrn Gray, a palace in the Plains of Oblivion.

name: Queen Haegstra's Tower
blurb: A ruined fortress in the northwestern interior of Phyr Island, deep within the Jungle of Spines.

name: Queen Ishari
blurb: The seven-year-old Queen of Gissemari, who reigns in name while her father Vhelan rules as regent.

name: Queen Khalira
blurb: Queen of Wadiyah, thirty-four years old, the most dangerous voice on the Council of Four Flames—and the one most certain the waiting is over.

name: Queen of Love
blurb: The Queen of Love is a interdimensional demon disguised as a mystical, solitary queen.

name: Queen Seraphel
blurb: Queen of Yaif, nearly eighty and sixty-three years on the throne. The Council's voice of patience: outlast Tarkhon, do not fight it.

name: Quiet Mountain
blurb: See entry under Krenglelan Range.

name: Rabbit River
blurb: Tangiern's primary trade artery.

name: Rakite
blurb: The grass-sea northwest of the Kelder Mountains, home to the sign-speaking Rakiten, who follow the buffalo and hold every river sacred from source to sea.

name: Rakiten
blurb: Towering bronze-skinned plains elves who communicate exclusively through intricate sign language and organize society around visual sight-bonds.

name: Ramakemizi Mountains
blurb: The Ramakemizi Mountains form the eastern boundary of Kadroka, separating the dragon-ruled savanna state from the Dalizi Confederation and Chechol beyond.

name: Raptor Hills
blurb: The southeastern corner of the Krell Lands, where the dense jungle thins into savannah-like hills prowled by predatory dinosaurs large enough to threaten even Krell…

name: Raric Bay
blurb: A large crescent bay along the southern Innerrim coast where the fish are already dead when you catch them.

name: Rathscar
blurb: The capital city, seat of the High Camarik Council.

name: Ratlins
blurb: A disgusting cross between goblins and rats, these creatures multiply tremendously fast, aren't super bright, and plague cities.

name: Recnaf
blurb: On the southern shore of the eastern peninsula, Recnaf is Roule's quietest city—a sheltered harbor town more focused on fishing than politics.

name: Red Desert
blurb: The arid northern reaches of Sestros, where iron-rich sands glow crimson at sunset and nothing grows for months at a time.

name: Red Gold
blurb: Red gold is one of the most valuable materials known on Alaria.

name: Red Platinum
blurb: Red platinum, a red-cast metal far scarcer than red gold and pointedly non-magical, is struck into the highest coin, the Crimson Crown.

name: Red, the Screaming Blade
blurb: Undefeated champion of the Free Isles coliseums: a massive Chargon clad in armor of her own glowing blood, wielding a sword full of screams.

name: Redreed Lake
blurb: Redreed Lake is a terminal lake in southern Tythikerys, fed by the Dutunimako River and ringed by distinctive reddish reeds.

name: Reflection Lakes
blurb: Lakes in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Regina
blurb: The capital of Ethadia sprawls across the southern shore of Strymün's main harbor—a natural bay so perfect for shipping that the city's founding was essentially…

name: Renaro Tovellin
blurb: A junior Camaran magistrate whose legally airtight petition against the anti-magic ban sits, unanswered, on the First Consul's desk.

name: Renato
blurb: Prince of Sylke, the quiet middle sibling who secretly sustains the war between Tornia and Joswik to keep his small kingdom the island's indispensable broker.

name: Rentar
blurb: Spiritually devoted Minotaur of Chechol whose peaceful faith has been covertly corrupted by the lich Gynor the Conqueror as his future Red Army.

name: Renzo
blurb: A Slavewatch lieutenant who runs his patrols by the book and empties the passes of the people his Tribune has sold.

name: Resolution Bay
blurb: A bay within Turquish Bay.

name: Revenant
blurb: Once you were dead, but now—well, you still are, kind of.

name: Rhea
blurb: The Rhea possess cool, grayish-blue skin that seems to shimmer like morning mist.

name: Ribwood Forest
blurb: A forest on the southern Tyvern Islands, home to a rare and valuable tree species known as ribwood.

name: Riki River
blurb: The easternmost river, closest to the Dalizi border and the School of Summoning.

name: Rilyn
blurb: A city-state on Petrona Septia Island in the Seacleft Coast region.

name: Rimihuica
blurb: The middle continent of Alaria, falling from northern desert badlands through a vast jungle basin to the slave-trading Shacklands in the south.

name: Ripmaw Sound
blurb: The western waters between Iqes and the Upoceax mainland, Ripmaw Sound earned its name from the volcanic rock formations that lurk beneath its surface.

name: River of Blades
blurb: The easternmost river has earned its grim name honestly.

name: River of Wights
blurb: A Crimson Coast river where the violently dead rise as wights, with no necromancer to raise them.

name: River Yuforhi
blurb: The infamous River Yuforhi flows through the heart of the White Wastes, carrying waters that induce euphoria.

name: Rivoleta River
blurb: The Rivoleta River drains the eastern Chull Lands, flowing south through the Hills of Meus before joining the River of Petals in the northern Cerulean…

name: Roclin Hills

name: Rodton
blurb: Married to Loyera.

name: Rokunuri
blurb: The Rokunuri are a terrifying race of predators inhabiting the southern region of Aboyuinzu.

name: Romath
blurb: Domain: kingship, law, order; dead cohort (Golden Age); the divine authority behind the Golden Age's great kingdoms.

name: Rootborn
blurb: The Rootborn are not born.

name: Rothogomos

name: Roule Channel

name: Ruby Rock

name: Rudding

name: Ruin Giant

name: Runeglades
blurb: The coastal lowlands southeast of Seyiy, where the Elder Wilds meet the Slumbering Abyss.

name: Rurbanza
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Rusalka

name: Ruvu
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Ryerton & Bexley

name: Ryujilin Hills

name: Ryunimir
blurb: A forest in the Westwilds.

name: Safety Bay
blurb: A small inlet on Phyr Island's northern coast, sheltered by a curved arm of jungle-covered land.

name: Safeway Passage
blurb: The only reliable route through the Cowardly Mountains, connecting the western territories to the grasslands and forests of Central Aboyinzu.

name: Sainu
blurb: Domains: violence, pain, suffering; current cohort; dark deity sustained by the worst of what living beings do to each other.

name: Sakatia
blurb: A ruined village of Old Tolaria, swallowed by the Faewoods when the forest erupted from nothing.

name: Sal'emri Bay
blurb: The northeastern bay where the coast curves toward the Tarns, fed by seasonal rivers descending from the highlands.

name: Salenial
blurb: A ruined mining village in Eshbya Hills, within Deo Esari's territory.

name: Sallaróth
blurb: Domain: salt, preservation, food that outlasts the season; dead cohort (Craggus era); the god of the lesson the Long Winter had taught the survivors.

name: Sallow Hills
blurb: Hills within Chechol.

name: Salomor
blurb: The desert king of Kazül, whose rule rests on a summoning ring recovered from the Ederhi ruins that answers him and no one else.

name: Samandoken
blurb: The western port of Samandoken faces the Inside Passage and serves as Roule's administrative center for Nylos.

name: Samani
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: San Isidrio
blurb: A central city along the main road between Cañas and Qintas.

name: Sand Dragons
blurb: Sand dragons are not dragons, though they do have breath weapons like dragons, thus their names.

name: Sandreach Mountains

name: Sandskull
blurb: A ruined fortress or temple at the southern extent of the Kunagi Hills, where the ridges flatten into the desert plain.

name: Sandwurm Coast
blurb: The southeastern coastal region south of Geyser Bay.

name: Sarki Tulo
blurb: The only significant settlement in Kirthdron Soytas.

name: Saron
blurb: Deeper into Grimreach, Saron is the last settlement before the Darkened Coast.

name: Satyr

name: Satyr Lands
blurb: A region inhabited by satyrs in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Satyr Wood
blurb: Three hundred miles of dense woodland stretching from Falsehaven Bay to the Fyrsan Al Troikas mountains, and roughly seventy-five miles deep.

name: Savant

name: Saygheod the Forgotten
blurb: Saygheod was forgotten about by the rest of the world, so wanted to punish world.

name: Scalawag
blurb: Mischievous, communal goblins born into enormous broods; fiercely loyal to their lineage and devoted to vibrant culture and absurd pursuits.

name: Scar
blurb: A brownish dragon laired in the Myjornis Mountains.

blurb: A dramatic mountain range in the southeastern Sandreach Mountains, known for the distinctive red-orange coloration of its rock faces.

name: Scatter Sound
blurb: The body of water between the Eskiloko coast, the Tyvern Isles, and the Isle of the Night.

name: Schousterwood
blurb: The eastern forest, closer to the coast.

name: Scorches

name: Scream Strait
blurb: A strait near Pesalolo.

name: Screamers
blurb: Wailing women, lost on plains.

name: Screech Peaks

blurb: The water between the Tarkhon coast and the eastern Western Isles, where Middle Sea cargo crosses to the Shattered Sea and the tolls begin.

name: Sea of Spiders
blurb: A point of interest in the Sea of Spiders.

name: Seabreach Forest
blurb: Southern coastal forest, along the approach to the Telphineas Strait.

name: Seacleft Coast

name: Seatower Island

name: Seavault
blurb: A northern coastal town with a natural harbor protected by rocky headlands.

name: Seaview
blurb: A settlement on Tykos's southeastern edge, overlooking open water.

name: Seawatch Mountains

blurb: The elevated heart of Venalthier—a vast icy plateau rising 5,000-6,000 feet above sea level, forming the source region for the glacial flows that define the…

blurb: The current human king of Tarkhon, governing from Tarkhetan—the first dynasty that no longer remembers what it replaced.

name: Selvauria
blurb: Domains: wild-running, hooved pact with the plain, the herd as one breath; current cohort; Centaur patron of the moving herd.

name: Sendawane
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Sennos
blurb: A city-state in the Celedrim Plains.

name: Senwyn the Agonizer
blurb: Dragon of venomous snakes.

name: Seputoro
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Seron Lake
blurb: The middle of the three western lakes, smaller than Sethlund but deeper.

name: Sestros
blurb: The heartland of western Ve—a kingdom of shepherds and silk-weavers ruled by a monarch who speaks for a god.

name: Sethlund Lake

name: Sevich
blurb: The capital city, where the Chechik River widens into a marshy delta before joining the Deadloop drainage.

name: Sevrai

name: Seyiki

name: Seyiy
blurb: The central highlands of the Elder Wilds—a dramatic region of hills, waterfalls, and treacherous terrain.

name: Sha'ra
blurb: A port town specializing in overland caravan trade—the route across the Dustwind Plateau to the Taflex Forest and beyond passes through here.

name: Shacklands
blurb: The Shacklands are not a single contiguous territory but rather a collection of southern regions united by their participation in the slave trade.

name: Shad Usk
blurb: The largest and most treacherous dune formation in central Kura, Shad Usk rises nearly three hundred feet at its peak and stretches for miles in…

blurb: A freed shadow in the Malstaric kingdoms of the dead: a husk of borrowed dark that holds the dead's secrets, not the person who died.

name: Shadewood Forest
blurb: A forest in Tornia, Ofrenia.

name: Shadowkeep
blurb: A keep in northern Shyona drowned in permanent shadow, where a long-dead dark-shaper tore open a Vulus seam and never closed it.

blurb: When the Black Sun Nydus sinks toward Malstaris's surface, darkness floods the Material Plane and the dead rise with no hand to raise them.

name: Shailin
blurb: Yellow-eyed halflings of Sestros shaped by daemon prophecies from Talresses; they build for futures not yet arrived and age strangely.

name: Shak Shak
blurb: Domains: pyre, oath, fire-tattoo memory; current cohort; pan-goblin patron of the scar that keeps a promise.

name: Shalif Mulak
blurb: A village of perhaps two thousand souls, located half a day's travel from Bani Bari on the western side of the ley line.

name: Shambling Forest

name: Shambling Giants
blurb: Enormous, moss and tree covered giants.

name: Shamerei Bay

name: Shamwet
blurb: A tree-settlement in the northern part of Stipen, near the Chedwin Strait coast.

name: Shananae

name: Shananae
blurb: A dragon of the Northlands.

blurb: Silver-eyed humans capable of true physical transformation who can perfectly mimic any humanoid; their distinctive eyes never change form.

name: Shardspires
blurb: Mountain range in the Celedrim Plains.

blurb: The southern reaches of Konai Mora, where the frozen basin begins climbing toward the mountain ranges that ring the region.

name: Shargon Hills
blurb: Hills in the Dragon's Spine Coast.

name: Shark Bay
blurb: A broad, shallow bay on Westrim's southwestern coast, providing Atriik's only maritime access.

name: Sharvatunis Fortress
blurb: The eastern guardian of the Tarkhon Passage, watching the approaches from the Middle Sea.

name: Shasalassere Mountains
blurb: A sprawling mountain range south of The Screech and the Gaplands, forming the backbone of this section of the Dragon's Spine before the land breaks…

blurb: The easternmost orc state, occupying the borderlands where the Wildwood thins into the Steppe of Aziirn.

name: Shazuihni
blurb: Hairy, ferocious boar-riding orcs whose brutal simplicity and giant mounts make them terrifyingly dangerous in battle.

name: Sheîr
blurb: A sprawling island kingdom on the eastern edge of the Western Isles, where the Shattered Sea meets the Tarkhon coast.

name: Shelwin
blurb: Blue-eyed halflings of Iqes, cave-ancestored and now river-coastal, navigating foreign-dominated trade cities as dockworkers and Ripmaw Sound pilots.

name: Shemazari
blurb: An ancient civilization that flourished in the Elder Wilds during the early Third Eon before vanishing without historical record.

name: Shemu
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Shenchen Forest
blurb: At the southwestern extent of the Pearly Mountains, where the slopes descend toward Tyberoskos, lies Shenchen Forest—a place travelers learn to avoid.

name: Shepherds' Stones
blurb: A dramatic mountain range running east-west across northern Ve, forming the natural border between Edari's empty savannah and the settled lands of Sestros.

name: Sherly & Lieler
blurb: Small settlements deeper still, barely more than camps.

name: Shimmer Lake

name: Shimmercreek Falls
blurb: A town built around a dramatic waterfall where a river tumbles off the northern highlands.

name: Shin-keth
blurb: Shadow gnolls.

name: Shinii
blurb: A Kor city-state in eastern Atriik, positioned at the gateway to desert trade on the western edge of the Kunagi Hills.

name: Shinishini
blurb: Interior city-state known for its performers.

name: Shinori Island
blurb: The largest island in the northern Pesalolo archipelago, and the lair of Agtakkeri the Flayed Banner.

name: Shipmarsh
blurb: The largest city in Adron after Adrak, built where the Tenori River meets the sea.

name: Shipton
blurb: The capital—if a town of a few thousand can claim that title.

name: Shir
blurb: An island standing alone in the channel between the northern mainland and the Sunshine Coast, walled off by the Scream Strait and the Lightning Passage.

name: Shorolyn Forest
blurb: The eastern forest of Avalon, covering the highlands where the kingdom approaches Dern and the Widebarrow Mountains.

name: Shoryaven
blurb: Human chronicler of the Great Expansion; reads history as the ascent of modern peoples toward rightful dominion.

name: Shrapnel Strait
blurb: A violent gash of water separating the central Dragon's Spine from the Pale Peaks to the south.

name: Shrazvi
blurb: Domains: canopy-call, the carrying voice, the answered cry; current cohort; Frialve patron of the cry that reaches across the trees.

name: Shredwreck

name: Shrieking Peaks
blurb: A mountain range in Adron.

name: Shtaneres
blurb: The ruins of an ancient city in Breia, built at the intersection of fire and air leylines.

name: Shunde
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Shyaliviin
blurb: Hills just south of the Eronia range that poke into the northern edge of the Dunes of Kunagi.

name: Shyka

name: Shykasas

name: Shyoka Saseidi

name: Shyona
blurb: Ceremonious humans of Ve's northeastern plains who prize craft, family honor, and the careful ordering of daily life.

name: Shyona
blurb: A state in Ve.

name: Shyonn
blurb: A southwestern settlement near the border with Eoga.

name: Shystani Forest
blurb: A small forest east of Eidlesandres, notable for its sacred sites and ancient power.

name: Sighing Canyons
blurb: Ships stuck in walls, fossils in the sides, wyvern gouged walls

blurb: Trømgar's operative inside the slave country: a Strømgodden living under a metals-trader's cover in Tamadrez, moving Levke's gold into the Moon Road.

name: Silver Grove
blurb: The largest Albersea island, named for the metallic-leafed trees that grow nowhere else.

name: Silver Slopes
blurb: A mineral-rich stretch of the central Dragon's Spine where silver veins run so thick that exposed rock faces gleam in sunlight.

name: Silwyn
blurb: The grass sea.

name: Silzar
blurb: Nomadic plains ogres who master mounted warfare on war mammoths equipped with cannons and ballistae; their weapons are sacred heirlooms.

name: Simonsor
blurb: Major city in the north, near the Hephake mountain passes.

name: Simphiwe
blurb: The western city, gateway to Ntando.

name: Singa Chike
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Singing Trees
blurb: like syrens.

name: Sivakr
blurb: Gray-skinned underground elves of Myorna who duel through memory manipulation and measure social status entirely by intellectual prowess.

name: Sivirine
blurb: The second city of Ethadia occupies the archipelago's northwest coast, facing the open Gindrik rather than the sheltered waters of the main harbor.

name: Skaag

name: Skoldar
blurb: A quarry town on a rocky island whose stone has been extracted for generations.

name: Skree

blurb: Domains: scorpion cult, venom-as-virtue, the sting that proves the warrior; current cohort; Husakas patron of the initiation that does not heal cleanly.

name: Skyloft Castle

name: Skyreach
blurb: A fortress carved into a high mountain saddle, built in a style that predates the Sylthik city-states by centuries.

name: Skywatch
blurb: Skywatch is an ancient observatory perched near the summit of one of the tallest peaks in the Kharvorn Mountains, on the northern edge of the…

name: Slavewatch
blurb: A Gorathi fortress carved into the central Freedom Mountains, overlooking the Slaver's Coast and the shipping lanes that connect the slave ports to Divinity Passage.

name: Slavos
blurb: The capital and largest settlement, built around the primary slave market.

name: Sleep Sauce
blurb: Sleep meds.

name: Slerp
blurb: White, slimy, translucent pray mantis-like creatures that dwell in caves.

name: Slickrun
blurb: The westernmost river descends from the Miramar Peaks through a channel of metamorphic stone polished to an almost glassy smoothness by millennia of meltwater.

name: Slonari Bay
blurb: A bay within Meadow Sound.

name: Slumber Deep

name: Smokestack
blurb: The volcanic peak at New Chimea's southern border, shared with Vystrilik.

name: Smokestack Island
blurb: Active lavaflow.

name: Smokey Hills
blurb: A range of hills south of Poum Titch, east of Aal Salma, and north of Chaal Nazzerox that have ceaselessly billowed smoke and ash for…

name: Snagwing
blurb: Red, winged, bat-cats.

name: Snakefoot Hills
blurb: Hills within the Grendenheim region.

name: Snakemarsh
blurb: A vast wetland in the western Krell Lands, fed by streams descending from the Hardshell Mountains.

name: Snøvgar (Village)
blurb: Population: ~200

name: Sobrevire
blurb: A forest in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Sokorgül
blurb: A forest in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Solas
blurb: Domains: cunning, wit, humor; current cohort; lightly-worshipped deity whose following spans every culture that prizes cleverness.

name: Soldiers of the Third Eye
blurb: Time-attuned mercenaries who read a battle's outcome at stretched speed before it is fought, and pledge their unhurried blades to no crown.

name: Solek
blurb: Domain: hearth fire, warmth, home; dead cohort (Craggus era); the hearthfire deity of the post-Long-Winter world.

name: Solera
blurb: The capital, seat of the Soleri family.

name: Solhalven
blurb: A city-state north of the Blistering Mountains, home to the valkyries.

name: Solis
blurb: A marked location on the remote northeast peninsula, deep in jungle that grows differently than the rest of the island.

name: Solum Impervium
blurb: The order of high priests that governs Avalon by interpreting the immortal Ecclesiarch's slow pronouncements into policy, and guards every approach to his throne.

name: Sonagrev
blurb: Hills within the Dragonsong region, home to Dragon's Gate.

name: Sondoros
blurb: The main forest within Qintas, renowned for a strange grape-like fruit-producing vine that grows on the trees throughout the area.

name: Songsilk

name: Sookap
blurb: A fishing village where the Sovia widens into lazy pools.

name: Soothesong Sound
blurb: A cold northern sound off Soya Grei, named for the cliff-songs the Neth take for the voices of their dead.

name: Sopo Calisando River
blurb: A southern river draining the Bolsaña P'Suelos forest toward the coast.

name: Sopona Fia
blurb: A lake within Grustos.

name: Sopona Strempele
blurb: A smaller lake in the central region, near the town of Opela Sopona.

name: Sorgül Mountains
blurb: The southeastern arm of the Gül Mountains, curving down toward the broader Kharvorn range.

name: Sorix Melar

name: Soros
blurb: An island in the Western Isles.

name: Sorveth
blurb: Domain: sunlight, day, truth; dead cohort (Golden Age); sun deity killed when the volcanic winter blotted out the sky.

name: Soso Mari
blurb: The great tidal bay that defines Shykasas's southern coast, where the archipelago opens into deeper waters.

name: Sothills
blurb: Hills in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Souljack
blurb: A captive soul clamped into a suit of white, bone-clad armor by Deoric thread-work, kept from its rise and made to serve in silence.

name: South Reach

name: South Suki
blurb: A jungle within Chimea.

name: South Wall
blurb: A wall in the Thundering Mountains.

name: Sova Chyda
blurb: Istora's lifeline river, clear-watered and lined with settlements, which the kingdom reads as proof the dead favor its claim.

name: Sovia River

name: Soya
blurb: A small village in the agricultural interior, unremarkable except for its beans.

name: Specter
blurb: A soul or shadow stopped mid-transit through the Ethereal or Nethereal overlay, stranded there when necromancy severs its thread before death can finish.

name: Specters
blurb: A soul or shadow that necromancy stranded mid-transit in the overlay, left without the spirit that gave it will, and bound to serve its maker.

name: Spindral
blurb: Underground elves who fled dragon Ural; void-attuned with light-absorbing midnight quills, surfacing only to hunt and watch for their ancient enemy.

name: Spine Goblin
blurb: Goblins afflicted with a leprosy-like disease that drives bones through their skin; they weaponize the deformity by binding spikes to exposed bone.

name: Spinefir Forest
blurb: The central forest, vast and ancient.

name: Spire of Ascension
blurb: A pale, unweathering pinnacle in Sunshine Meadows whose apex opens a door out of the Alarian planar stack — for a price few survive paying.

name: Spirit Damage
blurb: A special kind of damage that ignores physical resistances and armor, but tears directly into a creature's spirit.

name: Spiritwood
blurb: A forest in the northeast where something has gone wrong with the natural order of death.

name: Splinkreen
blurb: The Splinkreen are the living legacy of a last druid's experiment, their souls drawn from mighty trees and seated in humanoid forms.

name: Splintercrests
blurb: Hills in Wycendeula.

name: Spotted Forest
blurb: Where the Dunes of Kunagi finally surrender to the savanna, the land doesn't transition cleanly.

name: Sprenwood
blurb: A coastal forest stretching along Shark Bay's eastern shore, providing Byonowidi and western Atriik with timber, game, and a buffer against the salt winds.

name: Sprites
blurb: Fae-born creatures of happiness and joy.

name: Srendar Mountains
blurb: A mountain range in Wycendeula.

name: Stalactites of Geth
blurb: Absolutely collosal stalactites, larger than mountains, hanging from Malstaris.

name: Stalking Fields
blurb: Open grasslands in the northwestern region, named for the tall grass that lets hunters—and warriors—stalk prey unseen.

name: Star Hag
blurb: Star Hags are ancient, malevolent beings born of starlight.

name: Starborn
blurb: Elves who dwell within the stars themselves, tending the celestial cores through nightly ritual and practicing cosmic neutrality to preserve universal harmony.

name: Starfall
blurb: At the center of Xabraedia lies Starfall—a pool of intense, otherworldly energy unlike anything else on Alaria.

name: Starfish Isles
blurb: A sprawling archipelago of low-lying islands, sandbars, and reef systems stretching north of Enavadi into open water.

name: Starluck Hills
blurb: Hills within Lenora.

name: Starreach
blurb: A tower that extends all the way from the ground to the Astral plane itself.

name: Stars, Suns, and Moons
blurb: Built by the titans to illuminate the darkness of the world.

name: States
blurb: Three nations control the Crimson Coast, each shaped by the region's bloody history:

name: Statue Hills
blurb: A ridge of hills running roughly north-south through Phyr Island's eastern interior, separating the Jungle of Spines from the relatively safer eastern shore.

name: Steadfast Mountains
blurb: Mountains within Thorum.

name: Steed Hills
blurb: A rocky highland in the west where the Gray Citadel stands—one of the last holdout fortresses against the undead empire.

name: Steppe of Aziirn
blurb: A massive area of grasslands, home to the nomadic people known as the [Jae-Kai].

name: Steppe of Aziirn
blurb: A vast expanse of dry grassland stretching east from the three eastern forests (Pystinwood, Milrar, and Telwood) toward the distant Kharvorn Mountains and the territories…

name: Stepsidae Peninsula
blurb: Southeastern Xoth extends into the Stepsidae Peninsula—drier, rockier, more isolated than the heartland.

name: Stillstone
blurb: A dead bond-forge of the Wyrmward, where Nagashi riders were once joined to their war-dragons.

name: Stillwater
blurb: Within Ámas Kiori, north of Adrak in the central basin.

name: Stipen
blurb: An Ezuri (Tree Gnome) gerontocracy in the forests along the Chedwin Strait.

name: Stone Men
blurb: Gray-skinned humans of the Wurn Mountains whose skin hardened in a single Earth-leyline surge; stoic, pragmatic, and fiercely independent.

name: Stone Titans
blurb: Earth-plane substance given coherent form: living stone that walks the deep seams once a century and leaves nothing when destroyed.

name: Stonebreach
blurb: A coastal fortress town on Tykos's eastern edge, positioned to watch the Tykon Channel and the ongoing chaos in Ofrenia.

name: Stonedrift
blurb: A navigational hazard and curiosity: a field of massive floating stones that drift through the central Alrock on unpredictable paths.

name: Stonetop Mines
blurb: A ruined mine on the Egül frontier, once worked jointly by Camaran and the Güli giants until the border tension closed it.

name: Stormfather
blurb: In the Blueshale Peaks dwells Stormfather, an earth-attuned cyclops of tremendous power.

name: Strait of Curses
blurb: The waters south of Xabraedia and north of Hagswood are known as the Strait of Curses, named for the hags who prowl these treacherous waters.

name: Straker
blurb: Tidal dragon.

name: Straker
blurb: A dragon on Glyn'Qoth.

name: Strangle River
blurb: The Strangle River drains the northern Foggy Mountains through the Misty Valley before emptying into the sea along the Shipwreck Coast.

name: Stratlan Sea
blurb: The deep, empty waters west of the Western Isles—the edge of the known world.

name: Stravko
blurb: Domains: scholar-tattoo, living ink, the unmarred archivist; current cohort; Stravlar patron of the legible page.

name: Stravlar
blurb: Fire-attuned goblin scholars whose embossed tattoos record family history in living ink; skin scarring is considered a tragedy greater than death.

name: Strolzaq
blurb: Strolzaq is the pet dragon of Bzulakar.

name: Strømgar
blurb: The dwarf who led the survivors of Argysis east into Hephake, and shaped the survivor-state they became.

name: Strømgodden
blurb: Battle-devoted dwarves of Trømgar whose spiked armor turns their bodies into weapons; glorious combat is a sacred offering and rite of passage.

name: Struvdik
blurb: Goblin oral historian; preserved the Stravlar fire-clan's living-ink genealogy tradition in written form before its extinction.

name: Strymün Island
blurb: The Strymün Isles rise from the Gindrik Sea like green jewels set in blue-gray water—a cluster of volcanic islands whose peaks remain visible for miles…

name: Stulov River
blurb: The central river, smaller than the Chechik but supporting the most productive farmland.

name: Suftos Hills
blurb: The contested spine between Klevnaf and Istora: a depopulated no-man's-land that belongs to neither kingdom and is claimed by both.

name: Suki Hills
blurb: A range of forested highlands marking the western boundary of the Krell Lands, the Suki Hills serve as the last defensible terrain between the insectoid…

name: Suki Jungle
blurb: The Suki Jungle totally encompasses Chimea.

name: Sulvath
blurb: Domain: love, beauty, desire; dead cohort (Golden Age); goddess of the arts and intimate life of the Golden Age.

name: Sulvyn
blurb: Sulus-air briefly gathered into a coherent, restless shape high in the open sky, holding form a few breaths before scattering back into the wind.

name: Sumda
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Sun-Forsaken North
blurb: The permanently frozen far north, beyond any prayer-coalition's reach, where ruined sunlit-era cities stand beneath glaciers and relict wildlife outlived the warmth that bred them.

name: Sun-Forsaken South
blurb: The permanently frozen far south, beyond Bryn's warmth-band, where the ruins of sunlit civilizations and stranded wildlife mark the end of a coalition that could not hold.

name: Sunbeam River
blurb: The central river flows down from the Silver Slopes, and its name requires no explanation to anyone who sees it at midday.

name: Sunglint
blurb: A forest in the Frostmarch Peninsula.

name: Sunigama
blurb: A forest within Shyona.

name: Sunrise Mountains
blurb: Mountains within Shyona.

name: Sunshatter Palace
blurb: A structure of impossible geometry hovering above the waves, home to a being called the Seahorse King—supposedly a god from another plane of existence.

name: Sunshine Coast
blurb: The northeastern coastal stretch where the Luquihn Desert meets the sea.

name: Sunshine Meadows
blurb: Cursed meadowy hills.

name: Sunshine Meadows
blurb: Cursed meadowy hills on the southern islands of the Free Isles, where the sun burns brighter than it should and the creatures have learned to…

name: Sunwing Peaks
blurb: Named after the beautiful, golden, luminant eagles that soar among the mountains.

name: Surkelm Coast
blurb: The eastern shore of Fernius Sound belongs to the Surkelm region — forested coastline stretching northeast from Petrona Septia toward the mainland.

name: Surrey Mahaila
blurb: The Dwelyn queen's ancient, emerald dragon—protector of Enapay since before written Dwelyn history.

name: Surrey Mahaila
blurb: A dragon of Westrim.

name: Surry
blurb: Translucent-skinned deep-cave dwarves whose sealed clan was remade in a single sinking into total dark; pragmatic cannibals who view all flesh as sustenance in their resource-scarce fungal kingdom.

name: Survivor's Island
blurb: At the center of the Sea of Sharks lies Survivor's Island—home to the city-state of Teku.

name: Svedlind (Capital)
blurb: Population: ~2,500

name: Svorinthak
blurb: Svorinthak is an ancient dragon who claims the Lyagnadarr Mountains as her territory—a domain that effectively separates the Luquihn Desert from the centaur plains of…

name: Swamp Heart
blurb: A condition spread by swamp mosquitos.

name: Swampdeep
blurb: A saltwater swamp and bayou region that technically separates Ofrenia from the Rimihuican mainland.

name: Sweet Water River
blurb: Phirexes's only freshwater river; it runs too fast for the fog to bind, which makes its banks the refugees' lifeline.

name: Sweetwoods
blurb: The northernmost forest in Phirexes, a relatively mundane woodland of sweetgum and cypress that provides most of Maurevelious's timber.

name: Sweivyon
blurb: A city-state of approximately twelve thousand occupying a defensible shelf between two peaks in the Silver Slopes.

name: Swindle Sound
blurb: Southeast of the Nekanzi Jungle, where the coastline curves inward, lies the body of water that foreign sailors call Swindle Sound.

name: Swinehead Forest
blurb: At the southern edge of the Sorgül, where the mountains descend toward the Breidleheis basin, lies Swinehead Forest—a dense, ancient woodland named for the enormous…

name: Swordsmen
blurb: The Swordsmen are a notorious seafaring people whose name strikes fear into merchant vessels across the known seas.

name: Swuigrach
blurb: The Swuigrach are large, imposing humanoids with the heads of pigs—pink, gray, or black depending on their lineage.

name: Sy
blurb: The eastern city-state, smallest of the four, perched between the Eskagon Hills and the Traevluong werewolf territory.

name: Sygozoki
blurb: "Burning Without End"

name: Sygum River
blurb: Southeastern river connecting Kessus Lake to the coast.

name: Sylke
blurb: Sylke is the center-west kingdom of Ofrenia, wedged between Tornia in the north and Joswik in the south.

name: Sylthik
blurb: The scar-ranked ruling caste of Kyagos's four city-states, grown wealthy on slave-worked mines and now reeling from the fall of Syvlius.

name: Sylthik
blurb: A serpentine people bred by Sythikranos, a false dragon who named herself a snake-god; their venom and gifts fail the moment communion with her breaks.

name: Symonair
blurb: Symonair is a blue dragon who has claimed the lake that bears his name for longer than any orc state has existed.

name: Symonair Lake
blurb: A deep, cold lake in the Wildwood's lake system, connected to Cykravikus Lake to the north.

name: Synger Ship
blurb: Flying galley ships powered by Syngers.

name: Syngers
blurb: The Syngers are perhaps the most mysterious race on Alaria, immortal beings who sail through the skies in ships that defy comprehension.

name: Syvlius
blurb: Status: Destroyed

name: Ta Akikis
blurb: Burial grounds for great chiefs of Ta Minn.

name: Ta Minn
blurb: An Oznak horse theocracy on the western Middle Sea coast—vast grassy steppes ruled by the Kazigh, the chief horse shaman.

name: Tacuarembo
blurb: A city in the agricultural heartland, surrounded by the farms that feed Grustos.

name: Tadinu
blurb: The administrative co-capital of Poum Titch, an inland city of limestone warrens and bureaucratic complexity.

name: Tafframel Hills
blurb: Hills on Phenol.

name: Tafleur Wood
blurb: A forest on Phenol.

name: Taflex Forest
blurb: The great forest of eastern Mueras, stretching from the windswept edge of the Dustwind Plateau down to the fog-shrouded approaches of Grimreach.

name: Talathere
blurb: Appear as normal humans, descendants of dragons, and possess unique ability to completely dominate a dragon.

name: Talressses
blurb: Domains: manipulation, deception, false prophecy; current cohort; self-styled oracle of the Shailin halflings.

name: Tamadrez
blurb: The nominally independent north of Pesalolo: a scatter of creole harbor villages that buy their freedom from Gorath in coin, in silence, and in bodies.

name: Tamari
blurb: A girl who received a null Declaration, the verdict no Avalon child has survived to adulthood; a priest forged her a future and she lives hidden.

name: Tamboxal
blurb: A creole broker out of Nadang who keeps the bribe economy running and makes a practice of never asking where the cargo goes.

name: Tambrielle Mountains
blurb: The Tambrielle Mountains form the spine of Vystrilik, rising 5,000-7,000 feet where the jungle meets the highland.

name: Tangiern
blurb: A human kingdom of the Void coast, jarl-ruled and forest-backed, whose people give their dead to the open ocean and never to the ground.

name: Tanim Oscurus
blurb: The western reaches of the Ngül overlook a region the giants call Tanim Oscurus—the Dark Grazing.

name: Taoinor
blurb: Aging prince of Klevnaf and uncle of the murdered Istor XXVI; blamed for a regicide he did not commit, guilty of the half-kingdom he seized once it was done.

name: Tareki
blurb: Domains: tactical mind, the duty to protect, the general's burden; current cohort; Lorean patron of those who hold the line for others.

name: Tarkha
blurb: The capital of Kerwin—an ancient city that predates Tarkhon itself.

name: Tarkhetan
blurb: Imperial capital on the Needle—seat of Selron II, home of the Evertorch cathedral, and the prize the Kingdoms of Fire have not stopped wanting back.

name: Tarkhon Empire
blurb: The Tarkhon Empire straddles the critical strait between Clueanda and Rimihuica, controlling territory on both continents and dominating the only sea passage between the Middle…

name: Tarn
blurb: An orc state in the north-central Wildwood, controlling a strategic stretch of the Grand Tolkarsus river where it passes through the Plenjorn Swamp.

name: Tarnak
blurb: Nomadic desert shamans of Ulkevolm who practice blood magic, commune with the spirits of the dead, and ride great desert tigers.

name: Tarni
blurb: Orcs who fortify their very souls against destruction and wield spirit weapons that attack enemies on a metaphysical plane.

name: Tarolin (Capital)
blurb: The seat of the Grand Imperial Ecclesiarch sits where the Honor River bends through the heart of Avalon.

name: Tasah Evikktus River
blurb: A southwestern river notable primarily for what it passes: the ruins of Skyloft Castle.

name: Tasqh Evikkris
blurb: A broad, slow-moving river marking the western boundary of Echem Yiakraxes, the Tasqh Evikkris is the only safe passage through the region—and "safe" is relative.

name: Taufig
blurb: Southern town, near the border with Kazül.

name: Taulo Magi
blurb: A state in Dalizi.

name: Teardrop Lakes
blurb: A cluster of small lakes in eastern Eyendra, marking the northern edge of the Kama Sa'malina lake district.

name: Techgnomes
blurb: Near-extinct technological gnomes whose civilization was destroyed by their own creation; surviving relics of a lost mechanical age.

name: Teflin
blurb: Reddish-skinned elves of Hik cursed by Deoric magic granting agelessness but burning them with accumulated power requiring ritual bloodletting.

name: Tekos
blurb: The southern city-state, built on the shores of Lake Teklan where the mountains give way to Mythlias jungle.

name: Teku
blurb: A city-state on Survivor's Island, in the heart of the Sea of Sharks.

name: Tel Askati
blurb: A settlement in southwestern Qet Yemani, near the Kos Askati river and the approaches to the Myjornis Mountains.

name: Telara
blurb: A secondary port on the eastern coast, smaller and rougher than Shipton.

name: Teldib
blurb: The great port on the Bay of Roule, Teldib is the commercial and naval heart of southern Roule.

name: Teleriwood
blurb: The western forest, bordering Thespia.

name: Telphineas Strait
blurb: The one deepwater channel between the halves of the Middle Sea, claimed by three states and commanded by none.

name: Telwood
blurb: The southernmost of the three eastern forests, Telwood lies between Milrar Forest to the north and the Grieb Lakes region to the southwest.

name: Temavori of Drachma
blurb: Human cosmogonist of the Age of Craggus; mourns the Golden Age pantheon as an unrecoverable loss.

name: Temple of Bryn
blurb: Ruins of a Bryn sun-temple in the foothills north of Belu Jenari, scoured when the volcano formed but its light leyline still dangerously live.

name: Temple of Icefire
blurb: North of Gymelstik (a goblin-dominated state) and south of the Aquendrakons mountains, at the convergence of water and fire leylines, stands the Temple of Icefire.

name: Temple of the Sun
blurb: An ancient temple complex in the central Luquihn Desert, south of the Dunes of Kalamesh.

name: Temple of the Wind
blurb: High in the Mountains Semaphois stands the wind-racked, abandoned Temple of the Wind.

name: Temple of Zicadia
blurb: At the northern tip of the Wurmling Mountains, where force and air leylines intersect, stands the Temple of Zicadia.

name: Temptari
blurb: Mountain-dwelling spiritual humans who read the subtle currents of time and weather; their timemasters are sought as advisors by rulers across Alaria.

name: Tenches
blurb: A city-state at the heart of Mikisapi Forest, built around an ancient grove that the trees have never reclaimed.

name: Tenekee Woods
blurb: The transitional forest between the Krenglelan foothills and the Walking Forest proper, the Tenekee Woods are what hunters call "honest woods"—dangerous, yes, but predictable.

name: Tenori River
blurb: The economic spine of Adron.

name: Tepheranos
blurb: Tepheranos is a black dragon, and brother of Rothogomos.

name: Tephrakus
blurb: The capital city, built at the base of the Black Hills near Tepheranos's lair.

name: Ternor
blurb: A point of interest in the desert.

name: Terrenia
blurb: Terrenia occupies the northwestern reaches of Aboyinzu, a region of temperate states stretching from the coast down to the Crystal Mountains.

name: Terrogones
blurb: Massive apex predators of Echem Yiakraxis who use crude tools and sophisticated hunting strategies; their jaws can crush armor and bone.

name: Terror Grove
blurb: Something went wrong in Terror Grove.

name: Terü
blurb: A forest in the Westwilds.

name: Terwin Lake
blurb: A lake in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Tethryn Mountains
blurb: The Tethryn range rises in northwestern SW Urok, younger and sharper than the worn Pearly Mountains to the south.

name: Tezina
blurb: The city closest to Murderwood, Tezina exists in the forest's shadow—sometimes literally, when the afternoon sun falls behind the treeline.

name: Thaes
blurb: Iqes god of the wind, sea, sailing, and oceans.

name: Thaleth
blurb: Domain: ocean, tides, storms; dead cohort (Golden Age); the sea goddess of the great maritime civilizations.

name: Thane
blurb: A town in central Camaran, between Galore and the capital Rathscar.

name: The Age of Craggus
blurb: 45,000–~33,400 years ago. Craggus rises as the first daemon of the reborn world, pushes back the Long Winter ice, and reigns as god of all men.

name: The Age of Titans
blurb: 23.5–12M years ago. Titan-gods Nydus, Aurus, and Celest shape Alaria; Lyzaria defines mortality; the Ezz Rift ends the age.

name: The Aktarvkus Mountains
blurb: The spine of Kyagos—a brutal mountain range that defeats casual crossing.

name: The Aldriktch Founding
blurb: Around 3200 SD five weak Middle Sea trade states signed a charter capping tariffs and pooling convoy escort, founding the Aldriktch Trade Alliance.

name: The Anchor-Vessel Priesthood
blurb: The specialist Golden-Age orders that performed the anchor-vessel rite for every other cult; the keystone class whose collapse doomed the pantheon.

name: The Apelands
blurb: South of L'Coth D'hari, across Farthing Inlet, lies a stretch of rainforest dominated by its most dangerous inhabitants: the great apes.

name: The Aphenlein Forests
blurb: West of Seyiy, descending toward Thrumbling Bay, the terrain flattens dramatically.

name: The Ash-Bearer
blurb: Domains: war-witness, survival of the conquered; rising cohort; cult of those who keep the names of the Chimean invasion's dead.

name: The Ashy Mountains
blurb: The Ashy Mountains are a range of active volcanoes running across central Aknimchuk.

name: The Atlas of Alaria
blurb: The shape of the inhabited world: its continents and seas, where the warm band holds civilization, and where the wild begins.

name: The Basin
blurb: The great basin that forms Anarak's territory—grassland and farmland stretching between the mountains.

name: The Bay of Whispers
blurb: The supernatural bay outside Hessam, where the wind carries secrets across time.

name: The Bejeweled Palace
blurb: The cursed palace of an ancient Mkinti king, abandoned for centuries.

name: The Bell-Witness
blurb: Domains: chivalric courage, mount-bond, the rite at first bonding; current cohort; Foxborne patron of the moment the fox chooses the knight.

name: The Bestiary
blurb: The beasts of Alaria, from common fauna to the monsters that haunt its wild places.

name: The Birintine River
blurb: The Birintine River rises in the central Pinnacles, fed by snowmelt and the numerous springs that emerge from the granite formations.

name: The Blades
blurb: An island chain off Adron's southwest coast whose strait is the hunting ground of the wild dragon Chavux.

name: The Bleached Garden
blurb: At the southern edge of the hills, where the terrain transitions to desert, lies an area called the Bleached Garden.

name: The Blerian Hills
blurb: Rising from the central tundra, the Blerian Hills provide the only significant elevation change between the Eceraen range and the Whitewall.

name: The Bone Fields
blurb: Where the hills meet the coastal flats, the remains of countless creatures lie scattered across the landscape.

name: The Breathing Pools
blurb: A cluster of hot springs in eastern Blyss, where geothermal vents heat the water to near-boiling.

name: The Burned Valley
blurb: A long depression between two hill ridges, roughly ten miles long and one mile wide, where nothing grows.

name: The Buzagi Wempu
blurb: More a mountain torrent than a proper river, the Buzagi Wempu crashes down from the northern Kilbyurns before calming into the Shizander.

name: The Bylzar Overreach
blurb: Around 3050 SD, Talressses tried to poach Bylzar's continuity-cult, lost his allies and most of his worshippers, and fell to the edge of oblivion.

name: The Cairns
blurb: Hills in Wycendeula.

name: The Camaran Blight
blurb: Around 3340 SD a Deoric working in Camaran drew its price from the living around it, killing hundreds and founding the republic's anti-magic statutes.

name: The Carried Fire
blurb: Around 2776 SD, Yaif's priests carried the Flame of First Fire north to light the Evertorch. The fire was lent, not given.

name: The Central Hills
blurb: Unnamed hills rise in Azawahasi's central region, breaking up the flat expanse of the surrounding grassland.

name: The Chained Caverns
blurb: The pre-human caverns beneath Mpehi where the dragon Bathemiel lies in self-forged chains, tended by the Gattorini.

name: The Chimean Approach
blurb: The northern edge of Phriorys, where the hills meet the Suki Jungle of Chimea.

name: The Citadel
blurb: At the heart of the Carcass Hills stands the ruin of an ancient city-state, known simply as The Citadel.

name: The Coinspine Mountains
blurb: The mountain range along Watar's western border.

name: The Cold That Hungers
blurb: The Cold That Hungers is the name the Fengruk give to whatever killed the titan Vorukar during the Second Eon.

name: The Concordance
blurb: The scholar-order of Mu that reconciles the Shailin prophecy-journals, whose inner circle has deduced their oracle-god is a finite fraud and chosen silence.

name: The Cosmos
blurb: A planar stack is a collection of tightly grouped planes, usually holding strong connections to each other.

name: The Crags of Geth
blurb: Mountain range along the western border, separating Erasnus from Griselia and the goblin city-states beyond.

name: The Croaking Briars
blurb: The living thorn-barrier separating Trømgar from Phirexes deserves special mention.

name: The Cyatri
blurb: A smaller tributary in the eastern forest, flowing south from the Kilbyurn foothills.

name: The Dark Ages
blurb: 0–231 SD. The Titansworn — Deoric cultists seeking to reawaken Hykravones — dominate the opening centuries of the Seventh Dawn.

name: The Darkened Coast
blurb: East of the Taflex Forest and south of Grimreach, along the northwest coast of the Agreben Sea, lies the Darkened Coast—a stretch of shoreline perpetually…

name: The Davred
blurb: A forest river in the northern portion of Fylvrae Sylvrym, flowing generally south before joining the Twilight River system.

name: The Dawnless
blurb: A heretical sect that holds Bryn's ceaseless motion to be a suffering mortals have no right to impose; works quietly to let the sun rest.

name: The Dead Moon Tribe
blurb: The displaced human people of Kyagos, waging a generations-long guerrilla war from Moonwood under the patronage of Nyxara, the dark moon.

name: The Diagonal Trail
blurb: The 180-mile trail from Hassera to E'lethelas crosses the forest's heart.

name: The Dolphin Islands
blurb: Southwest of Sheîr proper, the Dolphin Islands are sacred to the triton.

name: The Dragon Betrayal
blurb: Rothogomos and Tepheranos's joint conquest of dwarven Argysis, ending in Rothogomos's betrayal at the moment of victory and the dwarven exodus into Hephake.

name: The Dragon Shadow
blurb: Northwest of Thespia lies Morgnor's Cradle—the mountain territory of Morgnor, King of the Mountains.

name: The Drevhalds
blurb: A Tangier forest-edge family that has secretly studied the ruin of Hildaneir for three generations, keeping a private record no one knows exists.

name: The Drift
blurb: The most treacherous terrain in the Jungles of Godahi.

name: The Drowned Ruins
blurb: The lake has risen over centuries, swallowing structures that once stood on dry ground.

name: The Dying One
blurb: The entity at the heart of the Agony Stones has no name that anyone living knows.

name: The Early Times
blurb: The first eon. Azus and Melera shape the cosmos; the Cyclone of Possibilities and The Contraction precede Alaria's formation.

name: The Elmynfrosk
blurb: A southeastern drainage from the Caffas range, the Elmynfrosk flows through the eastern White Wastes before reaching Pugrai Naihim.

name: The Face
blurb: A massive cliff formation rising between West Whitewall and East Whitewall, forming the central portion of Venalthier's northern ice barrier.

name: The Fallen
blurb: The Fallen is an order comprised of a single champion.

name: The Firemage Corps
blurb: The Tarkhon Empire's elite order of fire-shapers, bound to the Yolus fire-seam beneath Tarkhetan, an edge it cannot carry to any other city.

name: The Firespine
blurb: A jagged fissure tears through the Nekanzi Jungle from north to south, following the path of the Yolus fire leyline where it runs close to…

name: The Fleimrut Awakening
blurb: 2500 SD. The Yolus seam beneath the Fleimrut Mountains tore open and never closed, and fire-plane elementals have poured from the vents ever since.

name: The Floss Islands
blurb: A scattered chain of small, rocky islands that form the northern boundary of Edko Sound.

name: The Forbidden Valley
blurb: A massive, impossibly deep valley north of Vad Ilghak.

name: The Forgotten Sea
blurb: The waters surrounding Pii are called the Forgotten Sea, though no one quite remembers why.

name: The Four Dragon Blades
blurb: Each of the four dragon blades is an ancient item that was forged by a dark elf master smith.

name: The Freedom Mountains
blurb: The central mountain range bisecting Pesalolo, running the full length of the island.

name: The Fungal Gardens
blurb: An area in western Blyss where bioluminescent fungi have colonized several square miles of forest floor.

name: The Gaeaic Eon
blurb: The second eon. 12M–~13,500 years ago. Ezz floods Alaria; Celestia blooms; Gaea is born; life and the druids reshape the world.

name: The Gathering Stones
blurb: A formation of standing stones on a prominent hilltop in eastern Phriorys, their origin unknown.

name: The Ghoul Forge
blurb: A Deoric mage of the Lost Ages staffed his forest estate with reanimated servants. Abandoned at his death, they bred into Bestacia's ghoul infestation.

name: The Glassing
blurb: The fall of the Ederhi, Kazül's glass civilization, when the Blight of Arcanus turned their own magic lethal and left the desert in their place.

name: The Glog
blurb: A vast expanse of dense, waterlogged forest sprawling across the northern Dragon's Spine coast between the open sea and the mountains.

name: The Gnomestone Mountains
blurb: The mountain range that defines Joswik's southern border, named for the gnome communities that supposedly dwelt here before humans arrived.

name: The God War & Long Winter
blurb: ~75,000–45,000 years ago. Daemon survival wars shatter the world; a follower of Dyos erupts Kajiit; ice age extinguishes the old pantheon.

name: The Golden Age of Man
blurb: 200,000–75,000 years ago. Humanity covers Alaria; technology peaks; daemons rise; the first great civilization ends in the God War.

name: The Golden Enchanting Table
blurb: A titan-wrought engine of Deoric rune-combination — a room of ever-turning golden gears that present working Deoric inscriptions to whoever stands at its center.

name: The Goldwatch
blurb: Mercenary companies of Keshwindi's Nameless Ones, who hire out the one inheritance exile left them: the blade.

name: The Grain Compact
blurb: The treaty-under-duress struck around 2876 SD that bound Enymu to feed Tarkhon at prices the empire sets, a compact still in force.

name: The Great Expansion
blurb: 9,950–0 BSD. Survivors rebuild after the Shattering; dragons return; the Frost Fall and the World Fire close out the pre-SD age.

name: The Greenway
blurb: The main road connecting Enymu to Tarkhetan and the rest of Tarkhon.

name: The Grey Mountains
blurb: The sub-range of the Kharvorn where Myorna is situated.

name: The Grinding
blurb: The Krell advance north through the old kingdoms of southeastern Ve, and the fall of Velkoron around 3150 SD that put the jungle directly on Shyona's southern border.

name: The Guiles
blurb: The Guiles is a dense forest wedged between the Hellion Hills to the west and the Twaan Forests to the north, stretching south toward Kobuk.

name: The Guiltless
blurb: Magically created humans incapable of deception or harm; exploited across millennia yet resilient through subtle methods of resistance.

name: The Heartwood Grove
blurb: At Tenches' center stands a ring of seven trees so massive they dwarf everything around them.

name: The Hide-Lender
blurb: Domains: beast-soul binding, animistic communion, the soul that wears a body it does not own; current cohort; Kuru patron of the body the shaman borrows.

name: The Highlands
blurb: Between the river valleys, the highlands rise in gentle swells of savanna grass.

name: The Hill Roads
blurb: A network of trails winds through Belanora, connecting the scattered settlements and linking the coast to the interior.

name: The Hills of the Damned
blurb: Southwest of Awobiso, visible from the highest terraces, the Hills of the Damned rise from the sea—a barren promontory where nothing grows and the soil…

name: The Honor River System
blurb: The Honor River rises in the eastern highlands and flows westward through Avalon's heart, eventually emptying into Munari Inlet.

name: The Ilyu
blurb: A western tributary system flowing from the lower Kilbyurn range.

name: The Impact Craters
blurb: Three massive craters punctuate the Dreamplains, remnants of an ancient cataclysm when objects fell from the sky in the distant past.

name: The Iri Mountains
blurb: The two massive peaks that make up almost all of Echea's territory.

name: The Iron of the Eternal March
blurb: Gorath's war-faith, which took the dwarves' forge-daemon Krondeum as its patron of conquest and now blesses the legions that enslave his people.

name: The Iron Orc
blurb: The Iron Orc is a legendary figure, particularly among the black orc tribes who take pride in their naturally tough skin.

name: The Ivory Hand
blurb: A hidden cabal that keeps the slave-sacrifice trade moving worldwide, because a life taken in blood yields Deoric power that years of prayer cannot match.

name: The Kendrel Kingdoms
