blurb: Three Kendrel states occupy the eastern Agreben, collectively known as the Kendrel Kingdoms despite their distinct governments and occasional rivalry:

name: The Kerwin Accession
blurb: Around 2876 SD the halfling king Ulyas negotiated Kerwin's entry into the young Tarkhon empire, trading modest tribute for near-total autonomy.

name: The Knives
blurb: A mountain range in the Kharvorn Mountains region.

name: The Knulak Lakes
blurb: The Knulak Lakes are a cluster of interconnected bodies of water fed by glacial melt from the Qynxis Mountains to the south.

name: The Krelfassa
blurb: Flowing east from the Caffas Mountains toward the coast, the Krelfassa passes through the northern White Wastes before emptying into Pugrai D'had.

name: The Kro Shiik Convocation
blurb: The annual gathering of every Rakiten tribe at Kro Shiik—the one pan-Rakiten institution, deciding by visible consensus, and paralyzed over Roule.

name: The Labyrinth
blurb: The folded-time structure beneath the Evertorch cathedral, where the Izzus leyline surfaces—and the reason the flame above never varies.

name: The Ladder-Blesser
blurb: Domains: coin-and-contract, noble-merchant tradition, the ledger that doubles as a charter; current cohort; Belenstrope household patron of the deal that lifts a house.

name: The Last Lamp
blurb: The Aureum resistance in Chimea's Blood Mountains, organized around Candelum and the last decanted sunlight their people own; it ends when the glass runs dry.

name: The Living Forest
blurb: The forest itself is Stipen's greatest defense.

name: The Lizard's Back
blurb: A geographic feature along the southern border with Zintacas—a ridge or rocky formation marking the edge of Stipen's forests.

name: The Lost Ages
blurb: 30,000–10,209 BSD. The Laughing Plague destroys Craggus' legacy; dwarves are born; a succession of calamities reduces Alaria to ruins.

name: The Lygrone
blurb: A slower, wider river in southeastern Wycendeula, fed by the Shivyll and several minor tributaries.

name: The Massacre Hills
blurb: Northeast of Yibiye, the Massacre Hills rise from the sea—a rocky promontory that has earned its name repeatedly.

name: The Megélren Exile
blurb: The casting-out of a Waterdark faction after the Severing; a real political expulsion the present myth later mistook for the banishing of a blood.

name: The Misery's Gift
blurb: The Misery River's waters produce a specific effect on non-pixies: crushing, inexplicable sorrow.

name: The Mobari Reach
blurb: The eastern portion of the Dunes, where sand gives way to rocky hardpan approaching Shyona's borders, is called the Mobari Reach.

name: The Modern Era
blurb: The third and current eon. ~13,500 years ago (10,209 BSD)–Now. The rebuilding from the Shattering, the World Fire's calendar zero, and the political world of today.

name: The Moon Road
blurb: The covert network that smuggles enslaved Drasnians out of Gorath through the Moon Wilds, the one border the empire will not cross.

name: The Moonwatchers
blurb: A secretive cult that venerates the lost Third Moon and prays, every Hollownight, for the catastrophe of its return.

name: The Mydruli
blurb: The largest river in eastern Wycendeula, originating in the central Kilbyurn Mountains.

name: The Needle
blurb: The narrowest point of the Tarkhon Passage—perhaps half a mile of water between Tarkhetan's walls and Torsnerhun fortress on the Astrelle shore.

name: The Neferati Founding
blurb: Around 2776 SD the Neferati came north, kindled the Evertorch from Yaif's sacred fire, and founded Tarkhon on the Needle.

name: The Northern Archipelago
blurb: A cluster of islands north of Pesalolo, between the mainland and Pukot Island.

name: The Nox
blurb: The hills at the heart of Eberri Ygonzi, where the forest grows wrong and light goes to die.

name: The Oblivion Years
blurb: 11,500–10,400 BSD. A void-heavy leyline convergence collapsed, and the coherence that lets a people hold memory and identity wore away across generations.

name: The Ofrenia Passage
blurb: The strait between Paluva Amati and Ofrenia, connecting the Gulf of Joswik to the open Middle Sea.

name: The Order of Bryn
blurb: The orthodox solar church that maintains the singing rota holding Bryn's warmth-band, collects the sun-tax, and enforces the law of light against unsanctioned prayer-coalitions.

name: The Oshkensi Plateau
blurb: A vast grassland plateau in the southwestern interior, rising above the surrounding valleys like a table set for giants.

name: The Pacification of the Eronia Range
blurb: Klor's seven-year extermination of the Eronia pass peoples around 3176 SD, which cleared Tarkhon's southern frontier and left no one to contest it.

name: The Pantheon
blurb: Alaria's gods were once mortals, and they live on prayer. When the prayers stop, the god ends.

name: The Peaks
blurb: Above the settlements, the mountains rise into regions where only the most advanced monks venture.

name: The Peonie River
blurb: The Peonie flows past Wisgarrd's walls, its banks carpeted with the flowering plants that give the river its name.

name: The Pin Isles
blurb: The northern boundary of Iqes Sound is marked by the Pin Isles — a chain of narrow, pointed islands stretching between Iqes and Gyosha.

name: The Pugrai Lakes
blurb: Three coastal lakes along Wycendeula's eastern shore where the White Wastes meet God's Bathtub.

name: The Purlin Hills
blurb: Running north-south through the heart of Titania, the Purlin Hills provide the closest thing the jungle has to highlands.

name: The Rattlespit Hills
blurb: A cluster of lower hills rises within the northern Jackal Mountains, forming a transitional zone between the main peaks and the approaches to the Thundering…

name: The Refusal
blurb: The founding choice of the Nameless Ones: generations ago, when Shyona's first name-stripped exiles survived the Hills of Dolor and decided to be a people rather than corpses.

name: The Reign of Dragons
blurb: 10M–2.5M years ago. Nagatayora's children dominate all six planes; elves are made by the druids; Eyachria challenges Gaea and falls.

name: The Return of Dragons
blurb: 8,104 BSD. Dragonkind recovered its numbers and forced its way back into a rebuilt world that met the returnees with fire.

name: The Ride of Ta Minn
blurb: The generations-long cavalry expansion that fixed Ta Minn's borders by running out of open grass, and set its frozen northern war with Anarak.

name: The River Elf Blade Master
blurb: The river elves, although small in population, are a people dedicated to honing the practice of blade dancing.

name: The River of Petals
blurb: The most notable waterway in the Cerulean Crests is the River of Petals, which rises from springs in the central range and flows south toward…

name: The River Settlements
blurb: Most of Kazül's population lives along the river that flows from Glorin.

name: The Royon
blurb: Where the Green Wilds meet the Dunes of Kunagi, the forest thins into scrubland.

name: The Salt Moors
blurb: Salt marshes in the lowlands, source of preserving salt for the fishing industry.

name: The Scattered Islands
blurb: Dozens of islands dot the sound, ranging from bare rocks that barely break the surface to forested islets large enough to camp on.

name: The School of Summoning
blurb: An ancient academy straddling the border between Chechol and the Dalizi city-state of Turbuni, specializing in communion magic, planar contact, and summoning techniques.

name: The Screech
blurb: A jagged range of peaks separating The Glog from the eastern coastline, named for the constant, keening sound the wind makes as it tears through…

name: The Screech Peaks (Ios Side)
blurb: The southwestern slopes of the Screech Peaks define Ios's northern boundary.

name: The Seawatch Mountains
blurb: A range of hills and low mountains in south-central Eloesi.

name: The Seventh Dawn
blurb: 0 SD–Now. The current age of Alaria, beginning after the World Fire; from the Titansworn war to present day (3,376 SD).

name: The Severance
blurb: The dynastic break three centuries ago when human hands took the Tarkhon throne from the diluted Neferati founding line.

name: The Severing of the Dragon-Bonds
blurb: Around 2976 SD the Waterdark order cut every dragon-bond in Adron in a single working, ending the Wyrmward without killing a single dragon.

name: The Sha Kariki
blurb: The central river provides the only reliable water in the forest during the dry season.

name: The Shattered Sea
blurb: The heart of the Western Isles is this chaotic archipelago—hundreds of distinct islands, atolls, and shoals packed into treacherous waters.

name: The Shirik River
blurb: The Shirik River rises in the eastern Hills of Sarrow, fed by snowmelt from the Bellowing Mountains.

name: The Shivyll
blurb: Balduahr's primary river, flowing southeast from the mountains into the plains.

name: The Shizander
blurb: Originating in the northeastern Kilbyurn Mountains at the Buzagi Wempu headwaters, the Shizander flows east through the northern plains before curving south toward the White…

name: The Shogi Monastery
blurb: The heart of Azuros, a complex of stone buildings that have grown organically over centuries.

name: The Shroud Isles
blurb: A fog-bound island group at the eastern edge of the Northern Isles, wrapped in standing mist that the lich Gelnor holds in place.

name: The Silwyn River
blurb: The lifeblood of the region.

name: The Singing Peaks
blurb: Several of the highest peaks produce sounds in high winds—deep, resonant tones that carry for miles.

name: The Singing Trees
blurb: A grove in the island's interior where the wind produces an eerie, almost musical sound as it passes through the branches.

name: The Sivakr Emergence
blurb: Fifty years ago the Sivakr came up from more than sixteen centuries underground to trade, betting the surface had forgotten why it drove them below.

name: The Skies
blurb: The open air above the Surfacelands, rising from the cloud-lanes of sky ships to the thin cold heights where the Syngers sail beneath the stars.

name: The Slick Silver Strike
blurb: In 3358 SD a deep shaft beneath Lohva broke into the first slick silver vein, beginning the silvertongue plague now destroying Ubrik.

name: The Southern Wasting
blurb: Around 3176 SD the southern grounds of Deo Esari emptied in a single season; the priesthood sealed the cause and has kept it sealed since.

name: The Springs of Vyowehr
blurb: Sacred springs bubble up in Ierya's heart—cold, clear water emerging from deep beneath the Walking Forest.

name: The Stone Forest
blurb: The western border between Enymu and Nektuna is marked by one of Alaria's great mysteries: an entire forest transformed into stone.

name: The Stone Slums
blurb: Foggy districts of flat, slate-built, short buildings where the poor of Myorna make their homes.

name: The Suki Jungle
blurb: The dense tropical jungle that covers much of Chimea's territory, divided into three regions:

name: The Sunless Vigil
blurb: Five hundred years when the sun's celestial-glass core was corrupted and Koras fought alone to reignite it; the bond between Koras and Bryn deepened after.

name: The Suverain River
blurb: The Suverain flows through western Tyberoskos, its course determined by the same limestone geology that creates the sinkholes.

name: The Swallowing Mire
blurb: A vast bog in southern Blyss where the boundary between land and water dissolves entirely.

name: The Syla Mur
blurb: A mountain-fed river on the eastern edge of the forest, draining the western Kilbyurn slopes before joining the Shivyll system in Balduahr territory.

name: The Tarkhon Grain Exchange
blurb: The Tarkhon institution that grades Enymu's harvest and fixes the empire's bread price each year — a monopsony in all but the word.

name: The Tarkhon Strait
blurb: The only sea passage between the Middle Sea and the open ocean, where every westbound cargo pays Tarkhon's toll or burns.

name: The Tarns
blurb: A region of highland lakes and pools in northeastern Mueras, fed by rainfall from the Mueras Highlands and streams descending from the Dustwind Plateau.

name: The Three Repulsions
blurb: Three times across the last century, Chimea marched legions on Pyaganos's hills of Phriorys. Three times the dragon turned the assault into sport.

name: The Titanic Priesthood
blurb: The theocratic order that governs Deo Esari, harvests the titan bones, and has spent two centuries sealing the cause of the southern disaster.

name: The Torn Hour
blurb: About 90,000 years ago, late in the Golden Age. Vyanoweir cartographers tried to bind a seam of time and tore it instead.

name: The Toxic Marshes
blurb: The heart of Nashua is poison.

name: The Training Grounds
blurb: Deep Promisewood contains the camps where young Temptari spend their formative decades.

name: The Troughs
blurb: A network of deep gorges cutting through the western Wanderlands peninsula, connecting to the sea and flooding violently with each high tide.

name: The Twin Cities
blurb: Yuki and its sister city Garlow exist in unusual symbiosis.

name: The Twins
blurb: Two contrasting islets off the southwest — one windswept rock, one sheltered scrub — used by seasonal fishermen.

name: The Tyerferniin
blurb: A southern river draining the lower Kilbyurns toward the Eceraen foothills.

name: The Unburied
blurb: A hidden Avalon network that intercepts null-Declared children and moves them out of their districts before their families grieve and let them go.

name: The Unhealed
blurb: Domains: wound-and-witness, testimony of the broken; rising cohort; cosmologically newest daemon, refusing to promise healing.

name: The Unmade
blurb: A void-bound remnant in the deepest reaches of the Caves of Nykthos, left half-erased by the working that canceled the cave's leyline signature.

name: The Vision Pools
blurb: Certain pools along Honor Creek are said to grant visions of the future to those who drink from them.

name: The Vogenfeld Pact
blurb: Around 2876 SD the Uline dwarves and the young Tarkhon empire bargained the northern passes into a tribute-for-shield compact, cut in stone at the Pass of Oaths.

name: The Wastes
blurb: Endless gray landscape above Zylogmus Eternus — burning rivers, deadly radiation, chimeric beasts, and tornadoes nearly daily.

name: The Wastes of Al G'hesh
blurb: A desert necropolis peninsula whose dunes are crowded with tombs, god-courts, and the dead-cities of a vanished dynasty.

name: The Watchtower
blurb: A grim settlement clinging to a sheltered valley at the western edge of the Pelmar Mountains, visible from the approaches to Wind Gap.

name: The Weeping Wood
blurb: The jungle surrounding Locquine grows thick and strange.

name: The Whistle-Stone Mountains
blurb: The mountain range that defines Glorin.

name: The White Wastes
blurb: A vast white desert in Wycendeula.

name: The Widebarrow Mountains
blurb: A moderate-sized mountain range runs diagonally from the southwest to the northeast, roughly bisecting the continent.

name: The Wolfmaw
blurb: The Wolfmaw is a peninsula extending roughly 30 miles into the bay, split at its tip into two rocky headlands that resemble open jaws—hence the…

name: The World Fire
blurb: 10 BSD, calendar zero. A lone fire-paragon over-channeled the Yolus seam until it ruptured, ending the BSD reckoning and opening the SD age.

name: The Wurmspine
blurb: The massive mountain range that forms Tarkhon's northern frontier, separating the empire from the orcish Wildwood and stretching from the coastal regions in the west…

name: The Wyrmward
blurb: The long age of the early Seventh Dawn when Adron's Nagashi bonded and rode war-dragons, and the kingdom held the eastern Middle Sea from the air.

name: The Xixes River
blurb: The Xixes River flows south from the Knulak Lakes toward the Qynxis Mountains.

name: Theramure
blurb: Home of a powerful firemage.

name: Thergon
blurb: The coral city Xynoth Azkonor ruled, died in, and raised as his first undead host before the Pity Knights silenced it to ruins.

name: Thermarift
blurb: Deep within the canyons of Crevakus in Blyzrynthine, huge fiery rifts tear through the ground.

name: Therozonaur
blurb: Crazy, dumb, but pain immune giant domesticated animal.

name: Therríneth
blurb: Domain: secrets, hidden knowledge, the unspoken; dead cohort (Golden Age); god of mystery cults whose last initiate died unfinished.

name: Thespia
blurb: A human agricultural kingdom in chaos.

name: Thizun
blurb: A ruined temple complex on a small island northwest of the main archipelago, visible only at low tide.

name: Thorne
blurb: An ancient druid who dwells in the interior of L'Coth D'hari, in the Elder Wilds.

name: Thorne
blurb: A region in the interior of L'Coth D'hari where an ancient druid of the same name has dwelt since before the Gamori arrived.

name: Thorum
blurb: Northernmost of the Widebarrow mountain states—a dwarven highland kingdom caught between Shyona's cultural gravity and the isolation of altitude.

name: Thraken Lake
blurb: A cold mountain lake on Istora's eastern boundary, which the Istori call the edge of their world.

name: Thrallwood
blurb: The western slopes of the Phalkrene Mountains drain into Thrallwood—a swamp-forest that stretches to Shamerei Bay.

name: Throatslit Mountains
blurb: Home to the Throatslit Murder Clan, descended from a single legendary goblin whose skill with knives became the foundation of an entire warrior tradition.

name: Thrumbling Bay
blurb: The bay on the western coast where the rivers of Aphenlein empty into the South Sea.

name: Thrygar
blurb: Domains: primal howl, untamed wilderness, the first breath at first light; current cohort; Thrygun patron of the cry before strategy.

name: Thrygun
blurb: Blue-skinned, red-haired wild orcs of the White Hills; the most primal orcish heritage, guided by instinct and ancient tribal law.

name: Thunder Plains
blurb: Named after the giants whose heavy footsteps give the plains its namesake.

name: Thunderdark Peaks
blurb: A rugged mountain range in the eastern Inner Rim, home to the Strømgodden dwarves of Trømgar.

name: Thundering Mountains
blurb: The Thundering Mountains form the great northern wall of the Dalizi Highlands, a massive range that separates the highland interior from the Wanderlands to the…

name: Tiber's Passage
blurb: The strait south of Erpeus, between the island and Tykos to the south.

name: Tidewalkers
blurb: Human islanders of the Aikon shallows whose lives follow the twice-daily tide, governed by the Tide-Counters who call the safe windows.

name: Tiira
blurb: Goddess of the silver elves; rising cohort; memory-wiped chosen one who carries divine power she does not understand.

name: Tikhaya
blurb: Self-certain, contemplative humans of central and northern Alaria; philosophers, mathematicians, and martial warriors who value strength and truth over idle talk.

name: Tiknurtir
blurb: A southern town near the Bejeweled Palace, positioned to watch the cursed structure without getting too close.

name: Tilswyurn Hills
blurb: Hills in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Time
blurb: The Kethic element of time's local rate, channeled through the Izzus leyline. It can stretch or hasten a moment, never touch the world's true clock.

name: Time and Seasons
blurb: The sun passes around the center strip of the sky every day, all year.

name: Timeless Wastes
blurb: Southwest of Blyzrynthine and west of Giantfell lie the Timeless Wastes—a frozen desert where time itself behaves strangely.

name: Timiru
blurb: Cross between poseidon and a decrepit old hag.

name: Tirenthar (Town)
blurb: Population: ~800

name: Tirexonos
blurb: The capital, such as it is.

name: Tishkung Mountains
blurb: Mountains on Pesalolo.

name: Titan
blurb: The titans were created by Azus.

name: Titan Blood & Bones
blurb: The blood and bones of the titans are among the rarest and most valuable substances in Alaria.

name: Titan Stairs
blurb: The Titan Stairs are the western gateway to the Dalizi Highlands, a dramatic stepped formation where the land rises from the lowland Dalizi Confederation toward…

name: Titanwurm
blurb: These now extinct beasts were literally larger than mountains—stretchnig sometimes up to 200 miles long.

name: Tixpi
blurb: Domains: gemstone mechanism, time-and-craft, the gear that measures the hour the gem holds; current cohort; Ohblex patron of the clockwork that is also a prayer.

name: Toben Ashling
blurb: Fieri halfling commander of Chimea's Second Legion, quartered at Gilla, who runs an unsanctioned back-channel with the Aureum resistance against the Bloodlings.

name: Toh Hural
blurb: Northern city, positioned near the contested border with Anarak.

name: Tolarian Mage-Kings
blurb: The seven-generation dynasty that ruled Old Tolaria and built the Apparatus of Severance to purify magic by tearing its tangled traditions apart.

name: Tollgate
blurb: A fortified city-state occupying a strategic island at the mouth of Phyndarr Sound—the self-proclaimed 'Gatekeepers of the North.' Tollgate charges tolls on ships passing between…

name: Tomb of Angi
blurb: High in the Ashy Mountains, carved into the side of a dormant peak, lies the Tomb of Angi.

name: Tomb of Braggus Solas
blurb: Dungeon tomb of the greatest light mage to have ever lived.

name: Tomb of Theoron
blurb: The burial complex of King Theoron III, last ruler of the Kingdom of Theoron, lies in the Winger Mountains—one of the few locations within the…

name: Tomgryir Eror
blurb: Contaminated hill country in the southern Kelder foothills, within Deo Esari's territory.

name: Tongueroot Forest
blurb: A forest in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Tonyfall River
blurb: The major river system running through South Suki, navigable for much of its length.

name: Toothlar Mountains
blurb: The Toothlar Mountains form the southwestern boundary of the Dalizi Highlands, a jagged wall of peaks that separates the highland interior from the lowland approaches.

name: Toothy Hills
blurb: Northwestern hills near the border with Thespia.

name: Top of the World
blurb: The highest peak in the western Nysanna Range, and by some measures the highest point in all of Clueanda.

name: Tora Mazîl
blurb: The heart of Qet Yemani—a vast elevated forest covering the central and northern portions of the nation.

name: Torguli Islands
blurb: A long, curving chain of islands stretching southwest from the main Tyvern archipelago into open water.

name: Torin Creek
blurb: A major tributary entering the Jiji from the west, Torin Creek provides access to the deep marsh territories.

name: Tormlek Lake
blurb: A shallow, mist-shrouded lake on the western edge of Fylvrae Sylvrym, where the forest gives way to coastal scrubland south of the Bay of Wolves.

name: Tornia
blurb: Tornia is the northernmost state within Ofrenia, one of the three kingdoms controlled by feuding siblings on the strategically vital island at the heart of…

name: Tornia
blurb: A state on Ofrenia.

name: Toros
blurb: The second-largest settlement, controlled by a clan known for military prowess.

name: Torsnerhun Fortress
blurb: The most prestigious military posting in Tarkhon, directly across the Needle from Tarkhetan itself.

name: Torulius Mountains
blurb: The northern range, less dramatic than the Sunwing Peaks but more accessible.

name: Torven Vorekan
blurb: The Moon Road's organizer inside the Pesalolo slave camps. Three centuries of bondage were meant to break the Drasnians. He is the standing proof they didn't.

name: Tothpa & Oychka
blurb: Towns in northern Lier, in the shadow of the Murder Hills.

name: Tower of Azül
blurb: Where the Beathamuir River crosses a water leyline, the Tower of Azül rises from an island in the river's center.

name: Tower of Chains
blurb: Kinda like Repunzel's tower.

name: Tower of Gwyndar
blurb: An ancient fortress deep in the jungles, visible above the canopy, serving as a landmark that sailors use for navigation even as they avoid approaching.

name: Tower Yprazi
blurb: A sealed black spire at the heart of the Hills of the Damned, prison of a thing whose name the Marrovini will not speak aloud.

name: Towers of Ederhi
blurb: Ancient spires of fused glass rising from the Jahazai sands.

name: Traduci
blurb: Major city in the west, gateway to the Crags of Geth and trade with the Griselian goblin city-states.

name: Traevluong
blurb: The eastern coastal jungle, running from the Eskagon Hills down to the southern waters.

name: Traitor Bay
blurb: A bay within Chimea.

name: Traitor's Watch
blurb: A fortified watchtower northwest of Erebinthias, overlooking the approaches to Wisdom Creek and the ruins of Argysis beyond.

name: Trakkozur
blurb: Weathered deep-cavern dwarves of Eoga who seem primitive but are the ancient wardens who chained the titan Hykravones beneath the Cascades of Ygg.

name: Trauma Lake
blurb: A large lake on the eastern edge of Eberri Ygonzi, where the forest gives way to the approaches of Kama Sa'malina.

name: Traut
blurb: The northern outpost, positioned near the Croaking Briars.

name: Triton
blurb: Tritons are the proud aquatic warriors of the deep, dwelling in magnificent underwater cities carved from coral and stone.

name: Triuci
blurb: A hilly region in the northern Sandreach Mountains, home to hill giant clans known throughout the region for their aggression and brutality.

name: Troenka
blurb: The Troenka are cruel, wood-like beings that inhabit the deep forests of Ichneum and Tenekee.

name: Troikan Strait
blurb: The channel separating the northern Farlands from the Seacliff Coast islands.

name: Trollkin
blurb: Half-breeds and outcasts—trollkin are the unwanted children of humans and trolls.

name: Trolls
blurb: Stupid, large, brown to green humanoids.

name: Tromaran
blurb: Domains: deep-current, battle-tide, the trident from below; current cohort; Triton patron of the strike out of dark water.

name: Trømgar
blurb: Stone, steel, and blood.

name: Trømgodd
blurb: Domains: glorious combat, sacrificial fury, the spiked rite-armor; current cohort; Strømgodden battle-witness who counts self-inflicted wounds.

name: Trøndmar
blurb: Domains: the wolf-pact, the snow-track, the kept word in the cold; current cohort; Zwaeron patron of the rider-and-wolf bond.

name: Tropizo
blurb: A town in the corridor route, existing primarily to service giant herder caravans.

name: Troyan Mountains
blurb: The Troyan Mountains form the southern wall of the Dalizi Highlands, a range of broken peaks and deep valleys that separates the highland interior from…

name: Troyt Loofos
blurb: A dense forest in the western reaches, known for its strange acoustics—sounds carry unpredictably, echoing from directions that make no sense.

name: True Name
blurb: One's true name is their magical identity in the world, and is far more powerful than a being's normal name used in everyday life.

name: Trusiin
blurb: A meadow in the east-central interior, another swampy clearing where the jungle's intensity lessens.

name: Tsam'iynu
blurb: gnome theocracy.

name: Tswili Lake
blurb: A lake in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Tugachi
blurb: A forest within Shyona.

name: Tugon's Castle
blurb: A fortress in Raric Bay that has stood for six centuries despite being built by a man who died before laying the first stone.

name: Tukiwood
blurb: The southern forest of Sestros, marking the transition between the kingdom's settled grasslands and the wilder country beyond.

name: Tuktuk
blurb: The Tuktuk are a warrior culture of turtle-folk who have claimed the volcanic island of Tyvern as their ancestral home.

name: Tul Azreitnok
blurb: In the southern hills and dunes region of Kura stands Tul Azreitnok—a fortress and specialized academy dedicated to the study of midnight fire, a rare…

name: Tunrrus
blurb: A fortified town in the northern jungle, positioned near the approaches to the Green Mountain passes.

name: Tuo
blurb: A ruined fortress on the Ovuluria River, strategically positioned between Tadinu and the eastern territories.

name: Turbuni
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Turiik Forest
blurb: The northern reaches of Shykasas transition from saltwater marsh to dense coastal forest where the tides still reach but the canopy blocks the sun.

name: Turquish Bay
blurb: A bay in Ve.

name: Turxis Golathi
blurb: Turxis Golathi means 'Northern Hell' in orcish.

name: Turxis Golathi
blurb: The northern section of the Twaan Forests, Turxis Golathi is the oldest and densest part of the woodland.

name: Tuskeselom
blurb: Qindu Halfling god of liveliness, energy, birds, colors.

name: Tuur (Capital)
blurb: Population: ~3,000

name: Twaan
blurb: Relatives of the skaag, though larger, more ferocious, and far less friendly toward other races.

name: Twaan Forests
blurb: The Twaan Forests are a sprawling woodland complex in the northeastern Westwilds, bounded by the Kharvorn Mountains to the east and the arid scrublands to…

name: Twilight Forest
blurb: A vast forest shrouded in supernatural darkness in the southeastern reaches of the Frostmarch Peninsula.

name: Two Brothers
blurb: A distinctive pair of peaks north of Erebinthias, visible for miles along the approaches to Argysis.

name: Twyl
blurb: Twyl is a magical crystal that produces oxygen with no apparent energy input.

name: Tyatach River
blurb: Southern river flowing through the Killing Hills to the coast west of Tekos.

name: Tybalexyn
blurb: Gnome sky-trade-route cartographer; the definitive authority on skystone shipping lanes and high-altitude waypoints.

name: Tyberoskos
blurb: South of the Pearly Mountains, where the land descends toward the coast, Tyberoskos spreads across rolling hills of limestone and clay.

name: Tygoss
blurb: A mountain settlement in the Green Mountains themselves, Tygoss occupies a defensible position along one of the main passes.

name: Tykon Channel
blurb: The waters east of Erpeus and west of Ofrenia, separating Erpeus from the three kingdoms' ongoing civil war.

name: Tykos
blurb: Tykos is an island chain south of Erpeus, home to minotaur clans who've practiced slavery for longer than most civilizations have existed.

name: Tykrenv
blurb: The Tykrenv are scavengers of the deep desert, humanoid in body but possessing the head and secondary wings of a vulture.

name: Tylloréth
blurb: Domain: trade, roads, the rebuilt routes; dead cohort (Craggus era); god of the merchant prayer, killed when the Plague made every road a vector.

name: Tyror Miig
blurb: The northern grasslands of Rakite—summer territory for the buffalo herds and the tribes that follow them.

name: Tyror Weklis
blurb: The southern grasslands of Rakite—winter territory where the herds shelter from the cold winds that sweep down from the north.

name: Tytheraxi
blurb: Domains: agony as purification, the bleach of long pain, scars-that-cleanse; current cohort; Tytheri patron of suffering turned inward.

name: Tytheri
blurb: Bleached-white orcs transformed by ancient ichor from the cursed Agony Stones; Tythmasters grow to ten feet and lead through blood-ritual power.

name: Tythikerys
blurb: Tythikerys is a vast stretch of arid scrubland in southwestern Central Aboyinzu, homeland of the Tytheri blood orcs.

name: Tyungula
blurb: Giant king crab under the Solemn Sea

name: Tyunigogo
blurb: "Where the Fire Meets the Sea"

name: Tyura River
blurb: A northern river flowing from the Green Mountains through rebel-held territory.

name: Tyvak
blurb: Domain: strength, iron, labor; dead cohort (Craggus era); god of the smiths and laborers who rebuilt civilization.

name: Tyvarn
blurb: Domains: volcanic island, the forge under the sea, the rock that holds beneath the storm; current cohort; Tuktuk patron of the standing place.

name: Tyvern Isles
blurb: A group of islands off the Seacleft Coast, home to the Tuktuk people.

name: Uara
blurb: An eastern coastal town on Whitefin Bay, traditionally focused on fishing and salt-drying.

name: Ubrik
blurb: A Uline dwarf nation in the mountains of the Middle Sea coast, between Camaran and Bestacia.

name: Uchaelv
blurb: A western settlement on the Hekat River, serving as Qet Yemani's gateway to the western highlands.

name: Uhionai
blurb: The far northwestern orc state, occupying the ancient Ryunimir Forest.

name: Uihona
blurb: Domains: tree-rebirth, cyclic renewal, the death that grows leaves; current cohort; Uihonaii patron of the labored cycle.

name: Uihonaii
blurb: Forest-bonded orcs whose dead become trees; bark-skinned and centuries-wise, they transform at death and are reborn from sacred wood.

name: Ulbrizil
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Uline
blurb: Industrious underground dwarves who adapted to mine life over generations; pragmatic and stubborn, masters of iron combat and air-circulation engineering.

name: Ulkevolm
blurb: Home to Tarnak

name: Ulkevolm
blurb: A state in the Luquihn Desert region.

name: Ulle Alari
blurb: A settlement on the western edge of the plateau where it meets the Mathavia Mountains.

name: Ulm
blurb: A point of interest in the desert.

name: Ulvma
blurb: Also known as the Wolfmother, Cannae'Empiress, as well as several other names, Ulvma was the second child of Gaea, and the last to die in…

name: Ulvsein
blurb: Ulvsjael who believe each wolf must survive alone, becoming legendary scouts and trackers whose solitary skills strengthen the whole pack.

name: Ulvsjael
blurb: Descendants of the warriors who drank the blood of Ulvma, the wolf-mother who fell beside the mortal armies against the titan Hykravones.

name: Ulvskyn
blurb: Ulvsjael who keep closest to Ulvma's pack teachings, forming meritocratic communities where contribution alone determines rank.

name: Ulyas
blurb: The immortal halfling king of Kerwin, ancient when Tarkhon was young, the living witness no dynasty can outlast.

name: Umbral Agents
blurb: Shadow-beings native to Malstaris, never alive and never anyone's. The shadow-realm's enforcers, and the first things through a Shadowrift.

name: Umbral Hills
blurb: Hills in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Umbryn
blurb: The umbryn are large, dark-attuned panther-like creatures.

name: Umlas
blurb: The holy capital rises from the central plains where the original covenant was struck.

name: Undead
blurb: When a strand of the dead is kept from leaving the material plane, it rises as undead—most mindless, spiritless, and easily enslaved.

name: Underbears
blurb: Large, gray-green tunnel-bears.

name: Untouched Sea
blurb: A sea bordering Central Aboyinzu.

name: Üod
blurb: One of the three leadership city-states of the First Brotherhood—the ancient dwarven coalition formed when the world was far more dangerous.

name: Upoceax
blurb: Upoceax (pronounced "YOO-poe-say") is one of the three major continents of Alaria, located in the southwest.

name: Ural of the Third Moon
blurb: Deep, purple color.

name: Ural of the Third Moon
blurb: The dragon Ural of the Third Moon dwells somewhere in or near the Twilight Forest, though its exact lair remains unknown.

name: Urchin Bay
blurb: The large bay south of Oyster Bay, named for the giant urchins that make it treacherous.

name: Urk Noulgas
blurb: On the southwest point of the Wyrm Teeth stands Urk Noulgas—a ruined fortress that was once home to the Keeper of the Wyrms.

name: Urkhuk
blurb: The second major city, positioned closer to the Erasnun border.

name: Urkrakr
blurb: A ruined city-state on the Xeltri Plateau, destroyed by hill giants.

name: Urok
blurb: A large island in the Greenwater Isles.

name: Urun Mountains
blurb: The eastern wall of Moigos, separating the swamplands from Fernius Sound.

name: Urzahd
blurb: The capital, in the northwest where the river from Glorin provides life.

name: Urzula
blurb: A city-state built beneath massive waterfalls in the Seyiy region of the Elder Wilds—the only significant settlement in the region outside L'Coth D'hari.

name: Uun
blurb: Domains: satisfaction, pride, joy, disillusionment; current cohort; deity of the cycle between fulfillment and its loss.

name: Uvedr
blurb: An eastern settlement near Deadwood Swamp.

name: Uzdakh
blurb: Domain: monstrous things, the deep, what should not be looked at; dead cohort (Golden Age); the chthonic deep-god worshipped with averted gaze.

name: Uzgaruk
blurb: Domain: vengeance, the unforgiven, the long memory of wrongs; dead cohort (Golden Age); chthonic god whose cult burned itself out in the God War's first decade.

name: Uzikni
blurb: A mountain town at one of the few reliable passes through the Gemstone Peaks.

name: Vad Ilghak
blurb: The capital city, built into the Grey Mountains.

name: Vadu Inat
blurb: The prison island at the mouth of Prison Bay, a black rock fortress that has held Aal Salma's most dangerous and valuable captives for over…

name: Vaelish
blurb: Roughly eight hundred survivors of the dragon-destroyed Kingdom of Vael, hidden for four centuries in the fortress of Mountainveil.

name: Vaelos
blurb: Domain: war, honor, conquest; dead cohort (Golden Age); the formal war deity whose theology helped ignite the God War.

name: Vaeloten
blurb: Domains: silence, the seen gesture, the sight-bond; current cohort; Rakiten patron whose true name is a sign, not a sound.

name: Vaerivra
blurb: Domains: the trackless forest, immediate justice, balance as physical practice; current cohort; Caerene wood-patron whom the Nemo still pray to as ghosts.

name: Vai-Vai
blurb: Domain: messengers, heralds, the trustworthy reporter; dead cohort (Craggus era); the cult-call god whose herald-cry was his name, killed by sacred-inviolability collapse.

name: Vainye River
blurb: The Vainye descends from the Phalkrene heights, fed by mountain springs and perpetual mist-rain.

name: Vakhreon
blurb: A Deoric mage of the Lost Ages whose abandoned household of reanimated servants became the ghoul infestation of Bestacia's forests.

name: Valentinas
blurb: A village on the southern fringe, near the Ojos Goros forest.

name: Valkyries
blurb: Magestic.

name: Valley of the Witches
blurb: The Valley of the Witches is a secluded vale in the Morygun Mountains, east of Korgül and northwest of Phaerhom.

name: Valley of Whispers
blurb: A high mountain valley deep in the Aktarvkus, marked on maps but rarely visited.

name: Varn
blurb: Domains: wakefulness, productivity; current cohort; patron of those whose prayer is a second hour of work.

name: Vasco
blurb: A slaver captain working the Slaver's Coast circuit, twice sunk by the dragon Agtakkeri and superstitious about every dawn at sea.

name: Vassanéth
blurb: Domain: fate, oaths, binding promises; dead cohort (Golden Age); the first major Golden-Age casualty, killed by oaths nullifying each other before the eruption.

name: Vassum
blurb: The northwestern city-state, positioned where Moonwood meets Jungula Serpentine.

name: Vastilin
blurb: The human capital on Mt.

name: Vaznik of Tarkhetan
blurb: Tarkhetan natural-philosopher of the Firemage tradition who held the Evertorch was leyline geology, not miracle.

name: Vazreth Sythrox
blurb: A thread-binder of Chaal Nazzerox sent up the River of Wights to leash the masterless wight-lord Mylanor; the binding failed and he did not return.

name: Ve
blurb: The isolated continent of the far east, walled off from the rest of Alaria by open ocean: dry savannah and desert in the north, deep beast-haunted jungle in the south.

name: Veilkeepers
blurb: A neutral order of water-shapers who hold the Foggy Isles under standing mist, selling sanctuary and keeping the secrets of everyone who buys it.

name: Vel Ithagas
blurb: Home to the lich queen Sirinas.

name: Velaendor
blurb: Domains: sea-current, oceanic memory, the depth that remembers; current cohort; pan-kendor patron of what the surface forgot.

name: Velarion
blurb: Domains: civic mankind, polity, reborn-faith continuity; current cohort; the patron a city prays to when it is being a city.

name: Veldrath
blurb: Domain: protection, shields, safety; dead cohort (Craggus era); the defender deity of the rebuilding world.

name: Velisca
blurb: Veramus's spymaster, sent under a false name to learn how far Slavewatch had rotted. What she found could unmake the emperor she serves.

name: Velorax the Unfinished
blurb: A young dragon frozen above the ruins of Elderran, caught in the moment of the Apparatus of Severance's activation.

name: Velorin of Istora
blurb: Elven mythographer of the Lost Ages; reads the three-soul death mechanics and daemon cycles as sacred cosmological texts.

name: Velthari
blurb: Secretive star-accountants of the Nysanna foothills who sell precise stellar records and, by founding compact, refuse to worship the stars they track.

name: Veluminante Forest
blurb: The purple-tinged forest that covers much of Xabraedia's interior, spreading outward from Starfall like a slow infection.

name: Velwey
blurb: Dream-harvesting dwarves of Movasi who extract and crystallize dreams via hallucinogens, Psy dream-walking, and Deoric ritual.

name: Velyv
blurb: Domains: the unseen hand, secret patronage, influence that leaves no trace; current cohort; Gyv patron of what is done behind.

name: Ven
blurb: Spirit slaves, usually kept in jars and other small containers.

name: Venalthier
blurb: Venalthier is the frozen polar region occupying the extreme southwest of Upoceax.

name: Venchi Lake
blurb: A lake in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Ver Kanis
blurb: The northern river, originating high in Vokas Enrisikna and flowing east through Tyror Miig before turning southeast toward Kro Shiik.

name: Ver Neles
blurb: The contested eastern river, draining Kro Shiik and meandering northeast through Roule colonial territory to the sea.

name: Ver Pes
blurb: The central river, rising in the foothills where Vokas Enrisikna meets Vokas Pendi and flowing east through the Extolmni Furis before feeding Kro Shiik.

name: Veramus
blurb: Emperor of Gorath: sixty-three, elected from its own generals, kept on the throne only by new victories. The Moon Wilds defeat is unmaking him.

name: Verréneth
blurb: Domain: fortifications, the night-watch, vigilance; dead cohort (Craggus era); god of the wall and the bell, whose watchmen died at their posts.

name: Versisi
blurb: Versisi is the half-druid, half-naga royal consort of the sapphire dragon Synthyd.

name: Verucan
blurb: Volcanic dwarves with glowing red-veined obsidian skin who build civilization inside active volcanoes; sacred fury and passion drive their forge mastery.

name: Vesimar
blurb: The Tolarian mage-king who fled Elderran before the Apparatus fired and so escaped the severance. Still alive, still loose, still chasing a purified magic.

name: Vetral
blurb: The triton theocracy claims sovereignty over every drop of the Alrock.

name: Vexlings
blurb: The apex predators of the Moon Wilds: spider-goblin things with a hypnotic gaze and corrosive silver blood that have lately begun moving as one army.

name: Viktryapos
blurb: A tower carved into the red mountainside of the central Scarlet Peaks, rising from a high ridge with commanding views of the surrounding terrain.

name: Vila Bonsa
blurb: The capital rises from the central archipelago on wooden platforms and stone foundations driven deep into the muck.

name: Viper Rapids
blurb: Not a river but a section of whitewater where western mountain drainage drops toward the Sea of Sighs coast.

name: Vog Dridril
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Vogenfeld
blurb: The northern shield of Tarkhon—three Uline dwarf settlements in the Wurmspine Mountains between the empire and the orcish hordes of Hedroscobb.

name: Void
blurb: The strangest of the nine: the Kethic element of absence, channeled through the Nilus leyline, the canceling principle that unmakes other elements' workings.

name: Void Walker
blurb: The void element given coherent form and a thread of agency: a near-invisible ripple that un-makes whatever crowds it.

name: Vokas Enrisikna
blurb: The western mountain barrier of Rakite—a rugged north-south range separating the plains from the Jeh Bli wilderness beyond.

name: Vokas Pendi
blurb: The southern mountain barrier of Rakite—a lower, wider range than Vokas Enrisikna, forming the boundary between the Rakiten plains and the territories beyond.

name: Volganor
blurb: Volganor, King of Demons is the lord of all of the Stalactites of Geth.

name: Voranthek
blurb: Human leyline theorist; the specialist on force-leyline mechanics and their interaction with inhabited landscapes.

name: Vørgar
blurb: The founding thane of Vogenfeld who set the terms of the Pass of Oaths bargain, speaking for the Council of Thanes around 2876 SD.

name: Vortek River
blurb: The Emerald Coast's main trade river, running east from the interior highlands to the sea at Garum; broken by one cataract that taxes everything.

name: Vossuróth
blurb: Domain: itinerant learning, the wandering scholar, knowledge-by-foot; dead cohort (Craggus era); the moving heir to Graelith, killed on the road with her manuscripts.

name: Vosx
blurb: The second city, positioned where the Jiji River approaches the coast.

name: Vrak'nik
blurb: Domains: blood-binding, shaman-pact, the price the body pays for the spell; current cohort; Tarnak patron of the irreversible bargain Ulkevolm doctrine refuses to abstract.

name: Vraskún
blurb: Domain: the dead, judgment of souls, the Astral threshold; dead cohort (Golden Age); the judicial counterpart to Morvith, killed by administrative impossibility.

name: Vraxuni
blurb: Orc naturalist; catalogued the creature ecology of the Plenjorn Swamp and surrounding borderlands.

name: Vrazhdaya
blurb: Domains: the broken spear, defiance against the unmaking; rising cohort; Dark Ages veterans' god of the war that should not have been winnable.

name: Vrazokh
blurb: Domain: cremation, funeral fire, the dispatched body; dead cohort (Craggus era); chthonic god of the pyre, killed by bodies produced faster than pyres could be built.

name: Vrokún
blurb: Domain: wilderness, untamed places, the margin beyond civilization; dead cohort (Golden Age); the chthonic god of the world that refused the cities.

name: Vrommol
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Vukaden
blurb: The northernmost village, closest to Edkovic and practically its suburb.

name: Vulcanagak
blurb: Fire dragons.

name: Vyanoweir
blurb: Scattered remnants of a great Deoric civilization whose power to reshape reality with spoken words has nearly faded from the world.

name: Vyko
blurb: Elves who traversed the planar void; deep indigo star-speckled skin, commanding presence, and cosmic patience that transcends mortal urgency.

name: Vykus
blurb: A city-state in the Innerrim.

name: Vyrréth
blurb: Domain: cold, winter, the still killing; dead cohort (Golden Age); the quiet ironist whose cult mistook her grave for her ascendancy.

name: Vystril
blurb: The capital of Vystrilik, situated in the central-western jungle at the base of the Tambrielle Mountains.

name: Vystrilik
blurb: Trading nation controlling the Tambrielle mountain passes, capital at Vystril in the central-western jungle.

name: Wadiyah
blurb: The southernmost and most isolated of the Kingdoms of Fire.

name: Walking Forest
blurb: A vast woodland south of the Tenekee Woods and east of the Everwood, the Walking Forest is named for its most unsettling quality: it moves.

name: Wandering Hills
blurb: Hills in the Dalizi Wildlands.

name: Wanderlands
blurb: The Wanderlands form the long southern peninsula of Aboyinzu—a great curved finger of land stretching southward into the South Sea.

name: Wastes of Dezolos
blurb: Water magically evaporates here—not just because it is hot.

name: Watar
blurb: A Dwarblin merchant state on a peninsula in the western Middle Sea.

name: Watchkeep Isles
blurb: A small archipelago off the western coast of the Beak, comprising three inhabited islands and numerous rocks and shoals.

name: Water
blurb: The Kethic element of water, channeled through the Pelus leyline. A heavy, shape-holding fluid that shades toward life, unlike thin air.

name: Waterdark
blurb: Hidden within the Gozwin Mountains.

name: Way Watchers
blurb: A group of archers committed entirely to bettering their abilities in archery through 'hunt' and 'sport'.

name: Weblin
blurb: Weblin, also known as plains-goblin, are short, tan, potbellied, and a strange mixture of kind and emotionless.

name: Wendel Corvos
blurb: A Honey Lord land-factor sent from Fozyader to the Kro Shiik convocation, offering the Rakiten water-rent for a river they hold sacred.

name: Wendi
blurb: Strawberry-blonde drake-riding warriors who hurl lightning spears at giants and hold death-defying sky races, defined by flamboyant aerial courage.

name: Wer Azenzi
blurb: A cluster of small lakes in northern Eyendra, scattered through the forest between Lean D'Riin and the coast.

name: West Selengreyb
blurb: The western margin of the plateau descends through a broad transition zone called West Selengreyb.

name: West Suki
blurb: A jungle within Chimea.

name: West Tolkarsus
blurb: The northern tributary of the Grand Tolkarsus river system, the West Tolkarsus flows from the highlands north of the Westwilds, passing through the Homdrum Hills…

name: West Whitewall
blurb: The western terminus of the great Whitewall—a continuous ice cliff stretching across southern Venalthier where the continental glaciers meet the sea.

name: Western Isles
blurb: The Western Isles occupy the waters between Clueanda to the north and Rimihuica to the south, marking the edge of the known world to the…

name: Western Lakes
blurb: Several towns dot the shores of lakes in western Adron.

name: Westrim
blurb: Westrim occupies the northwestern quadrant of Rimihuica, dominated by the massive Dunes of Kunagi desert and ringed by mountains and deadly jungles.

name: Westwilds
blurb: The Westwilds occupy the southwestern corner of Clueanda, forming a transitional zone between the frozen Northlands and the maritime regions to the south.

name: Wetu
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Whisper Bay
blurb: A bay within Meadow Sound.

name: Whisper Isles
blurb: A silenced archipelago in the Western Isles where no sound carries, left mute by the Pity Knights' working against the lich who took Thergon.

name: Whispering Mangroves
blurb: The eastern edge of Phirexes, where the swamps meet the Crocodile Coast.

name: Whispering Sea
blurb: Completely still water, never any wind, almost completely quiet except for the quiet lapping of waves against your boat.

name: White Hills
blurb: Home to the Thrygun orcs.

name: White Oracle
blurb: A seer who works a western surfacing of the Izzus time-seam, selling hard judgment to the pirate isles and owing Tarkhon nothing.

name: Whitefin Bay
blurb: The major bay dominating Poum Titch's eastern coast, named for the silver-scaled whitefin fish that have sustained coastal communities for centuries.

name: Whiteling
blurb: Undead halflings risen from emotional turmoil; pale corpse vampires who hunt former loved ones with perfect memory and absent emotion.

name: Whitewood
blurb: A vast forest of silver-barked trees running north-south along Aal Salma's eastern border, forming a natural boundary with Poum Titch.

name: Widdleto
blurb: A northern mountain town in the upper Seawatch range.

name: Wights
blurb: Corpse-undead that keep a dead person's cunning and spite without their mind, the held shadow having caught more than a skeleton's did.

name: Wildwood
blurb: The Wildwood is the vast forested region that dominates the eastern Westwilds, stretching from the mountain passes of Vogenfeld in the south to the great…

name: Wind-singers
blurb: Massive, solitary apes found only in Howlwood on the continent of Ve.

name: Windor
blurb: Windor occupies the northern coast of Terrenia, a wedge of fertile lowland backed by the forested bulk of the Green Mountains.

name: Windorf
blurb: Seafaring halflings who read probability currents; their navigators plot courses through possibility space and gamble with luck itself.

name: Winger Mountains
blurb: A smaller range in the central-southern Krell Lands, the Winger Mountains are less densely populated by the insects than their northern counterpart.

name: Winter Lake
blurb: A frozen lake in the northwestern reaches of Argysis, nestled in a high cirque near the sealed city of Morphi.

name: Winterwood
blurb: Istora's sacred evergreen forest, where the Istori say their ancestors wait between lives and where Queen Lamenrae had herself crowned.

name: Winterwood
blurb: The Winterwood is the largest forest in the Celedrim Plains region, a vast expanse of ancient conifers that clings to the northern foothills of the…

name: Winzy Strait
blurb: A narrow passage of water at the southern end of the Tyvern island chain, separating the archipelago from the lands to the south.

name: Wisdom Creek
blurb: Wisdom Creek flows from the spring at Othyndria (the ruined capital, now home to Rothogomos the cruel dragon) down into the valley between Argysis and…

name: Wisewatch
blurb: At the headwaters of Wisdom Creek, overlooking the ruins of Othyndria from a safe distance, stands Wisewatch—a small fortification that predates even the Argysisean kingdom.

name: Wisgarrd
blurb: A dwarven city-state inland from Maun, built into the foothills where the Pearly Mountains descend toward the coast.

name: Wispen
blurb: Silver-skinned nomadic halflings whose bare touch absorbs object histories; the greatest trackers and investigators in Grendenheim.

name: Wister Valley
blurb: East of Xi Chin, the mountains open into Wister Valley—a high bowl of terraced farms and scattered villages that feeds the Timekeeper city.

name: Witchbreath Bay
blurb: The southern edge of the Jinxz Plains meets Witchbreath Bay, an unsettling body of water with a dire reputation.

name: Withepest
blurb: Withepest is a vast, featureless expanse of frozen steppe west of the Dreamplains and east of the Northlands transition.

name: Witvein
blurb: The eastern Nysanna Range, on its northern slopes facing the polar wastes, is dominated by the region known as Witvein—a maze of tunnel-laden glaciers that…

name: Wizzik
blurb: The current capital, seat of House Wizzik and now the center of Mkinti's government.

name: Wodhuubo
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Wolfspine
blurb: A small mountain range in the Celedrim Plains.

name: Woods of Systoril
blurb: A dense, dark coastal forest east of Melodia and south of the Piktiniti Desert.

name: World Timeline
blurb: Time, to the most modern scholar's best understanding, has consisted of three eons of full world cycles.

name: Wurmling Mountains
blurb: Not mountains at all, but composed of the collosal vertabrae of a titanwurm.

name: Wurn Mountains
blurb: Part of the greater Wurmspine range, the Wurn Mountains form Enymu's northern border.

name: Wurnfleur (Town)
blurb: Population: ~700

name: Wycendeula
blurb: Wycendeula encompasses the entire southern portion of Upoceax—a vast region stretching from the Eceraen Mountains in the far south (above Venalthier) northward to the Sand…

name: Wydling
blurb: Pale blue frost goblins of the frozen north; patient nomadic hunters who track prey across the tundra in small, tight-knit family packs.

name: Wydlings
blurb: Also known as icelings, these goblins are icy blue, have long noses, wear heavy furs, and teeth like razor sharp icicles.

name: Wykakas
blurb: The southernmost Kor city-state in Atriik, built on the Sha Sonisst as the first line of defense against Naruaghin incursions from the Bloodlands.

name: Wyrm Teeth
blurb: A range of hills in Westrim, east of Kura and north of the Milras Forest.

name: Wytrolape
blurb: The Wytrolape are white-furred giant apes residing in Kyorda.

name: Wyverns
blurb: Two legs, two wings.

name: Xabraedia
blurb: An island east of the Free Isles, across The Spout channel.

name: Xalahir
blurb: Cursed vampiric elves who traded nature for immortal hunger; eternal wanderers who feed on populations and vanish before victims realize the truth.

name: Xalvaakr
blurb: Domains: scarred memory, the vengeance of the displaced; rising cohort; the Sivakr underground-faith older than Tiira by a hundred years.

name: Xaphkiel
blurb: Shadow-born fallen angels who embrace exile as enlightenment through suffering, integrating into mortal society as torturers, surgeons, and confessors.

name: Xcraya Pallir
blurb: A region in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Xcraya River Valley
blurb: A river valley in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Xeltri Plateau
blurb: A broad, elevated tableland in the northern Giant Lands, positioned between the Hills of Gezzeri to the north and Malo Kon Greikh to the south.

name: Xhodon
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Xi Chin
blurb: A marble city grown into the wreck of a fallen Mira orb-ship, high in the Tethryns, whose aura traps anyone who stays past a few days.

name: Xi'ivrach
blurb: Deeply religious stone-bearing orcs of Jeh Bli whose ritual scarification and Deoric mastery express devotion to their stone gods.

name: Xibli
blurb: Blue-skinned aquatic goblins of the islands around Melrox with webbed hands and spiny backs, living semi-aquatic lives as freedom-loving hedonists.

name: Xibli Islands
blurb: A scattered archipelago east of the Aikon Tidal Islands, where the inhabitants have built their entire culture around competitive gift-giving.

name: Xibrik
blurb: Domains: reef-craft, coral architecture, tide-as-shelter; current cohort; Xibli goblin patron of the living wall.

name: Xicrein
blurb: Dark-featured island elves of the Bynü Tribes who wage silent clan wars through honorable assassination with oyster-shell blades.

name: Xicrenvael
blurb: Harsh-branch elf historian of the Reign of Dragons; specialist on dragon-elf territorial conflict during the Gaeaic Eon.

name: Xikraen
blurb: Domains: the oyster-shell blade, the silent kill, clan-feud honor; current cohort; Xicrein assassin-patron of the Bynü Tribes.

name: Xislmere
blurb: Xislmere resides in the far north, among the Knives Mountains.

name: Xoth
blurb: The theocratic nation of Xoth dominates southeastern Urok—a multi-racial covenant-state bound by shared worship of the Seven Patrons rather than blood or ancestry.

name: Xynoth Azkonor
blurb: Leads Chaal Nazzerox.

name: Xyrionn
blurb: In the hills at the center of Lethos stands a tree stump the size of a castle.

name: Yaif
blurb: The westernmost of the Kingdoms of Fire, and the oldest.

name: Yalami
blurb: A small river settlement in northern Kazül, on the water road toward Glorin.

name: Yanayazi
blurb: "Water-That-Remembers-Fire"

name: Yava
blurb: The largest city on Pesalolo, at the island's southwestern tip where the Ryujilin Hills meet the sea.

name: Yegnoth
blurb: A vertical shaft forty miles across, punched clean through the Myjornis Mountains into the earth below.

name: Yelláreth
blurb: Domain: motherhood, childbirth, the maternal line; dead cohort (Golden Age); one of the earliest gods, killed when infant mortality became universal.

name: Yibiye
blurb: The warded city, where ancient magic guards the harbor and old grudges run deeper than the sea.

name: Yift
blurb: The village closest to Glezamos, practically a suburb of the capital.

name: Ylidor
blurb: An eastern town near the broken coastline.

name: Yngli
blurb: A squat, one-eyed people of inborn force-attunement, bound to silence by an ancient Deoric curse and exploited as outsiders across the Western Isles and Kura.

name: Yokani Cliffs
blurb: Cliffs within Shyona.

name: Yompur Liko
blurb: The site of the annual winter bazaar—a small island cliff connected to the surrounding canyon walls by rope bridges.

name: Yorinthian
blurb: Frost-touched dwarves serving dragon goddess Nabuhe in frozen Hannoweir; cruel and rigid, they believe strength is only earned through suffering.

name: Yorx
blurb: A frontier town at the edge of the Taflex Forest, serving as the gateway to Grimreach proper.

name: Ysolveth
blurb: A Starborn who broke the order's cosmic neutrality, steering two titan-glass stars toward a convergence above the foothill city of Ilthenvar.

name: Ystaeria
blurb: Domains: ice-glass, mathematical precision, the cold that preserves; current cohort; Istori patron at the center of the export schism.

name: Ythari
blurb: Solitary elves each bound from birth to a single tree. They live exactly as long as it lives, and die the moment it dies.

name: Yugurbas Dyos
blurb: The bloody ground between Hedroscobb and Glivornax—a transitional marsh where the southern Wildwood gives way to the Tolkarsus watershed.

name: Yuki
blurb: A fortified city-state in the Suki Hills, standing as the first line of defense against Krell expansion into Chimean territory.

name: Yukum's Wall
blurb: Although not actually a wall, this mountain range is rife with giants, wyverns, snow spirits, and an eternal blizzard, making passing through so treacherous that…

name: Yumasa
blurb: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Yzre's Grave
blurb: Grave of an ord goldminer.

name: Zambullón
blurb: A small village in the southern forest fringe, surviving on herbalism and logging.

name: Zamrat Aqabah
blurb: A northern port city on Masaad Takid, Aal Salma's second city and its moral opposite.

name: Zarn'ik
blurb: Domains: desert survival, sand-spirit watch, the eye behind the eyelid; current cohort; Bledreon patron of the second sight that filters dust.

name: Zaru
blurb: Capital of Enymu, sitting at the junction of the Greenway (the main road to Tarkhetan) and the river that waters the central farmlands.

name: Zelidia
blurb: The state of Zelidia encompasses all Zelle settlements along the Sovia River.

name: Zelle
blurb: Theocratic pixies of Zelidia, condensed from a Faesong pool gone discordant; their High Priestesses feed the hungry current with sacrifice and hide their realm from a hostile world.

name: Zeror Gokh
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Zetl Hesk Rou
blurb: "The Lake of Last Breath" in the giant tongue.

name: Zetl Heski Voth
blurb: "The Lake of First Breath" in the giant tongue.

name: Zintacas
blurb: A tribal Gozi (Goshwen) nation in the canyon country south of Stipen.

name: Ziru
blurb: A dragon dwelling in the Dragonsong region.

name: Zlyverth Korn
blurb: The central forest is where the fire ley line enters Twaan territory, running north-south along the eastern edge of the woodland before converging with the…

name: Zodine River
blurb: River draining through the United Free Nations interior, poisoned by Erdel's dye industry and responsible for Donokasi's abandonment.

name: Zor
blurb: Zor is the only permanent settlement in the Eyendra forest—a city-state of roughly 15,000 souls perched on the western shore of Moornik Bay, facing outward…

name: Zord Oshbag
blurb: A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Zorheim Mountains
blurb: Home to Zorheim, the dragon guardian of the Cendelle dwarves who traveled back from Celestia.

name: Zosuun
blurb: Zosuun is an albino dragon who lairs in the coastal mountains of Gümloskos, north of Solhalven where the land meets the frozen Ocean of Azuros.

name: Zremmad Khag
blurb: A ruined city-state on the Xeltri Plateau, destroyed by hill giants.

name: Zubaydah
blurb: A settlement in the west, closer to the coast and the main trade routes to Kazül.

name: Zukisani
blurb: The northern city on the Myusha River, controlling river trade from the northern forests and serving as Iqes's connection to Gyosha and the waters beyond.

name: Zuko
blurb: The southern interior city, controlling land routes through Iqes's forested heart.

name: Zunuki
blurb: The second city, traditionally controlled by one of the now-headless houses.

name: Zure
blurb: A small northern town serving the communities around the Smokestack region.

name: Zwaeron
blurb: Gray-blue, frizzy orange-haired tundra dwarves who abandoned mines for the open snow; nomadic dire wolf riders who scorn underground life and prize freedom.

name: Zylogmus Eternus
blurb: An enormous maze of corridors and tunnels built on the lower surface of the Material Plane — the 'infinite labyrinth,' a megadungeon spanning thousands of miles.

name: Zyquirk
blurb: On Prophecy Island
